races.race_name: Changing Young Lives At Jamie's Farm Fillies' Juvenile Handicap Hurdle (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Only One Blue
runners.spotlight: Successful on debut over hurdles and has proved very consistent since, most recently a rallying second in 4yo handicap over C&D when the only filly in the field; very solid, back against her own sex.
runners.comment: Consistent; rallying second against males over C&D most recently; very solid claims

runners.name: Made U Blush
runners.spotlight: Successful in final Flat attempt and is 2-3 over hurdles for new yard, most recently bagging Listed honours at Musselburgh; one for the shortlist, with further progress plausible.
runners.comment: 2-3 over hurdles for new yard, bagging Listed honours at Musselburgh most recently

runners.name: Siorai
runners.spotlight: Has tended to take a good hold since racing over hurdles but managed to bag silver medals the last twice; in good hands and may progress now handicapping, especially if settling better.
runners.comment: May progress now handicapping, especially if settling better

runners.name: Queen Maeve
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who represents notable connections and may be capable of big improvement now handicapping; interesting, even though Harry Skelton prefers stablemate Made U Blush.
runners.comment: Interesting handicap debutante, even though Harry Skelton prefers Made U Blush

runners.name: Cloaks Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Had further wind surgery prior to Stratford success (2m2f, soft) last time, beating a solid yardstick in clearcut fashion and taking hurdles record to 2-3; improving in this sphere and looks one to consider.
runners.comment: Clearcut success at Stratford took her hurdles record to 2-3; improving filly

runners.name: The Procrastinator
runners.spotlight: Has shown consistent form that features two wins; ran creditably against her elders at Chepstow most recently; possibilities on handicap debut and back against her own age group.
runners.comment: Ran creditably against her elders last time; possibilities on handicap debut

runners.name: La Luna Artista
runners.spotlight: Developing into a useful filly and readily landed the Adonis (2m, good to soft; Grade 2) at Kempton most recently, taking record to 3-4; the class angle on handicap debut and has earned top weight; respected.
runners.comment: Readily landed the Adonis, taking record to 3-4; the class angle and has earned top weight

runners.name: Whitletts
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden who left Dan Skelton prior to Chepstow last time when bouncing back from a disappointing effort; however, even off bottom weight this looks a difficult task.
runners.comment: Appears to have a difficult task on second start for new stable

races.race_name: bet365 Novices' Championship Final Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Minella Yoga
runners.spotlight: Improving 4yo whose record is 3-4; bought for £360,000 after sole Irish hurdles start; ran well in Grade 2 for sole defeat; easily justified favouritism at Taunton (2m, good) last time; major player.
runners.comment: Improving 4yo whose record is 3-4; scored easily at Taunton last time; major contender

runners.name: Cosmic Connection
runners.spotlight: Scored in tidy fashion at Chepstow (2m, good to soft; handicap debut) last time, taking rules record to 2-3, and is probably still well treated even after a 6lb rise; improving.
runners.comment: Scored in tidy fashion at Chepstow (handicap debut) last time, taking rules record to 2-3

runners.name: Kadastral
runners.spotlight: Non-stayer over 2m4f last time; solid record at 2m otherwise, including two efforts behind smart Sober Glory; this Skelton runner likely still has more to offer; respected handicap debutant.
runners.comment: Has a solid record at 2m and likely still has more to offer; respected handicap debutant

runners.name: Kocktail Bleu
runners.spotlight: Proving consistent and, following a good effort in a Grade 2, he made all at Newbury (2m, good to soft) back down in class last time; far from sure to get an easy lead in this bigger field, however.
runners.comment: Made all at Newbury most recently but is far from sure to get an easy lead in this field

runners.name: Secret Sniper
runners.spotlight: No match for Minella Yoga at Taunton, taking record to 0-6; however, he's related to several winners for his connections and may have potential off his opening mark; one to consider.
runners.comment: Related to several winners for his connections; may have potential off his opening mark

runners.name: Legal Weapon
runners.spotlight: Reasonably consistent but is 0-5 over hurdles, finishing fourth to Wilstar at Warwick most recently; chance of finding the necessary progress largely depends on the effects of first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Chance of finding the necessary progress largely depends on the effects of tongue-tie

runners.name: Way Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Prevailed in second-time hood at Taunton (2m, good) most recently, adding to his Flat win, and may have more to offer in the retained headgear; in deeper waters today but could go well.
runners.comment: Prevailed in second-time hood at Taunton and may have more to offer in this headgear

runners.name: Wilstar
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to Douvan and Jonbon; not in their league but is progressive and has won his last two starts, latest at Warwick (2m, good to soft); enters calculations on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well bred; progressive and has won last two starts; enters calculations on handicap debut

runners.name: Kapitein Kool
runners.spotlight: Dual novice winner but came up well short at Newbury (2m3f) on handicap debut most recently and can be opposed in this competitive field.
runners.comment: Came up well short on handicap debut last time; can be opposed

runners.name: Dutch Corner
runners.spotlight: Posted consistent efforts prior to ready win in Newton Abbot maiden (2m2f, good to soft) last time; may improve further and can't be dismissed, even though Harry Skelton prefers Kadastral.
runners.comment: Scored readily at Newton Abbot last time and may improve further; yard also runs Kadastral

runners.name: Stanners Glen
runners.spotlight: Made all in Wincanton maiden (1m7f, good) in latest hurdles attempt, adding to his point/bumper wins and taking overall record to 3-6; however, this scenario is much more demanding.
runners.comment: Made all at Wincanton most recently but this scenario is more demanding

runners.name: Race To Base
runners.spotlight: Had wind surgery since failing to convince over 2m4f in the EBF Final on this track last time on stable debut; return to 2m looks a plus but he's still seeking first rules win away from Hereford.
runners.comment: Return to 2m looks a plus but he's still seeking first rules win away from Hereford

runners.name: Last Round
runners.spotlight: Finished behind Kadastral on penultimate start but may fare better in this rematch, having scored comfortably in Fontwell maiden (2m1f, good to soft) latest; further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Scored comfortably in Fontwell maiden last time; further progress is possible

runners.name: Bespoke Tailor
runners.spotlight: Has consistent hurdles form; however, his chance of gaining a first success in this sphere largely depends on how well he responds to a physical tweak (wind surgery since last run).
runners.comment: Chance of gaining a first hurdles win depends on how well he responds to wind surgery

runners.name: Desertmore News
runners.spotlight: Made all in small field at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) last time, taking hurdles record to 2-3; however, he faces a stiffer task in this deep handicap with competition for the lead.
runners.comment: Made all in small field at Sedgefield last time; faces stiffer task in this deep handicap

runners.name: Talakan
runners.spotlight: Flat winner in France and knocking at the door over hurdles for new yard, second to Wilstar at Warwick (2m) most recently; possibilities, provided he shows some progress on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Knocking at the door; possibilities, provided he shows progress on handicap debut

runners.name: Love You Back
runners.spotlight: Flat winner whose hurdles form comprises a couple of second-place finishes and a late fall (beaten at the time); even off bottom weight, this looks a difficult task upped in class.
runners.comment: Even off bottom weight, this looks a difficult task upped in class

runners.name: Ambiente Friendly
runners.spotlight: Showed smart Flat form as a 3yo; managed to win in December but has otherwise broadly been too free for his own good since switched to hurdles; ideally needs to settle better.
runners.comment: Broadly too free for his own good since switched to hurdles; needs to settle better

races.race_name: bet365 Oaksey Chase (Grade 2) (For The Menorah Challenge Trophy) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Matterhorn
runners.spotlight: Chase record is 5-11, the most recent win at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) last spring; seen only twice since but ran well in a notable handicap at Plumpton (2m1f) the latest time; possibilities at the weights, provided he stays the new trip.
runners.comment: Ran well in notable handicap at Plumpton last time; possibilities if he stays the new trip

runners.name: Doyen Quest
runners.spotlight: Useful novice chaser whose form features second-place finishes in 3m Grade 2 events at Sandown and Windsor; came up rather short in Grade 1 at Aintree last time but looks one of the main contenders returned to this level.
runners.comment: Useful novice chaser who has Grade 2 form at Sandown and Windsor; one of the main players
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late N W Lake

runners.name: Blow Your Wad
runners.spotlight: Has shown good form for new stable, mostly in handicaps, including at the Cheltenham Festival (3m1f); defied top weight at Chepstow (2m3f, good to soft) most recently, looking better than ever; leading chance at the weights in his bid for a second Grade 2 success.
runners.comment: Defied top weight in Chepstow handicap last time, looking better than ever; leading claims

runners.name: Etalon
runners.spotlight: Form since wearing cheekpieces includes good performances (career-best form) at Ascot, winning over 2m3f then third over 2m5f (good to soft and soft); may rebound from a lesser effort last time but Harry Skelton prefers stablemate Doyen Quest.
runners.comment: May rebound from a lesser effort last time but Harry Skelton prefers Doyen Quest

runners.name: Teddy Blue
runners.spotlight: Has registered a couple of useful handicap wins this season, including at Kempton (2m4f, good) on penultimate start; faces a stiffer task conceding weight all round in this grade, with 4lb penalty incurred.
runners.comment: Two handicap wins this term but faces a stiffer task under 4lb penalty in this grade

runners.name: Mahons Glory
runners.spotlight: Broadly consistent in handicaps for current yard and ran respectably in the Topham at Aintree (2m5f) most recently; made all, despite jumping left, at Huntingdon (2m4f; also on good to soft) the time before; this is a harder task.
runners.comment: Consistent in handicaps for current yard but faces a harder task upped in class
runners.owner: C Nightingale & Hold My Beer Partnership

runners.name: Jonbon
runners.spotlight: Multiple Grade 1 winner whose excellent record at Sandown includes form figures of 112 in this contest; perhaps ideally needs further now, having been upped to 2m5f for the second leg of this season's Ascot double, then posted a Ryanair effort (2m4f) that took his Cheltenham Festival form figures to 2222; leading player provided the drop back in trip doesn't backfire.
runners.comment: Multiple Grade 1 winner; good record at Sandown includes form figures of 112 in this race

runners.name: Solness
runners.spotlight: Smart front-runner whose Grade 1 form features three wins at Leopardstown (2m1f) in 2024/2025 and neck second, showing career-best form, at Aintree (2m4f, good to soft) on latest start; one of the main contenders back down in distance.
runners.comment: Close second in similar event at Aintree last time, showing career-best form; major player

runners.name: Thistle Ask
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has shown excellent progress over fences and whose record for current stable reads 11112, the last two wins in Grade 2 handicaps; collared by Jonbon in soft-ground Grade 1 at Ascot (2m1f) when last seen; may turn the tables returned to forecast ideal ground, being 5-5 on good; possibly hasn't reached his ceiling yet; respected.
runners.comment: Progressive; may reverse Ascot placings with Jonbon returned to better ground; 5-5 on good

runners.name: JPR One
runners.spotlight: Form since upped to 2m4f comprises a handicap win at Musselburgh and fourth-place finishes in Grade 1 events at Cheltenham and Aintree; return to shorter may not be an issue but he ideally needs a drop back in class.
runners.comment: Return to shorter may not be an issue but he ideally needs a drop back in class

runners.name: Mirabad
runners.spotlight: Belied his 50-1 odds with 6l success in Grade 1 novice chase at Aintree (2m, good to soft) two weeks ago; however, needs to prove he can back up that form and Harry Skelton prefers solid stablemate Thistle Ask.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he can back up the form of 50-1 success in Grade 1 novice at Aintree

runners.name: Edwardstone
runners.spotlight: Admirable veteran, albeit isn't quite the force of old and gained this season's win in substandard Grade 2 at Kempton (2m4f); also perhaps ideally suited by further than about 2m nowadays; beaten twice in this race.
runners.comment: Admirable veteran but perhaps needs further now; beaten twice in this contest

runners.name: Brookie
runners.spotlight: Has proved difficult to place since last win in March 2025 and form has taken a downturn of late; has the worst chance at the weights back up in class.
runners.comment: Difficult to place and has the worst chance at the weights back up in class

races.race_name: bet365 Gold Cup Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Rock My Way
runners.spotlight: 2-14 record over fences includes some big runs, including when he hacked up at Ascot (3m5f, good) in November and took second in the Midlands National at Uttoxeter (4m2f, soft) on latest outing; the Ascot race was going this way round but so was his penultimate start when he kept jumping to his left at Musselburgh (12l second to Transmission); that's a niggling concern but he's handicapped to go well if this is another good day.
runners.comment: Hacked up at Ascot and second in 4m2f Midlands National; jumped to his left two runs ago

runners.name: Montregard
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to top-class Protektorat; withdrawn from Scottish National last week due to the heavy going; made three notable steps forward on varied ground over 3m at Ascot (unraced here) this season, including for a good prize on latest start when he led two out for a clearcut win; raised another 4lb but he's firmly on the upgrade, likely to prove well handicapped if he gets this trip, which is also feasible; a bold show is on its way.
runners.comment: Firmly on upgrade; can prove well handicapped if he gets this trip, which is feasible

runners.name: Havaila
runners.spotlight: A rising force in the last 15 months and that's gained serious momentum in his last two starts, when winning easily by 7l at Newbury (extended 2m7f, good to soft) and 5l in the Sussex National at Plumpton (3m5f, good); strong-travelling sort who's now seeing out his races really well too; up another 10lb but could still have more in the locker.
runners.comment: Won easily by 7l at Newbury and 5l at Plumpton (3m5f Sussex National, good) last two races

runners.name: Resplendent Grey
runners.spotlight: Won this race (good ground) last year in first-time cheekpieces off a 10lb lower mark; first and second in two small fields this season, alongside two heavy defeats in top handicaps; tried blinkers when fading right out of it at the Cheltenham Festival last time and now reverts to cheekpieces; also back on firmer ground but a new career best is needed.
runners.comment: Won this race (good ground) last year in first-time cheekpieces off a 10lb lower mark

runners.name: Ask Brewster
runners.spotlight: 3m4f resulted in an 8l win at Cheltenham last April and he's won over 3m/3m2f in two of his three starts this season; latest was the Kim Muir at Cheltenham in which he was always front rank under Shane Cotter and responded admirably when Road To Home joined him on the run-in; up 7lb but this trip makes plenty of appeal and all five chase wins have been on good ground or good to firm (withdrawn from Scottish National last week due to the heavy); has a bit to prove going right-handed, with a run here in December prompting concern, but otherwise lots to like.
runners.comment: Bit to prove right-handed but otherwise lots to like, likely to be suited by trip/ground

runners.name: Livin On Luco
runners.spotlight: Failed to fire in the Eider at Newcastle on penultimate start; commendable otherwise this season, making all in the Southern National at Fontwell (3m3f, good to soft), fourth in Welsh Grand National at Chepstow (3m7f, good to soft) and short-headed at Exeter (3m7f, good) five weeks ago; raised another 2lb but has place claims.
runners.comment: Commendable over 3m3f/3m7f in three of his four starts this season; place claims

runners.name: Henry's Friend
runners.spotlight: Seems best making the running or racing prominently; his six races in the last 14 months have been a mixed bag, with an Ascot win (3m, good) in November the highlight; just 1lb higher today and ran respectably on penultimate start, but his two earlier attempts at 3m6f/4m did not work out; a tongue-tie (worn twice in 2023, with a win on first occasion) returns.
runners.comment: Six races in last 14 months were a mixed bag, Ascot 3m win in November the clear highlight

runners.name: Invincible Nao
runners.spotlight: Won Sussex National at Plumpton (extended 3m4f, soft) last February in his first long-distance race but less convincing since in forays over 4m2f, C&D and 3m5f; had a small-field 2m4f win on good ground here in November but this race, from 2lb out of the handicap, demands a big personal best.
runners.comment: Won over extended 3m4f last term; this from 2lb out of handicap demands big personal best

runners.name: In d'Or
runners.spotlight: 3m6f for the NH Chase at Cheltenham last March was his only attempt beyond 3m and he was below form; that was also his last run for Venetia Williams (bought for £220,000 in October at the David Maxwell dispersal) and he's been a good third this winter in 3m handicaps here and at Ascot; needs to build on that, as he's edging up the weights, but he's not fully exposed.
runners.comment: Good third this winter in 3m handicaps here and at Ascot; needs to build on that

runners.name: Transmission
runners.spotlight: Tapped back into last season's initial promising form, and then some, to win by 12l in the Edinburgh National at Musselburgh (3m7f, good to soft; from Rock My Way) in February with a first-time visor, which has been kept since; ridden by 7lb claimer when fair third off this 8lb higher mark behind Havaila in the Sussex National at Plumpton 19 days ago but needs to hit new heights again.
runners.comment: 12l win at Musselburgh (3m7f) with a first-time visor; fair third to Havaila at Plumpton

runners.name: Road To Home
runners.spotlight: He was set to be the only Willie Mullins runner in last week's Scottish National but was withdrawn due to the heavy ground; 7yo who was delivered with what seemed a winning challenge under Patrick Mullins in the Kim Muir at Cheltenham (3m2f, good; furthest he's been) six weeks ago only to find that Ask Brewster battled back; up 6lb but has to be in serious calculations.
runners.comment: Neck 2nd to Ask Brewster in the Kim Muir at Cheltenham (3m2f, good; furthest he's been)

runners.name: Gabbys Cross
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 11yo (six races for current yard) who has not won since 2022 but went close with first-time cheekpieces (kept since) over 3m2f at Newbury in February; second of three at Ascot (3m) in another veterans' race next time was creditable in the end, after he'd been detached at one point; that helps to envisage him as a stayer but he jumped poorly on heavy in Scottish National (40-1, pulled up) last week and this race also looks tough.
runners.comment: Jumped poorly on heavy in Scottish National (40-1) last week; this race also looks tough
runners.owner: J P McGrath & R N Pallas

runners.name: Certainly Red
runners.spotlight: Seventh in this race in 2023 and fourth on good ground in 2024; missed all of 2025 and although he ran encouragingly on reappearance here (3m, soft; 50-1) seven weeks ago, still disputing the lead when making a mess of the second last, others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Plenty of best runs have been at this track but other runners have more pressing claims

runners.name: Our Power
runners.spotlight: Withdrawn from Scottish National last week due to the heavy ground; 6lb lower than this time last year (when third in the Scottish National) as he's not fired on all cylinders this term, but excuses can be made; he's won two major 3m handicaps going right-handed (Ascot and Kempton) on good ground, back in the 2022-23 season admittedly but this 11yo may be dangerous to dismiss, including as first-time blinkers replace his usual cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Not firing on all cylinders this term; first-time blinkers replace his usual cheekpieces

runners.name: Jingko Blue
runners.spotlight: Has shown good form over 2m4f/2m5f at Cheltenham this term, finishing second to Kabral Du Mathan in Grade 2 and recording an emphatic success from the front in Festival handicap; failed to get home in 3m Grade 1 at Aintree last time; one of the main contenders, back down in distance.
runners.comment: One of the main contenders, albeit may struggle to reverse placings with Kabral Du Mathan

runners.name: Rubaud
runners.spotlight: Form dipped in Cheltenham Festival handicap returned to fences last time; multiple Grade 2 winner but has raced mostly at about 2m and isn't crying out for this step back up in trip; first go beyond a sharp 2m4f.
runners.comment: Raced mostly at about 2m and isn't crying out for this step back up in trip

runners.name: Lucky Place
runners.spotlight: Dual Grade 2 winner last term; finished third at the same level on penultimate start but was behind Kabral Du Mathan and stablemate Jingko Blue in that Cheltenham race; seems set for another bronze medal at best.
runners.comment: Finished behind Kabral Du Mathan and Jingko Blue on penultimate start
runners.owner: Mrs G Van Geest & M George

runners.name: French Ship
runners.spotlight: Form has gone rather awry in last three outings but he has been progressive otherwise, culminating in 2m4f handicap wins at Cheltenham and Newbury in the autumn; may rebound and run a good race.
runners.comment: Form has gone awry in last three starts but progressive otherwise; may rebound

races.race_name: bet365 Josh Gifford Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Palamon
runners.spotlight: Returned to form and rewarded heavy market support at Bangor (2m4f, good; tongue-tie refitted) last month, his fifth start for Dan Skelton; just a respectable sixth under penalty in the Red Rum at Aintree (2m, good to soft) nine days later and his mark is another 5lb higher here, but he remains very much of interest.
runners.comment: Landed a big punt at Bangor last month and could still be well treated after 10lb hike

runners.name: Calimystic
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 7yo who is 2-2 over fences, the latter win gained here (3m, soft) last month; recently withdrawn with good ground deemed unsuitable but his first two wins (one over hurdles) were gained on good; should cope with this drop back in trip and has plenty of scope for further progress; respected.
runners.comment: Scored over 3m here last month and is now 2-2 over fences; should have more to offer

runners.name: Go West
runners.spotlight: Didn't jump well enough when well beaten in Kempton Grade 2 novice in February last time but had doubled his chasing tally with ready success at Musselburgh (2m4f, good to soft) three weeks earlier, and will be dangerous if back in similar form.
runners.comment: Didn't jump well enough last time; contender if judged on Musselburgh win two runs ago

runners.name: Kahavari
runners.spotlight: 0-13 since winning over hurdles on debut in France in 2024; runner-up in three chases since fitted with a tongue-tie this year, most recently at Kelso (2m7f, good) three weeks ago, but he is 3lb higher in a stronger race here and needs to find something extra.
runners.comment: Clear second at Kelso this month but is 3lb higher in a stronger race here

runners.name: Millforce
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Gavin Cromwell in Ireland with hard-fought win at Galway (2m6f, yielding) in the autumn but was pulled up when reappearing for new stable at Haydock (3m1f, good to soft) ten days ago and needs to turn things around quickly.
runners.comment: Won in Ireland in the autumn but pulled up on stable debut just ten days ago

runners.name: Fasol
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent 7yo who recovered from a jumping error at third-last flight to take fourth in Sussex Champion Hurdle at Plumpton (2m, good) there weeks ago; also suited by 2m4f and this track; considered each-way.
runners.comment: Ran well in Sussex Champion Hurdle three weeks ago and this longer trip also suits
runners.owner: P J Vogt And Bryan Drew

runners.name: Trustintimes
runners.spotlight: 3-5 over hurdles, the latest win gained at Ascot (1m7f, good) in November; never really got involved when upped to 2m4f at Aintree a fortnight ago but could plausibly still have untapped potential over this trip.
runners.comment: 3-5 hurdles; below form when upped to 2m4f this month but may still have potential
runners.owner: The Time On Our Hands Boys' Syndicate

runners.name: Lucky Manifest
runners.spotlight: Started off for new stable in excellent form in the autumn, winning at Kelso (2m6f, good) before big-field second at Cheltenham (2m5f, soft), but his progress has stalled a bit since; needs combination of last month's wind op and first-time tongue-tie to have positive effect.
runners.comment: Did well in the autumn but progress has stalled since; given wind op last month

runners.name: Listentoyourheart
runners.spotlight: Just held on to land big prize at Windsor (2m4f, good) on New Year's Day but wasn't in quite the same form on next two outings (2m3f/3m) and probably needs to find hidden reserves.
runners.comment: Landed big prize at Windsor on New Year's Day but not quite in same form either run since
runners.owner: S T Racing - The Founders' Syndicate

runners.name: Across Earth
runners.spotlight: Won against his own age-group here (2m, soft; also suited by good) in December and has finished close up on all three outings since (1m7f-2m); must be considered on this first attempt at 2m4f.
runners.comment: 2m winner here in December; still in good form; considered on first attempt at 2m4f

runners.name: Tour Ovalie
runners.spotlight: Gained all six hurdle wins at around 2m but ran quite well when fourth at Chepstow (2m3f, soft) last month; current mark is workable (even from 1lb out of the weights) and Sean Bowen takes over today; could have a say.
runners.comment: Ran quite well at Chepstow last month and Sean Bowen takes over today; a possible

runners.name: Royal Way
runners.spotlight: Exploited reduced mark when winning quite comfortably at Ludlow last month and was one of two to pull clear of the others when second at Ascot (also 2m5f, good) ten days later; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Won off reduced mark last month and ran well in defeat ten days later

runners.name: Surrey Lord
runners.spotlight: Made good progress towards end of 2025, with latest of three wins gained at Kempton (2m5f, good) on Boxing Day; not discredited when fourth at Ascot (2m3f, soft) in January and has good claims if tuned up after a break.
runners.comment: Made good progress towards end of 2025 (three wins); returns from a short break

runners.name: Gnomon
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful Flat racer; runner-up on first three hurdle starts (all 2m) before cruising clear in four-runner Ascot maiden (2m3f, good) last month; could easily have more improvement to come in handicap hurdles; interesting.
runners.comment: Cosily won small-field Ascot maiden in March; could easily have more to offer in handicaps
runners.owner: Andrew And Sarah Wates And Gt Racing

runners.name: Favour And Fortune
runners.spotlight: Seen only twice this season but posted career-best performance when close third of 22 at Aintree (2m4f, good to soft) a fortnight ago, despite Tom Bellamy's saddle slipping after the last; respected.
runners.comment: Career-best form when close third of 22 at Aintree a fortnight ago

runners.name: Aurigny Mill
runners.spotlight: Seemingly back in prime form when clear winner at Taunton (2m, heavy) in February; also suited by good ground and stays at least 2m3f but he disappointed on latest outing and others in this field look better handicapped.
runners.comment: Good winner at Taunton two starts ago but others in this field look better handicapped

runners.name: Last Kingdom
runners.spotlight: Won big-field Listed handicap in first-time cheekpieces at Listowel (2m, good to yielding) for Willie Mullins in September; badly out of form for new stable this year (four runs) but may fare better now that the cheekpieces are reinstated.
runners.comment: Out of form for new stable this year but the refitted cheekpieces might aid his cause

runners.name: No Ordinary Joe
runners.spotlight: Kept on for sixth of 21 in first-time visor at Aintree (2m, good to soft) a fortnight ago and will be suited by this move back up in trip, but his last win came in 2022 and he's patently not the force of old.
runners.comment: Ran well at Aintree a fortnight ago but his last win came in 2022; others preferred

runners.name: Dargiannini
runners.spotlight: Returned from a break with narrow win at Uttoxter (2m4f, good) in October but pulled up on both starts since, latterly at last month's Cheltenham Festival; younger rivals have much more persuasive profiles.
runners.comment: Narrow winner off today's mark in October but pulled up both starts since

runners.name: Authorised Speed
runners.spotlight: Dual 2m winner here; absent since finishing fifth in a beginners' chase in May 2024 but was placed off mark 7lb higher than today's in this race two years ago (good ground); worth a glance in the market.
runners.comment: Placed off 7lb higher in this race two years ago; returns from long layoff today

runners.name: Pursuit Of Love
runners.spotlight: Sent off the odds-on favourite for his Southwell debut (1m3f) eight weeks ago and he ran on strongly to win by just shy of 3l; that form looks solid and he is open to plenty of improvement; looks a good prospect.
runners.comment: Strong finish to win a 1m3f novice at Southwell eight weeks ago; plenty more to come

runners.name: Quiet Summer
runners.spotlight: 13,000euros yearling; ninth foal; half-brother to seven winners including Quian (1m-2m Flat including 2yo/German Group 3, RPR 104; 2m-2m3f hurdle) and Laggata (9.4f; 76); dam German 1m4f winner, half-sister to triple 1m4f Group 1 winner Quijano; should have a bright future but he makes his debut in a good novice.
runners.comment: 13,000euros yearling from a good German middle-distance family; tough task on debut

runners.name: Ravenspire
runners.spotlight: Created a positive impression when easily winning a 1m4f novice at Southwell last month, coming home 3l clear of a stablemate of Pursuit Of Love; likely he will need more to follow up but he is still of serious interest.
runners.comment: Impressed at Southwell (1m4f) last month; up against a useful opponent but can do better
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Poker
runners.spotlight: Ran to a modest level in novices on good to soft ground last autumn (7f and 1m1f); upped in trip and tried in headgear for his reappearance and this 4,300,000gns yearling remains capable of better.
runners.comment: Upped in trip and fitted with headgear for his return; needs to leave 2yo form behind him

races.race_name: William Hill Price Boosts Every Day EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Symbol Of Majesty
runners.spotlight: Smart pedigree; did really well to win a 1m fillies' novice at Chelmsford in December, blowing the start and having plenty to do turning for home but producing a fast finish to win under a hand ride; strong claims despite her penalty.
runners.comment: Did really well to win on debut (1m, AW) given she did a lot wrong; looks a good prospect

runners.name: Forbidden Colours
runners.spotlight: Last September's Newmarket debut (7f, good to firm) was full of promise; failed to build on it when fifth of ten at Chelmsford last month but that run may have been needed and she can surely do better back on turf and upped in trip.
runners.comment: Promising 2yo debut; less good on AW return but still has time to do much better

runners.name: Brighton Beach
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister to six winners including Alkandora (10.5f Listed; RPR 107), Persian Royal (1m; 97) and Cambric (1m4f-1m6f; 84); dam Listed-placed 1m 2yo winner (103); one of two for William Haggas but Tom Marquand rides Chimes Of Thunder.
runners.comment: Half-sister to six winners and should have a future; trainer also runs Chimes Of Thunder

runners.name: Silken Bay
runners.spotlight: Never in contention on her Wolverhampton debut in February (8.6f); fitted with a hood for her second start and she recorded a shock 50-1 success in a five-runner event over 1m at Southwell 23 days ago; this looks tougher under a penalty.
runners.comment: Improved for a hood when springing 50-1 surprise at Southwell (1m) this month; more needed

runners.name: Chimes Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Dropped away in the final furlong on her Salisbury debut last October (7f, good; trained by David Menuisier), a race that developed into a three-furlong sprint; should appreciate today's longer trip and she's an interesting contender starting out for William Haggas (same owners).
runners.comment: Promise on sole 2yo start for D Menuisier (7f, good); open to improvement over 1m+

runners.name: Mountain Of Light
runners.spotlight: 425,000gns yearling; third foal; half-sister to winners Manara (1m-1m4f; RPR 89) and Dance Desire (Saudi Arabia 7f-1m1f); dam 6f winner (96), out of close relative of 1m2f Group 1 winner Wavering and half-sister to 1m2f 2yo Group 1 winner Mandaean; market strength would look significant.
runners.comment: 425,000gns half-sister to two winners out of a useful 6f winner; market check advised

runners.name: Sedeirah
runners.spotlight: Second foal of a Group-placed 1m/1m2f winner (RPR 115), herself from a good family; makes her debut for a stable in good form and she needs a look in the betting.
runners.comment: Dam a Group-placed 1m/1m2f winner (RPR 115); makes her debut with her trainer in fine form

runners.name: Golden Mabel
runners.spotlight: 300-1 and finished well beaten on last month's Wolverhampton debut (8.6f).
runners.comment: 300-1 and finished well beaten on last month's Wolverhampton debut (8.6f)

runners.name: Sarab Star
runners.spotlight: His latest Newcastle defeat isn't the run to judge him on and he had been progressing well beforehand; no problem with the return to turf and stepping back up to 7f will be of benefit; interesting with cheekpieces now given a go.
runners.comment: Below par over 6f last time but on the up over 7f beforehand; now tries cheekpieces

runners.name: Cosi Bello
runners.spotlight: Began his career with two 7f AW wins and he was a fine second at Chester on his handicap debut last June (7f, good); fourth of 17 in a 1m handicap at Ascot when last seen nine months ago, a run that can be marked up as he raced away from the first three home; still has potential and he returns with his stable back in better form.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo; stable in better form now and he is one to consider after nine months off

runners.name: Khafiz
runners.spotlight: Began 2025 with 7f novice wins at Kempton and Doncaster; ended his 3yo campaign with a close third at Doncaster (7f, soft) 183 days ago; sold out of Roger Varian's stable for 85,000gns in October; still has low mileage, is yet to run a poor race when wearing a hood and is starting out for a trainer in great form; lots to like.
runners.comment: Conditions should suit and he has good record in a hood; new stable going well at present

runners.name: Korker
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at Doncaster on his return but he wasn't well drawn and has a modest record fresh; stepping up to 7f will give him more time to claw back the ground he's likely to forfeit at the start; on a dangerous mark and he's a lively outsider, for all his losing run stretches back to October 2023.
runners.comment: Doncaster return easy to excuse; on dangerous mark and 7f looks an avenue worth exploring
runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing,Mrs Burke & C Wood

runners.name: Great Acclaim
runners.spotlight: Strong handicap form over 7f last summer, winning at Goodwood and Chepstow as well as finishing second in a 20-runner event at Ascot (all when wearing blinkers); still feasibly weighted on that form and he went without headgear when down the field in the Lincoln; the blinkers return today and he's a leading contender.
runners.comment: Strong 7f handicap form in blinkers in 2025; headgear absent for return but back on today

runners.name: The Angel King
runners.spotlight: The winner of two of his six starts in France, both over 7f on ground slower than good; sold for 36,000euros in February; makes his stable debut in a competitive race.
runners.comment: Two 7f wins on slower ground in France; sold 36,000euros since latest run; others safer

runners.name: Caburn
runners.spotlight: Won a valuable pot as a 2yo; slow to come to hand as a 3yo but he changed yards last summer and made an impressive winning return from a gelding operation last September (6f, good to soft; cheekpieces on for the first time); fine reappearance when third in the Listed Cammidge Trophy at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) four weeks ago; raised 3lb for his troubles and still has to prove his stamina for 7f.
runners.comment: Improved effort when 3rd in 6f Listed event four weeks ago; up 3lb and stamina not assured

runners.name: Croupier
runners.spotlight: Progressive handicapper for the Crisfords and he was last seen finishing second in the Buckingham Palace at Royal Ascot (7f, good to firm) in June 2023; sold for 20,000gns in May 2024; only dropped 3lb during his 1038-day absence; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Smart handicapper for the Crisfords but he has a 1038-day absence to defy on stable debut

runners.name: Cerulean Bay
runners.spotlight: Had strong handicap form over 1m in the second half of 2025, finishing second at York's Ebor meeting (good to firm) before recording back-to-back wins at Goodwood (good to soft and good); ran well for third in a Nottingham Listed race when last seen 178 days ago; only seventh in this race 12 months ago and he's 4lb higher today.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 in fine form at 1m; may come on for this first start since October

runners.name: Darkness
runners.spotlight: Came good at Goodwood last August (7f, good to firm), running on strongly to bag a Class 2 handicap; faced a stiff task in a Thirsk conditions event on his reappearance two weeks ago; opposable with his usual visor remaining absent.
runners.comment: Stiff task on his return and finished well held; the absent visor remains a concern

runners.name: Marshman
runners.spotlight: Close third in Listed company at Wolverhampton last month on his first attempt at 7f and he wasn't beaten for a lack of stamina; not at his best in a hot sprint handicap at Newcastle on Good Friday; no surprise to see him bounce back with a big run.
runners.comment: Unexposed at 7f; better than he showed at Newcastle three weeks ago; not ruled out
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 2 & Mrs E Burke

runners.name: Henlein
runners.spotlight: Finished second in a Group 1 in Australia in October 2024 (1m2f, good; visored first time); well short of his best in two runs in early 2025 and has since been gelded and transferred to Britain; had a wind op prior to this first start for Kevin Ryan; no easy task under top weight.
runners.comment: Ex-Australian; Group 1 placed; absent 420 days; looks to face stiff task on British debut
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

races.race_name: William Hill Bet Builder Winnings Boost Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Ted Le Saux
runners.spotlight: Ready winner of a 1m novice at Newcastle in January on his second outing; flopped when odds-on at Wolverhampton last month but was reported to have an irregular heartbeat afterwards; moves back up in trip for his handicap debut and he remains capable of better.
runners.comment: 1m win in January; probably best to forgive latest defeat; now handicapping with potential

runners.name: Winter Flower
runners.spotlight: Only four ran when she won her novice at Newcastle (1m2f) last September; better form in handicaps on her last two starts, second at Newcastle (1m4f) and a close sixth in a Class 2 at Lingfield when last seen in December; still has time to better now returned to turf.
runners.comment: Lightly raced filly; conditions should be fine and drop back in class will help; chance

runners.name: Cavolo Nero
runners.spotlight: Two wins over shorter as a 3yo; improved when second at Kempton on his seasonal return last month (1m) and when third at Nottingham 17 days ago he found himself poorly positioned; well worth another crack at this trip and he should make a bold bid.
runners.comment: Good 2nd on AW return last month; poorly positioned when 3rd over 1m2f latest; solid

runners.name: Cancelled
runners.spotlight: Shaped okay at Wolverhampton on her return from 11 months off in December but she failed to back it up at Southwell last month; dropping down the weights and this return to turf could be beneficial.
runners.comment: Not at her best last month; down in the weights and today's return to turf should suit

runners.name: Roarin' Success
runners.spotlight: On a fair mark on her best Flat form for Harry Charlton; some promise over hurdles for new stable and 1m4f may have stretched her when back on the Flat at Thirsk two weeks ago.
runners.comment: On a fair mark and dropping back in trip could help; others are more persuasive though
runners.owner: Hale Racing, Lyle Farming Company & Ptnr

runners.name: Naples
runners.spotlight: His two peak efforts for Paddy Twomey came over 1m on soft ground; 50-1 when tailed off in the Spring Mile at Doncaster on his seasonal return; some encouragement for the longer trip on pedigree but he is not a solid option.
runners.comment: 1m maiden win in Ireland came on soft ground; well beaten on stable debut; new trip today

runners.name: Empire Of Light
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways with a front-running 10.3f success in the mud at Chester last September; this course winner will need to return at the top of his game if he is to defy this 4lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Made all to win at Chester (10.3f, heavy) 218 days ago; this is tougher up 4lb after break

runners.name: Due To Henry
runners.spotlight: Three wins, two on soft ground, as a 3yo and he is now 9lb lower than for the latest; generally struggled last year though, before and after wind surgery, and he has too much to prove for comfort.
runners.comment: Did well in 2024 but last season was difficult; returns with a point to prove

races.race_name: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap

runners.name: Strength Of Spirit
runners.spotlight: Has taken great strides since winning a seller last June, winning with authority on handicap debut at Newbury (7f, good to firm) on final 2yo start and he again had reserves when successful at Musselburgh (1m, good to soft) this month; up 4lb but he's anything but exposed; respected.
runners.comment: 2-2 in handicaps, readily over 7f (good to firm) and 1m (good to soft); up 4lb; unexposed
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXV & E Burke

runners.name: Dunkeld Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Ten 2yo runs, winning three handicaps at about 1m (good to firm, good and soft); took a hefty rise for the latest of them and is now 12lb higher; creditable comeback at Musselburgh recently but surprising if she's well enough treated to win today; yard also runs Strength Of Spirit.
runners.comment: Won 3 handicaps at about 1m as 2yo but still 12lb higher than for the latest of them

runners.name: Gold Dawn
runners.spotlight: Maiden who ran well in all three handicaps at 7f and 1m, making most when runner-up over this trip at Newmarket (good) in September; this looks a tough race in which to get off the mark; yard also runs Mr Seagull.
runners.comment: Ran well in all three handicaps as 2yo, at 7f and 1m; tough to get off the mark today

runners.name: Commanding Officer
runners.spotlight: Ran up against smart types on first two 7f starts, on this track and at York (both on good), keeping on pleasingly into third on debut, then ran to similar level next time; was in the right place at the right time, unlike the second, when winning a C&D novice in September; open to improvement as a 3yo; goes handicapping with cheekpieces fitted.
runners.comment: Off the mark on third start, a C&D novice in September; open to progress; handicap debut

runners.name: Go Rimbaud
runners.spotlight: Golden pedigree, dam had four Group 1 winners (at 1m up to 2m4f) among her siblings, including Friendly Soul for this yard; this one hasn't been as dynamic as the latter so far and his defeat of some newcomers and handicap prospects on Kempton AW (1m; made all) 17 days ago was hard to assess in merit terms; needs more now handicapping but that's quite possible.
runners.comment: Golden pedigree; hard to quantify recent 1m AW maiden win when making all; could progress

runners.name: See Blue
runners.spotlight: Hit the front in the nick of time when justifying support on his Kempton AW debut in September but he was easily outpointed by a promising type on Southwell reappearance (1m) last month, when much more use was made of him; interesting to see the tactics on turf debut but should do better.
runners.comment: Arrived late to justify support on AW debut; toppled by useful type since; turf debut

runners.name: Mr Seagull
runners.spotlight: Bright start, winning at Pontefract (6f, good to firm) then had work cut out trying to give weight to a promising newcomer on AW; big prices and well beaten in two sales races when last seen in September; gelded since and now goes in cheekpieces on handicap debut; faces a tough return upped to 1m.
runners.comment: 6f winner on debut; well held in two sales races in September; tough return upped to 1m
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four

runners.name: Electrical
runners.spotlight: Bred to stay further than 1m but was sharp enough to win 6f Newbury novice (good to firm) on debut; found wanting in two later runs but in Listed company first and soft ground may not have suited back at Newbury (1m); gelded since; this first handicap looks competitive.
runners.comment: Bred to stay well but sharp enough to win over 6f on debut; excuses since; handicap debut

races.race_name: William Hill Each Way Extra Handicap

runners.name: Paranjape
runners.spotlight: 6f maiden win at Wolverhampton in November; better form when second of 12 in a 6f handicap at Southwell 18 days ago; 1lb higher today but still a key player if able to transfer AW form back to turf.
runners.comment: Good 2nd in an AW handicap 18 days ago; big player if transferring that back to the grass
runners.owner: G H Thompson & S P C Woods

runners.name: Full Gas
runners.spotlight: More encouraging signs with his staying-on fourth of 13 at Newcastle (6f) 12 days ago; dropped 2lb for his troubles and now has headgear added for the first time; could have a bigger effort lurking.
runners.comment: More encouragement to be gleaned from recent AW 4th; headgear added today; one to consider

runners.name: Flash Rascal
runners.spotlight: 6f AW winner in November (form solid) and several good placed runs followed; he has more to prove on turf but he won't be far away if able to transfer his form back to the grass.
runners.comment: 6f AW win in November and several good runs have followed; he has more to prove on turf

runners.name: Black Endeavour
runners.spotlight: Followed August's 6f win at Nottingham with two solid efforts over 7f; down the field on this month's return to action at Kempton but that should have sharpened him up and he is feasibly weighted.
runners.comment: Needs to come on for this month's reappearance but not out of it on his 2yo best

runners.name: Stoic Poet
runners.spotlight: No immediate improvement for the switch to handicaps when eighth of nine over 7f here seven months ago; his novice and maiden runs had threatened better though and he still has time to fulfil that promise; betting useful.
runners.comment: Low-key nursery debut here (7f) seven months ago; still has time to do better

runners.name: Jenny Jerome
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden but she went close at Bath (5f, good) three weeks ago on her return from a six-month absence; returning to 6f should be fine and she is not fully exposed just yet; major contender.
runners.comment: 0-6 but ran a good race when second at Bath (5f, good) three weeks ago; major contender
runners.owner: Pcf Racing Ltd Ceramic Tile Merchants

runners.name: Call Me By My Name
runners.spotlight: Early promise for James Horton last season but his campaign rather fizzled out; starts out for a new trainer (sold for 5,000gns in October) off a reduced mark; worth a market check.
runners.comment: 2yo campaign fizzled out but he starts out for his new stable off a reduced mark

runners.name: Adalida
runners.spotlight: Eight of her nine runs have come in novice/maiden company but her one handicap run saw her finish second over C&D last July off an 8lb higher mark; returns from an 86-day break without any accessories; betting useful.
runners.comment: Returns to handicap company off a reduced mark; has C&D form; no accessories today

runners.name: Ellie's de Vega
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2yo campaign with a couple of creditable runs in 7f AW nurseries; drops back to 6f for his seasonal return and with blinkers enlisted (wore cheekpieces final 2yo run); others look stronger.
runners.comment: Needs the new headgear/return to 6f to give him a lift judged on his 2yo exploits

runners.name: Houndhill
runners.spotlight: Failed to win in five 2yo runs but has shown ability on both AW and turf; this is a deeper race than he was contesting when last seen though.
runners.comment: Promise as a 2yo but this is a better race than he was contesting when last seen
runners.owner: J Raybould N Skinner D Jenkins

runners.name: John Barleycorn
runners.spotlight: Second of seven on his nursery debut last July (7f, good to firm) but that was followed by two lacklustre efforts (gelded before latest); back down in trip for his reappearance and goes in blinkers for the first time.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with two low-key runs; blinkers added for his return; drops back to 6f

runners.name: G'Emmethegreenlite
runners.spotlight: Two promising 5f runs to begin his career (trained by Adrian Keatley as a 2yo) but he didn't get home over 6f when last seen; gelded and sporting a hood on this seasonal/stable/handicap debut; opening mark not an obvious gift though.
runners.comment: Makes stable/seasonal/handicap debut off a tough-looking mark; hooded for the first time

runners.name: Andalprofit
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; close second at Newcastle (6f) last month but he failed to back it up at Southwell three weeks ago; all his best efforts have come on a straight course so perhaps can be given the benefit of the doubt for last time; one of two runners for the stable; not discounted.
runners.comment: Chance on his best efforts this year and the return to a straight track may help

runners.name: Dandy Dinmont
runners.spotlight: Conditions shouldn't be an excuse and he didn't run badly at Bath (5f, good) three weeks ago after a short break; Tom Marquand a notable booking; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Not beaten far at Bath three weeks ago (5f, good); Tom Marquand booked; each-way shout

runners.name: Punchbowl Flyer
runners.spotlight: Knocking at the door over 6f and 7f on AW this year; won't mind getting back on turf and he should give it another good go from the front.
runners.comment: Knocking at the door on AW (6f/7f) this year; another bold bid likely back on turf

runners.name: Sedgemoor
runners.spotlight: It took a drop into classified company for him to return to winning ways in February (6f, AW; blinkered first time; final run for John Butler); gained a second Newcastle win of the year last month but he raced far too freely back there on Monday; up in class today and likely vulnerable.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins at Newcastle this year; too free back there on Monday; up in class today

runners.name: Good Earth
runners.spotlight: The winner of two of his six starts this year, both over 6f at Southwell; he could only manage fourth of the nine runners back at Southwell 20 days ago and he will need to raise his game to defy this mark.
runners.comment: Two Class 6 wins over 6f at Southwell this year; only fourth off this mark 20 days ago

runners.name: Margorie
runners.spotlight: Sprung a surprise in a soft-ground 6f maiden at Windsor last September; 50-1 and well beaten in two handicaps since (1m and 7f, both Polytrack); return to 6f and the addition of cheekpieces may turn things around but it is still easy to have reservations.
runners.comment: 6f win (soft) last September; poor form in two AW handicaps since; now gets headgear

runners.name: Cherry Cobbler
runners.spotlight: Promising return to action at Chelmsford in October (6f) and that was off a 6lb higher mark; no progress since but 7f has stretched him on his last two starts and he returns to 6f in a lower grade; big player with cheekpieces added for the first time.
runners.comment: Not gone on from Chelmsford 3rd in October but several factors that point to better today

runners.name: Harry Mole
runners.spotlight: No better than midfield in three sprint novice runs in the latter part of 2025 and his opening mark looks tough on that evidence; looks the type his trainer will do well with now handicapping though and a much more prominent showing could be on the cards.
runners.comment: Modest form in sprint novice events last year but may leave that behind now handicapping

runners.name: The Childe Of Hale
runners.spotlight: Strong at the finish when winning a 7f handicap at Doncaster last summer (good to soft); three lesser efforts followed and he returns from his 172 break over a trip that may be too sharp; he is drawn near some good pace though and looks worth a market check.
runners.comment: 7f win last summer; failed to build on it after and drops back in trip after 172 days off
runners.owner: The Good Racing Company Ltd & Partner

runners.name: Judicature
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f novice at Newcastle on his second start last summer; handicaps have proved more troublesome but he is edging down the weights and now drops into 0-70 company for the first time.
runners.comment: Struggled in handicaps following a 6f AW novice win; drops into a 0-70 on his return

runners.name: Fiscal Policy
runners.spotlight: No win on turf since September 2024 but he ran well for third at Thirsk (6f, good) two weeks ago, form that has been boosted by the winner and second running well in big fields since; one to be interested in off the same mark.
runners.comment: Good 3rd at Thirsk two weeks ago; solid contender off the same mark
runners.owner: S Thompson, Mrs R Carr & G Shields

races.race_name: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Turnstile
runners.spotlight: Ran green and hung left from halfway but he was only just run out of third on his debut at Thirsk (good) two weeks ago; that was an encouraging start behind a useful prospect and he's a key player if he can build on that.
runners.comment: Showed ability behind a useful prospect at Thirsk; key player if he can build on that

runners.name: Donegal Rose
runners.spotlight: 35,000euros foal, £44,000 yearling; Kodiac filly who is closely related to 7f AW 2yo winner Dynamite Katie (RPR 78); dam unraced half-sister to French 1m4f Listed winner Balaythous and useful 7f-1m1f winner Saphirside; yard 2-3 with 2yos this season and she needs a close look on debut.
runners.comment: £44,000 yearling; yard 2-3 with 2yos this season and she needs a close look on debut

runners.name: Mr Macartney
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns February foal; well backed into 3-1 at Wolverhampton (AW) two weeks ago but he never got involved before finishing a well-held fourth of seven; should benefit from that experience but he needs plenty of progress on this switch to turf.
runners.comment: Well backed into 3-1 at Wolverhampton but he never got involved; needs improvement

runners.name: Allaboutthelunch
runners.spotlight: March foal; 30,000gns foal, 4,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners Bill Silvers (7f; RPR 99) and Gaelic Approach (1m4f; 77); dam placed 1m-1m4f (78), half-sister to 1m4f winners Empyrea and La Joya; could be one for later on and he's probably best watched on debut.
runners.comment: 4,000gns yearling; could be one for later on and he's probably best watched on debut

runners.name: Indian Land
runners.spotlight: February foal; Land Force half-brother to winners Tribal Craft (1m2f-1m6f including Group 3; RPR 110), Seattle King (7f 2yo/1m2f; 90), Star Maker (11.6f Flat, 81; 2m-2m3f hurdle) and Dream Seeker (German 6.5f); dam 1m/1m2f winner (93); yard has had a 2yo winner this season and had a 20% record with juveniles last term.
runners.comment: Yard 20% with 2yos last season and he needs a close look on debut

runners.name: Top Cote
runners.spotlight: March foal; 7,000euros foal, 15,000euros yearling; first foal of a 7f 2yo winner (RPR 97), out of French 10.5f winning half-sister to 1m1f Flat and 2m-2m3f hurdle/chase winner Authentic Act; others look more likely and he's best watched unless market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: 15,000gns yearling; others look more likely and he's probably best watched on debut

runners.name: Parading
runners.spotlight: Ardad colt; third foal; dam 9.4f/1m2f winner (including 2yo; RPR 87), half-sister to 1m Listed winner Staying On, out of maiden half-sister to Rockfel winner Yawl, family of Oaks winner Talent; has speed and stamina in pedigree and interesting to see how he figures in market.
runners.comment: Has speed and stamina in pedigree and interesting to see how he figures in market

runners.name: Sky Secret
runners.spotlight: February foal; 15,000gns foal, 42,000euros yearling; fifth foal; half-brother to 6f AW winner Black Cab (RPR 56); dam 6f winner (86), sister to 5f/6f Group 3 winner Swiss Diva, half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Swiss Spirit, out of 5f Listed winner; has lots of speed in pedigree and she's an interesting newcomer for yard that that won this race last year.
runners.comment: Has speedy pedigree and she needs a close look for yard that that won this race last year

runners.name: Alpha Legend
runners.spotlight: 32,000euros foal, £24,000 yearling; first foal; dam unraced sister to winners Rhythmic Intent (useful 1m2f-14.5f) and Fortune Triple (HK 1m1f/1m3f), is granddaughter of French Oaks winner Caerlina; has plenty of stamina on dam's side and he could be a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: £24,000 yearling; has plenty of stamina in pedigree and could be a longer-term prospect
runners.owner: Heart Of The South Racing 146 & Partner

runners.name: From Me To You
runners.spotlight: January foal; £55,000 yearling; didn't live up to market expectations when seventh on Newbury debut (good; 7-2) last week but he ran green after a slow start; should know much more this time and it wouldn't be a surprise if he took a major step forward.
runners.comment: Didn't live up to market expectations at Newbury but he should know much more this time

runners.name: Postcard
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns yearling; 16-1 at Bath (good) three weeks ago and she dropped out before before finishing a remote eighth of ten.
runners.comment: 16-1 at Bath three weeks ago and she finished a remote eighth of ten

runners.name: Everatease
runners.spotlight: Big market drifter at Wolverhampton (AW; 11-1) two weeks ago and he finished into a remote sixth of seven; should be more streetwise this time but he needs a transformation on his second start.
runners.comment: Big market drifter at Wolverhampton and he finished a remote sixth of seven

races.race_name: Caffreys Black Stout King Richard III Cup Handicap

runners.name: Zoulu Chief
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably at Lingfield in January when bidding for a 6f AW double; 8lb higher than when second at Newmarket (7f) on last turf attempt but he's proved consistent since being gelded and his first two handicap wins were on good to firm; in the mix.
runners.comment: Reliable type who is very effective on fast ground and he's respected back from a break

runners.name: Betsen
runners.spotlight: Won on stable debut at Southwell (6f, Tapeta) in November and he's posted some solid efforts since then; hard to rule out another good run but this is a step back up in grade and sole turf win was 19 months ago.
runners.comment: In good form on AW but this is a step back up in grade and his sole turf win was in 2024

runners.name: Berkshire Whisper
runners.spotlight: Completed an AW hat-trick when scoring on his handicap debut at Newcastle (6f) last April; held in six subsequent runs but he went close off this mark at Southwell in November and was a creditable third at Newcastle three weeks ago; now returns to turf but he ran well in a fast-ground Listed event last spring and he's still unexposed in this sphere; in the mix.
runners.comment: Placed off this mark at Newcastle latest and he's still unexposed on turf; in the mix
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Strike Red
runners.spotlight: On lengthy losing but he's a smart handicapper on his day and he returned from five months off with a good third at Newcastle (6f, AW) two weeks ago; should benefit from that run and he remains well treated on his second in the Stewards' Cup last August; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Second in Stewards' Cup last summer and runner-up at Newcastle 12 days ago; respected

runners.name: Fast Track Harry
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner who made it 2-2 on AW when completing a double at Newcastle (6f; off 4lb lower) last month; didn't fire in his hat-trick bid at Kempton but he still has potential and is not ruled out in suitable conditions back on turf.
runners.comment: Held in hat-trick bid at Kempton but he still has potential and is not ruled back on turf

runners.name: Dark Cloud Rising
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who ended his 2025 campaign with a success and creditable effort over 5f at Newcastle (AW); was as good as ever in those two runs but he was well below form on his reappearance at Pontefract (5f, good to soft) 18 days ago; may have needed that run but he needs more progress to defy this mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner who scored on AW in November but he needs more progress to defy this mark

runners.name: Roman Dragon
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on his best form last season but he's not easy to predict and has been up and down during another spell in Bahrain since December; comes with risks attached back in Britain and most of his wins have been at Chester; tongue-tie worn last time is removed.
runners.comment: Chester specialist who is not easy to predict and others are preferred

runners.name: Ten Pounds
runners.spotlight: Record of 5-10 for Harry Charlton and his last win was in a conditions event at Kempton (6f, AW) in October; ran without his declared tongue-tie when fourth on stable debut at Southwell in January but he finished down the field in another Class 2 handicap at Newcastle two weeks later; conditions should be fine on this return to turf but he needs to step up again after another break.
runners.comment: Record of 5-12 but he was down the field at Newcastle last time; needs more after a break

runners.name: Annaf
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a game win in Group 3 at Ascot (6f, soft) in October; repeatedly met trouble when only seventh in Saudi Arabia (a race he'd won two years earlier) in February and things didn't really go his way again in a Listed race at Doncaster last month; has possibilities on this drop back in class and his last handicap win was off 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner over 6f last autumn and he has possibilities back in a handicap

runners.name: Prince Of India
runners.spotlight: Won four times during a progressive campaign last year but he's come up short in Listed/Group 3 events this spring; seems ideally suited by fast ground but he's still 4lb higher than for his last win and has some work to do back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Four wins last year but his progress seems to have flattened out; others preferred

runners.name: Fivethousandtoone
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner who has finished close up on Tapeta (5f/6f) in his last three runs; record of 1-26 on turf but he's on a lower mark in this sphere and he ran well in the Great St Wilfrid at Ripon (6f, good to firm) last summer; dangerous.
runners.comment: 1-26 on turf but he's been in good form on AW and he's on lower mark back in this sphere

runners.name: Kylian
runners.spotlight: Listed winner (5f/6f) for his previous yards and he's run respectably in two 5f handicaps for his new trainer this spring; needs to find some extra spark but he's edging down the weights and this step back up in trip looks a good move.
runners.comment: Fair efforts in two runs for new yard and he could be dangerous back up in trip
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xii & Mrs G Cowell

runners.name: Russet Gold
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins for Roger Varian but latest was in June 2023 and he was quiet last season; returns for new yard after wind surgery but he has a mixed record when fresh and this is highly competitive; down the list.
runners.comment: Returns after wind surgery but has mixed record when fresh and his last win was in 2023

runners.name: Wreck It Ryley
runners.spotlight: Winner of both C&D starts, in October (good to soft) and when pulling well clear (good) 12 days ago; has won on good to firm so the warm weather shouldn't be a problem but the new 9lb higher mark might be.
runners.comment: 2-2 over C&D, pulling well clear 12 days ago (good); needs to defy a 9lb rise

runners.name: Mr Lightside
runners.spotlight: Can lead; smart 2yo who began 3yo season on a mark of 102; has shown just humdrum form in three turf handicaps, despite a sliding mark, and was way back behind Justcallmepete at Bath last week; has it to prove now.
runners.comment: Smart 2yo; humdrum form in three turf handicaps, way back at Bath last week

runners.name: Dapper Valley
runners.spotlight: Had ups and downs since showing decent form earlier in career and was well treated when starting out for this trainer in 2025, winning three of his last four 6f starts on AW and turf; went down only narrowly back on turf (good to firm; headgear left off) in UAE during March and he's of interest now back with former yard.
runners.comment: Well treated when winning 3 of last 4 on AW/turf (6f) in 2025; good turf run in UAE latest

runners.name: Justcallmepete
runners.spotlight: Has a better strike-rate on AW than turf but he's capable of useful 6f form on grass and made short work of his rivals at Bath (extended 5f, good) last week; now 6lb higher but this mark isn't beyond him on past exploits; would have welcomed rain but still considered.
runners.comment: Clear winner at Bath (extended 5f) last week; 6lb higher but this mark is not beyond him

runners.name: Dashing Dick
runners.spotlight: Said goodbye to 2025 with a Newmarket win (6f, good to soft) in November; has won on good to firm but he missed a recent intended return on the surface and this might be needed anyway (soundly beaten here last April).
runners.comment: Won at Newmarket (good to soft) in November; well beaten in this race on return last year

runners.name: Under The Twilight
runners.spotlight: Should be all the better for her Doncaster reappearance and she's back on the same mark as for her Windsor success (6f, good to firm) last June; however, all her turf wins have come there and at Salisbury and this is the first time she's run on this track.
runners.comment: Back on the same mark as Windsor win (6f, good to firm) last June; first run on this track

runners.name: Ormolulu
runners.spotlight: Weighted to go well on best turf form and the drying ground shouldn't cause her any grief, having run so well on good to firm at York (6f) last June; also a winner of her one C&D start, in 2023, and the booking of Billy Loughnane signals intent; has prospects despite lacking a recent run.
runners.comment: 1-1 over C&D; lacks recent run but weighted to go well on best 6f form on good to firm

runners.name: Von Krolock
runners.spotlight: 0-31 on turf but he acted perfectly well on the surface in Ireland; first two AW wins for this yard came over 7f but he wasn't bothered by the return to 6f when winning at Wolverhampton 19 days ago; this stiff 6f should suit fine; solid chance.
runners.comment: 0-31 on turf but acts perfectly well on grass; successful return to 6f on AW latest; solid

runners.name: Shark Two One
runners.spotlight: Five turf wins at 6f-7f, the latest two over the latter trip on this track; he's had some excuses for low-key reappearances in the past but it's still a stretch to expect him to be on song for this one.
runners.comment: Likes it here and last 2 wins on this track (7f); needs more on usual reappearance efforts

runners.name: Mickey Mongoose
runners.spotlight: Sole turf win came over 1m2f in 2023 and three subsequent successes have been on the AW (1m and 7f); has continued to run well since the latest of them in February but this is his first 6f run since his debut.
runners.comment: Sole turf win at 1m2f; three AW wins at 1m/7f; in form but having first 6f run since debut

runners.name: Cameley Days
runners.spotlight: Well held on AW in November but progressive on turf for new yard previously, following a close second at Yarmouth (6f, good) with wins there (good to soft) and at Bath (extended 5f, heavy); may well do better on good to firm as her dam's best form for this yard came on the same going.
runners.comment: Two sprint wins (good to soft and heavy) for new yard in the autumn; can find more

runners.name: Starsong
runners.spotlight: Both turf wins on Brighton's sharp track but she made up plenty of ground when a never-nearer third in this race (good) last year and it wouldn't surprise to see her return to form after a low-key Southwell run last time, having gone close there on her reappearance the time before.
runners.comment: Close 2nd on AW return; well held since but chance on strong-finishing 3rd here last year

runners.name: Just King High
runners.spotlight: Won 2yo AW maiden; his 3yo season never really got going but he was on a much-reduced mark when runner-up on two occasions (7f) in March; well held on yard debut since, though, and likely best watched back on turf.
runners.comment: Won 2yo AW maiden; tough in 2025; found some form on AW in March; held latest for new yard

runners.name: Instant Bond
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two turf handicaps either side of a break in 2025 but he was off the mark on the AW in March and has run respectably in defeat since; might well have more to offer on this surface.
runners.comment: Well beaten in 2 turf handicaps in 2025; broke duck on AW in March; may do better on grass
runners.owner: David Tate, Danny Lawson & Kevin Broadley

runners.name: Dark Sun
runners.spotlight: Not yet off the mark but she's had two close calls on the AW this year, notably when only just failing to get up at Wolverhampton (6f) in February, against one on the fifth leg of a seven-timer; acts on turf, too, and may well figure back from short break.
runners.comment: 0-15 but had two close calls over 6f on AW this year; acts on turf and may well figure

runners.name: Call Glory
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins in January, over 6f and 7f; just averagely handicapped now and it's a long time since he's run well on turf but he wouldn't be out of it on past form on grass.
runners.comment: Two AW wins in 2026; long time since he's run well on turf but not out of it on past form

runners.name: Dragon God
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden for two yards but he didn't get the best of runs after a slow start at Bath (first 5f run; good) and may well be ready to play more of a part now back at 6f following a further 3lb drop.
runners.comment: Lightly raced maiden; not disgraced over 5f latest; down 3lb; can do better back at 6f

runners.name: Heyzoom
runners.spotlight: Brother to 1m/8.5f winner Rowayeh (RPR 94), closely related to 8.6f AW 2yo/1m2f turf Listed winner Falakeyah (106); held in third when odds-on at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in November but he finished well behind two rivals who were ridden more prominently; sets a good standard on that promising effort and he's open to progress upped in trip.
runners.comment: Sets standard on his debut third at Wolverhampton and he's open to progress upped in trip

runners.name: Act Of Generosity
runners.spotlight: 875,000gns yearling; first foal; dam Listed-placed 1m/8.5f winner (RPR 98), half-sister to winners Bite Of The Cherry (useful 1m5f), Pietra Dura (7f 2yo) and Ever Rigg (1m4f; dam of 1m2f-1m4f Group 1 winner Postponed), out of Moyglare Stud/Lowther winner; interesting newcomer and he holds Derby entry.
runners.comment: 875,000gns yearling; striking newcomer and he holds a Derby entry

runners.name: King Of Thebes
runners.spotlight: Cost 1,500,000gns as a yearling but he's already been gelded and was a remote third with cheekpieces applied on Southwell debut (1m3f, Tapeta) two weeks ago; that was behind a smart prospect but he has quite a bit to find here and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Cost 1,500,000gns as a yearling but he was well held with headgear applied on recent debut

runners.name: Yafreh
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; brother to 7f-1m2f winner Al Mubhir (including Listed; RPR 115), half-brother to three winners including Yabher (1m-1m4f; 96); dam 7f-1m1f winner (including Group 3; 112); has good standard to aim at on debut but he needs checking in market.
runners.comment: Brother to smart 7f-1m2f winner Al Mubhir; needs checking in market on debut

runners.name: Superior Choice
runners.spotlight: 1,100,000gns yearling; never involved when 3-1 at Newmarket (1m, good) in October but he made some good late headway to finish fourth of seven; that was a promising start and he needs a close look upped in trip on his return.
runners.comment: 1,100.000gns yearling; eye-catcher at Newmarket in October and needs close look on return

runners.name: Astracornus
runners.spotlight: 20-1 on Chelmsford debut (1m) in November and he was always in rear; been gelded since and can only be watched on his return.
runners.comment: 20-1 on Chelmsford debut (1m) in November and he was always in rear; been gelded since

runners.name: Silkies Sib
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner who has form figures of 133221363 on AW since Boxing Day and his last win was over 1m2f; still looks feasibly treated on his best form and he's in the mix back on turf (acts on good ground but he's untried on quicker).
runners.comment: In good form on AW this year and he's in the mix back on turf

runners.name: Blackwater Lilly
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner who has finished close up in handicaps at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) in her last two starts; 0-8 on the Flat but she ran respectably in big field at Doncaster (1m2f) last month and is not ruled out back in this sphere.
runners.comment: In good form over hurdles in last two starts and she's not ruled out back on the Flat

runners.name: Happy Chandler
runners.spotlight: On reduced mark and he returned from a break with a good second behind an improver at Bath (1m, good) three weeks ago; has claims if he can back that up but he's still a maiden 16 starts and has stamina to prove at this new trip.
runners.comment: Back from a break with a good second at Bath but he's now 0-16 and is untried at this trip

runners.name: Evelyn's Phoenix
runners.spotlight: 5yo who turned things around with a rallying win on his Nottingham reappearance (8.3f, good; 13-2) last Saturday; back up 4lb in the weights but he remains well treated on best form and his penultimate win was over this trip; key player.
runners.comment: Turned things around with a rallying win on Nottingham reappearance (8.3f); key player

runners.name: Beauty Generation
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner (1m) but he's 0-10 on turf; on reduced mark and was fair third at Wolverhampton (9.4f) last month but he's struggled in his last five turf runs and has something to prove back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner but he's 0-10 on turf and has something to prove back in this sphere

runners.name: Shabu Shabu
runners.spotlight: Won a small-field handicap at Yarmouth (11.5f) last August but has mixed record since and was tailed off at Wolverhampton (1m6f) 19 days ago; may have had stamina issues last time but he's now 1-13 and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Won at Yarmouth last summer but has mixed record since and others are more convincing
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xxii & Da Bin Lo

runners.name: He's Our Cracker
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt when landing a Chelmsford handicap (1m2f, AW) last month; justified support with that convincing win but he was only fifth of ten in his follow-up bid at Wolverhampton (9.4f); has bit to prove again after that and he's struggled in two previous runs on turf.
runners.comment: Won at Chelmsford (1m2f) last month but was only fifth of ten at Wolverhampton last time

runners.name: Iftikhaar
runners.spotlight: Back to form with a near-miss at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in January but he's taken backward steps in two runs since; overall record is now 1-37 and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Overall record of 1-37 and he's finished down the field on AW in last two starts

runners.name: Porter's Song
runners.spotlight: Well held in sole start for Andre Fabre in France and same outcome in two runs for new yard this spring; still early days and now goes handicapping but he needs improvement at this new trip.
runners.comment: Well held in three qualifying runs and he needs improvement at new trip on handicap debut

runners.name: Not So Sobers
