runners.spotlight: Bumper winner and a fair maiden hurdler; 0-6 on the Flat but he was a good third in a Chelmsford handicap (1m6f) in February and the drop to back to 8.6f looked against him at Wolverhampton last time; should get a good tow into this race and he could be dangerous back up in trip.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who is lightly raced on the Flat and could be dangerous back up in trip

runners.name: Celtic Spirit
runners.spotlight: Big prices and he finished in rear in three novice events (6f/7f) towards the end of last year; now goes handicapping after a break but he needs a transformation on this step up to 1m2f.
runners.comment: Didn't show a great deal in novice events and he needs a transformation on handicap debut

runners.name: Miss Chester
runners.spotlight: Won a Kempton maiden (7f, AW) last spring but she's struggled in handicaps since and was beaten 7l back at Kempton two weeks ago; continues to drop down the weights but she has plenty to prove back up in trip.
runners.comment: Kempton maiden winner but she's struggled in handicaps and has lots to prove

runners.name: Shifra
runners.spotlight: Struggled at big prices in her four Flat runs and same story in two maiden hurdles this year; this is only her second handicap but she needs major improvement back on the Flat.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much at big prices in six runs under both codes; passed over

runners.name: Yokkell
runners.spotlight: Ex-Kevin Philippart de Foy; formerly a useful performer and was successful at Nottingham (8.3f, good to firm) last April; now 24lb lower than for that win but she's struggled since and has form figures of 9050876 for her current yard; opposable.
runners.comment: 7yo who has lost her way and has form figures of 9050876 for current yard; opposable

runners.name: Lion Of Mali
runners.spotlight: Beaten a nose on handicap debut at Newcastle (7f), after which he was gelded; won on Tapeta and Polytrack in next two starts and was a creditable second in his hat-trick bid at Kempton (7f) three weeks ago; still lightly raced and he's a big player if he can transfer his progress back on turf.
runners.comment: Record of 2112 in AW handicaps; big player if he can transfer his progress back to turf

runners.name: Horwich
runners.spotlight: Won novice/maiden events at Wolverhampton (7f) for Clive Cox on his first two runs; finished in rear in three handicaps for his current yard but he ran into plenty of trouble over 6f at Southwell 18 days ago; now makes his turf debut but he's on a reduced mark and is not ruled back up in trip.
runners.comment: On reduced mark and he ran into plenty of trouble at Southwell (6f) latest; not ruled out

runners.name: Pretty Danielle
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden but she returned after a break with a close third in a Lingfield handicap (7f, AW) last month; should benefit from that run and she's respected off only 1lb higher back on turf.
runners.comment: 0-7 but she returned after a break with a close third at Lingfield (7f, AW); respected

runners.name: Venetian Romance
runners.spotlight: Made a promising start when third at Lingfield (7f, good) last August but she's been well held in three subsequent runs including on handicap/stable debut at Nottingham (8.3f, good) two weeks ago; should be sharper with that run under her belt but she needs improvement back in trip; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly but she's not progressed so far and needs improvement back in trip

runners.name: Daretobedifferent
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who has gone the wrong in handicaps and was always in rear on Newmarket reappearance (7f, good; hood added) 11 days ago; mark continues to fall but she needs a big turnaround on this drop back in grade.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden who has gone the wrong way in handicaps; others preferred

runners.name: Circle Of Trust
runners.spotlight: No progress in three qualifying runs but he found improvement upped to 7f on nursery debut when a strong-finishing second at Yarmouth (good to soft) in September; only 1lb higher than for that close call and he's an interesting contender with cheekpieces added on his return.
runners.comment: Went close in Yarmouth nursery in final 2yo run; respected with headgear added on return
runners.owner: Mrs R G Hillen And Partner

runners.name: Yachtsman
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but he's reached the frame in two handicaps for his current yard and was beaten less than 2l at Lingfield (7f, AW) last month; hasn't looked entirely straightforward but he didn't get the best of runs last time and he's in the mix back on turf; hood reapplied
runners.comment: Has reached the frame in two AW handicaps for current yard; in the mix with hood reapplied

runners.name: Castlekeely
runners.spotlight: Well held in three qualifying runs (6f) for Eve Johnson Houghton last season; 4-1 favourite on handicap/stable debut over C&D (good) 12 days ago but he was always in rear and finished 15th of 17; has good pedigree and is still early days but he needs to leave that latest form a long way behind.
runners.comment: Disappointing favourite over C&D on recent handicap/stable debut; bit to prove

runners.name: Chelio
runners.spotlight: Well held in his first two juvenile runs but he showed clear promise when runner-up in a Lingfield novice (1m, AW) in December; drops back in trip after a break but he pulled hard last time and is open to more progress on this handicap debut; interesting contender with Rossa Ryan booked.
runners.comment: Promising second at Lingfield (1m) and he looks interesting back in trip on handicap debut

runners.name: Sprinting Speed
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three runs for Richard Hannon last summer and was tenth of 12 on his recent handicap/stable debut at Southwell (6f, Tapeta; 11-1); still early days but he needs a transformation at this new trip.
runners.comment: Finished down the field in all four runs and he needs a transformation at this new trip

runners.name: Great Success
runners.spotlight: Close third of four on debut at Pontefract (6f, good to firm) last August; didn't match that in two runs during the winter but he's been gelded since and is a possible improver now returned to turf on his handicap debut; needs close look for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Been gelded and he's a possible improver back on turf on handicap debut; needs close look

runners.name: Eduardo Calderon
runners.spotlight: Third of nine on his debut at Brighton (6f, good) last May but went backwards from that in two subsequent runs last season; still unexposed and now goes handicapping at this new trip but he needs to raise his game after six months off.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer but he needs to raise his game after six months off

runners.name: Sporting Hero
runners.spotlight: Nine AW wins but is a maiden on turf after 18 runs; however, he ran a cracker on soft ground at Bellewstown last time and this drying surface should suit better; a squeak.
runners.comment: Turf maiden ran a cracker on soft ground at Bellewstown last time and has  a squeak

runners.name: Asdana
runners.spotlight: Won over this trip at Cork last August and some fine efforts in defeat since including a fourth at Bellewstown last time; back to her winning mark so considered; can be slowly away.
runners.comment: Fourth at Bellewstown last time; back to her winning mark so considered

runners.name: Andosky
runners.spotlight: Best effort when runner-up in a Sligo nursery last year; soundly beaten over 6f and 7f both starts in soft this term; has to improve for better ground and drop in trip.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten both starts in soft this term; has to improve for this ground down in trip

runners.name: An Laochmor
runners.spotlight: Won over this trip on AW at Lingfield and Dundalk the last twice, his only wins; has run well at times on turf including at this course; has to do more but has to be considered.
runners.comment: Two AW wins lately; turf maiden but has run well here; needs more but considered

runners.name: Arctic Steps
runners.spotlight: AW winner over this trip in November and fine efforts in defeat back there last twice, including when just behind Sporting Hero; has won on turf so has a shout.
runners.comment: Fine efforts in defeat on AW last twice; won on turf last year so has a shout

runners.name: Edmond Halley
runners.spotlight: Best efforts came in 5f-6f maidens at this track last October on yld/sft ground; well below that level in three handicaps including on the AW last time; this track does suit; cheekpieces back on.
runners.comment: Best efforts came in 5f-6f maidens at this track; poor since in h'caps; cheekpieces back

runners.name: Nouvel Espoir
runners.spotlight: Three wins including over C&D; well beaten in soft ground on Curragh return but this surface will suit better; inexperienced rider.
runners.comment: Well beaten in soft on Curragh return but this surface will suit more; inexperienced rider
runners.owner: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill

runners.name: Apache Star
runners.spotlight: Three wins when trained in UK with the last in 2023; well beaten in testing ground last twice but might go better today; cheekpieces instead of blinkers.
runners.comment: Well beaten in testing ground last twice but might go better today; cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Poweracclaim
runners.spotlight: Has posted some fine runs in defeat in maidens over 6f-7f including here last time; has early speed so could cope with the drop in trip with the hood worn last time retained; considered.
runners.comment: Solid 6f here last time; has early speed so could cope with the drop in trip; considered

runners.name: Irish Ballet
runners.spotlight: Best run came when third in a C&D maiden as a 2yo; however, she has been well below that since including three AW runs back from an absence; opposed.
runners.comment: Third in a C&D maiden as a 2yo but well below that since including three AW runs; opposed

runners.name: Verified
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 19 starts for former yard but several fine efforts in defeat including here; ground-versatile and could run well if ready enough on return.
runners.comment: Several fine efforts in defeat including here; ground-versatile; could run well on return

runners.name: Romantic War
runners.spotlight: Maiden is out of form and 9lb out of the handicap so look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Maiden is out of form and 9lb out of the handicap so look elsewhere

runners.name: Polar Bear
runners.spotlight: On a losing run since April 2023; however, put up a sound effort here over 6f here (sft) on return off this mark; handles better ground so if improving from that has a chance.
runners.comment: Sound effort here over 6f here on return off this mark; handles better ground so chance

runners.name: Skillman Ave
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over this trip at Tipperary last July off 68 for Gavin Cromwell and a fine second over 6f here next time; however, has been regressive since and has to get back to form.
runners.comment: A 5f winner last year and 2nd over 6f here; regressive since and has to get back to form

runners.name: Kilmood Susan
runners.spotlight: Won a Cork maiden over this trip for her former yard last summer in first-time blinkers; has been well below that level on soft ground since but should do better on this drying surface; blinkers left off; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Well below best on soft ground since Cork win but should do better on this drying surface

runners.name: Verhoyen
runners.spotlight: Fine servant is a seven-time turf winner though didn't score in 2025; has run well in defeat at this track but has never won over 5f; poor last time; opposed.
runners.comment: Has run well in defeat at this track but has never won over 5f; poor last time; opposed
runners.owner: Paul Cullen & Joseph E Keeling & M C Grassick

runners.name: Arizona Raider
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far on recent Curragh debut and can run respectably but likely to find some of these too smart also.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on Curragh debut but likely to find some of these too smart also

runners.name: West Of Eden
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 81), half-sister to 1m4f Listed winner Heliac, out of French 1m5f winning half-sister to winners Meteor Storm (US 1m2f Grade 1) and Polish Summer (UAE 1m4f Group 1); Eur500,000 yearling is by a speed influence with quality stamina in damline; of obvious interest so check the market.
runners.comment: A Eur500,000 yearling by a top sire and from a quality damline; check market

runners.name: Barrow Boy
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to 7f-1m2f winner for this yard Snellen (inc 2yo/Group 3; RPR 113); dam 1m2f turf/1m4f AW winner (103), half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 3 winner Night Lagoon (dam of King George winner Novellist); by a first-season sire; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Half-brother to yard's smart Group 3 winner Snellen; worth a market check

runners.name: Margot Mae
runners.spotlight: A 115,000gns yearling; eighth foal; half-sister to winners incl' New Endeavour (7f-1m1f inc 2yo/AW/Australian Group 3; RPR 117), and Johnny Boom (8.6f-1m4f inc AW; 80); dam unraced half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Luminous Eyes; by a quality sire and related to lots of winners so check market.
runners.comment: A 115,000gns yearling; by a quality sire and related to lots of winners so check market

runners.name: King Of Cloughan
runners.spotlight: Eur110,000 yearling; first foal; dam US 5.5f/6f turf 2yo winner (inc stakes; RPR 99), half-sister to US stakes-placed 1m/8.5f winner Tempers Flair, out of 5.5f stakes winning half-sister to BC Juvenile winner Boston Harbor; by a promising sire; from a quality US damline; a likely player.
runners.comment: Cost Eur110,000 as a yearling; by a promising sire; from a quality US damline; a player

runners.name: Abletai
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 5f winner (RPR 83), half-sister to winners Texas Rock (6f/7f Listed) and We Dare To Dream (7f 2yo/7.4f), out of unraced half-sister to useful 5f/6f 2yo winner Rosabee; dam was useful over this trip and a sibling to some useful sorts; by a quality sire; not ignored.
runners.comment: Dam a 5f winner and sibling to some useful sorts; by a quality sire; not ignored

runners.name: Tribeca
runners.spotlight: Eur250,000 yearling was later resold for 575,000gns; second foal; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 80), sister to 1m1f Group 3 winner Carla Bianca, half-sister to 7f Listed winner Joailliere, out of unraced half-sister to 1m4f-1m6f Group 3 winner Profound Beauty; by a leading sire and from a fine Moyglare damline; check the market.
runners.comment: A 575,000gns yearling; by a leading sire and from a fine Moyglare damline;  market check
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Mrs A O'Callaghan

runners.name: Amigos Legacy
runners.spotlight: Decent pedigree but was beaten 13l on debut at Dundalk and has to do much better.
runners.comment: Beaten 13l on debut at Dundalk and has to do much better

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Committed Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Charles Darwin
runners.spotlight: Brother to Bluebeard (Prix Morny and Middle Park); only fourth of seven when 4-6 for his Curragh debut (5f, good) last April; made handsome amends with wins in a C&D maiden (soft), a four-runner Naas conditions event (5f good) and the Norfolk Stakes, at the main expense of Middle Park winner Wise Approach; top-class sprinting prospect, reportedly in good shape.
runners.comment: A cut above these rivals on his Norfolk Stakes defeat of the subsequent Middle Park winner

runners.name: Chicago Call
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f Fairyhouse maiden over this trip last July; best form when just denied in a Listed event at the same track in September (6f yielding); below form in the Goffs Million on only 7f attempt; soft ground probably not ideal on seasonal debut at Cork.
runners.comment: Winner and Listed-placed at two, has a run under his belt, soft ground may not have suited
runners.owner: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit

runners.name: Frescobaldi
runners.spotlight: Ex-Aidan O'Brien won the 7f Convivial at York on his third outing; confirmed a fair standard of form in 7f Listed races at Doncaster and Dundalk before changing hands for 100,000gns; useful prospect for Danny Murphy; unlikely to match former stablemate Charles Darwin.
runners.comment: Ex-Ballydoyle, Convivial winner, unlikely to match his former stablemate Charles Darwin
runners.owner: N W T Dalzell & R P Behan & Mrs John Murphy

runners.name: Focaccia
runners.spotlight: Debut third at the Curragh four weeks ago suggests she can win races; this looks much too demanding at this stage of her career, but worth a shot with black type available.
runners.comment: Debut third at the Curragh suggests she can win races, this looks much too demanding

runners.name: Starman Tom
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in finishing second behind last season's Queen Mary fourth on debut in a 5f maiden at Cork last month; beaten favourite when third in a big-field 6f maiden of a relatively modest standard at the Curragh last Sunday.
runners.comment: Maiden who failed to build on his promising Cork run when third at the Curragh last Sunday
runners.owner: Poor Fools Syndicate/Deirdre Cotter/Thomas W. McGrath

runners.name: Kindergarten
runners.spotlight: Made most of the running when a 7/1 winner on debut over C&D last October; placed horses Ocean's Breath and Anushka have both won at Dundalk; should not be out of place at this level.
runners.comment: Winner over C&D on debut last October, should not be out of place at this level

runners.name: Silk Braid
runners.spotlight: Beat a Joseph O'Brien-trained odds-on chance in a 6f Curragh maiden last October on her only start at two; hard to know what level she can reach; tough task against Charles Darwin.
runners.comment: 6f Curragh maiden winner, tough task against a colt of the calibre of Charles Darwin

races.race_name: BAR 1 Betting Vintage Crop Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Scandinavia
runners.spotlight: Won four of his five races at three, the exception when a close-up fifth behind Carmers in the 1m6f Queen's Vase; improved with cheekpieces, winning a five-runner 1m5f Group 3 at Newmarket by almost 9l and mastering older stablemate Illinois in the Goodwood Cup (2m, good); dug deep to hold off Rahieb on soft ground in the St Leger (Carmers fifth); top-class stayer.
runners.comment: Goodwood Cup winner, confirmed progression with St Leger triumph, Gold Cup candidate
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Emit
runners.spotlight: Consistent last season apart from a poor run in the Hampton Court Stakes at Royal Ascot. went close in a 1m2f Group 3 at the Curragh in May; Listed winner at Gowran (1m4f, good) in July before runner-up in Group 2 and Group 3 (both over 1m7f on very soft ground) in France; failed to show his best form when fourth behind Layfayette on reappearance at the Curragh.
runners.comment: Good form last season on home soil and in France, well below his best on reappearance

runners.name: Carmers
runners.spotlight: Won his first three races, notably 1m6f Group 2 Queen's Vase at Royal Ascot, beating the subsequent Geoffrey Freer winner; Furthur; lost his unbeaten record but emerged with credit in taking second in the 1m4f Great Voltigeur at York; hung left and dropped away to finish fifth behind Scandinavia when running on soft for the first time in the St Leger.
runners.comment: Queen's Vase winner with Scandinavia fifth, that rival turned the tables in the St Leger

runners.name: Dallas Star
runners.spotlight: Won Leopardstown Group 3 in April 2024 (1m2f, heavy); last win was at Dundalk early last year; no match for Leinster in a St Leger trial at the Curragh last August; twice placed in small-field AW events during the winter; well held in third behind Layfayette at the Curragh early this month; up against it.
runners.comment: Well held in third behind Layfayette at the Curragh early this month, up against it

runners.name: Sunchart
runners.spotlight: Useful dual-purpose sort, 3-34 on Flat (and second on ten occasions); his only two stakes race wins from 28 attempts have been in the 1m2f Naas event in which he was third of eight last month; suited by testing conditions, drying ground against him.
runners.comment: Durable sort, suited by testing conditions, drying ground against him

runners.name: Leinster
runners.spotlight: Stepped outside of handicap company (fourth in Chester Cup and in Ascot Stakes) to win a four-runner St Leger Trial at the Curragh last August, readily brushing aside front-running Dallas Star in the final furlong; faced a very stiff task but ran no sort of race when tailed off in the Irish St Leger.
runners.comment: Beat Dallas Star in a 1m6f Curragh event last August, tailed off in the Irish St Leger

runners.name: Layfayette
runners.spotlight: Winner of ten races; last of six stakes race wins was in 2023 Royal Whip over 1m2f at the Curragh; however, he proved that he retains considerable ability and enthusiasm when winning a conditions event over this trip at the Curragh on seasonal debut;  (Dallas Star third, Emit fourth), that was his first 1m6f attempt, on his 47th outing.
runners.comment: Retains considerable ability and enthusiasm judged on Curragh win on first 1m6f attempt

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Salsabil Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)

runners.name: Thundering On
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly, whose dam won the 1m2f Pretty Polly for this stable; fine effort on second juvenile start when second in 1m Group 3  at the Curragh, 4 3/4l in front of third-placed Ice Dancer; touched off in a 1m maiden at Leopardstown 13 days ago; trip will suit, stable in terrific form; could feature here despite her maiden status.
runners.comment: Trip will suit, stable in terrific form; could feature here despite her maiden status

runners.name: Minerva
runners.spotlight: Ran seven times at two, opening her account on final start, when odds-on in a 7f maiden at Leopardstown; best effort when second to top-class stablemate Diamond Necklace (unbeaten Prix Marcel Boussac winner) in a 7f Curragh conditions event; possible stamina doubt, and Ryan Moore prefers the lower-rated, less exposed Ice Dancer.
runners.comment: Possible stamina doubt, Ryan Moore prefers the lower-rated but less exposed Ice Dancer

runners.name: Emmeleia
runners.spotlight: Half-sister by Camelot to 2025 Lingfield Derby Trial winner Puppet Master; won a 7f barrier trial at Leopardstown last August, second to the promising Yellowstone Lake n a 7f maiden at Dundalk in September; batted well to beat a stablemate over a bit further than this at Dundalk; needs to improve, drying ground should help.
runners.comment: Beat a stablemate at Dundalk, gets this trip, needs to improve, drying ground should help

runners.name: Chasing Paradise
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when 7l second to an experienced Ballydoyle-trained filly over 1m at Leopardstown on sole start at two; stayed this trip well when a winning favourite on soft-to-heavy at the Curragh on reappearance.
runners.comment: Stayed this trip well when a winning favourite on soft-to-heavy at the Curragh
runners.owner: Mrs M V Magnier & Mrs Paul Shanahan & S E Sangster

runners.name: Camino Vino
runners.spotlight: Invincible Spirit filly created a good impression when beating an 86-rated previous winner in a 1m maiden at Dundalk in December on her only juvenile start; interesting, although not guaranteed to stay as far as this on pedigree.
runners.comment: Won 1m maiden at Dundalk, not guaranteed to stay as far as this on pedigree

runners.name: Ice Dancer
runners.spotlight: Had a troubled run on debut; put that behind her when landing the odds in a 1m maiden at Gowran last September; ground was testing then, and same when she was third in a 1m Group 3 race at the Curragh in October; pedigree implies she can improve at this trip; Ryan Moore prefers her to Minerva who is rated 10lb superior (official and RPR figures).
runners.comment: Pedigree implies 1m winner can improve at this trip, Ryan Moore prefers her to Minerva
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Bloom
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly out of a Listed winner by Galileo; stepped up from unplaced debut at Leopardstown to win a 1m maiden at Cork last September; stable's two other runners are more appealing.
runners.comment: Stepped up from unplaced debut at Leopardstown to win a 1m maiden at Cork last September.
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden (Smullen Series)

runners.name: Latin America
runners.spotlight: Gleneagles colt cost 280,000gns as a yearling; unraced at two; chased home possible Irish Derby candidate Shaihaan at the Curragh four weeks ago on debut; form was boosted by his third-placed stablemate Asakir when beating a Joseph O'Brien-trained favourite at Leopardstown.
runners.comment: Chased home possible Irish Derby candidate Shaihaan at the Curragh, sets the standard

runners.name: Huff'n'puff
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when keeping on from off the pace in a 1m Curragh maiden last October; confirmed maiden-winning potential  with third placing over 1m here on seasonal debut, almost 4l in front of Battle Of Maldon.
runners.comment: Comes into the reckoning based on his seasonal debut, has an edge over Battle Of Maldon

runners.name: Genchev
runners.spotlight: Made a very encouraging debut when third behind the Aidan O'Brien-trained Daytona in 1m Listed event at Naas last July; but below that level in a Leopardstown maiden later that month on softer ground; faded late into fourth in a 1m1f maiden at Tipperary in August; has been gelded.
runners.comment: Third in a Listed race on debut, failed to build on that in two subsequent outings
runners.owner: David Spratt & Sean Jones & Mrs Lynne Lyons

runners.name: Battle Of Maldon
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Naas barrier trial last May; faded in the final furlong of a 7f maiden at Leopardstown the following month; reportedly scoped badly; made an encouraging seasonal debut over 1m at this venue but has a bit to find Huff'N'Puff.
runners.comment: Made an encouraging seasonal debut over 1m at this venue but has a bit to find Huff'N'Puff

runners.name: Glen Echo
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to the talented winner Snellen (Chesham Stakes and Group 3 Meld Stakes for this stable); dam 1m2f turf/1m4f AW winner, half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 3 winner Night Lagoon (dam of King George winner Novellist).
runners.comment: Noteworthy as a half-brother to the trainer's Chesham Stakes/Meld Stakes winner Snellen

runners.name: Croi Gra
runners.spotlight: Alkumait half-sister to four winners including Holiday Magic (ten wins, all on AW), Coolnagree (two 7f wins, Group 3-placed); respectable debut when third at a big price in a 1m Gowran maiden dominated by market leaders; can improve.
runners.comment: Respectable debut at a big price in a Gowran race dominated by market leaders, can improve
runners.owner: Gary Devlin & Kevin Osborne & Richard J Fitzsimons

runners.name: Tintinabulla
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister by U S Navy Flag to two minor winners; dam, by Fantastic Light, was a heavily raced AW Listed winner; hooded for debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister to two minor winners, dam a heavily raced AW Listed winner, hooded for debut

runners.name: Now Maybe
runners.spotlight: Struggled on heavy ground first time out at the Listowel festival; looks a possible future winner on his Dundalk second in November.
runners.comment: Struggled on heavy first time out, looks a possible future winner on his Dundalk second

runners.name: Dreamkeeper
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to 6f AW 2yo winner Lady Amanda; dam won twice for Mark Johnston.
runners.comment: Fourth foal, half-sister to 6f AW 2yo winner Lady Amanda, dam won twice for Mark Johnston
runners.owner: Mrs H Shanahan & Mrs Jane Shanahan

runners.name: Amethyst Stone
runners.spotlight: Newcomer is a half-brother to winners in France, Colin Keane rides his 83-rated stablemate.
runners.comment: Newcomer is a half-brother to winners in France, Colin Keane rides his 83-rated stablemate

runners.name: Good Man Jack
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; half-brother by Inns Of Court to winners, including multiple scorers Booyea and I Am Not Here; dam Listed-placed 7f winner.
runners.comment: Inns Of Court half-brother to winners, including multiple scorers Booyea and I Am Not Here

runners.name: Kingcharles
runners.spotlight: E38,000 yearling; eighth foal; half-brother to four winners; dam French maiden, out of half-sister to French 2m4f Group 1 winner Karkour.
runners.comment: Half-brother by Golden Horde to four winners, dam maiden, likely to need experience

runners.name: Youcansaythatagain
runners.spotlight: 11th foal; half-brother by Ghaiyyath to six winners, notably Dermot Weld-trained Group 2/3 scorer Brooch, and Caponata (Listed) dam, French 5f Listed winner, sister to 1m Group 1 winner Emulous.
runners.comment: Half-brother to six winners, notably Dermot Weld-trained Group 2 scorer Brooch, noteworthy

runners.name: Cascade Canyon
runners.spotlight: Showed up well for a long way on debut over C&D; others have much stronger claims.
runners.comment: Showed up well for a long way on debut over C&D, others have much stronger claims

runners.name: Teologia
runners.spotlight: Dam has produced seven winners, including Rocques (Group 3) and Mea Domina (Listed); showed some ability at Galway last October, but not enough to make her a leading fancy.
runners.comment: Showed some ability at Galway last October but not enough to make her a leading fancy

runners.name: Mergus Serrator
runners.spotlight: Respectable run at Leopardstown in October on only outing at two; will need to have improved considerably to win this.
runners.comment: Fair run on only outing at two but will need to have improved considerably to win this

runners.name: Fay Og
runners.spotlight: Huge price when last of 15 at this venue on debut; can be discounted.
runners.comment: Huge price when last of 15 at this venue on debut, can be discounted

runners.name: Santa Bravado
runners.spotlight: Two satisfactory runs last season; likely to be more interesting as a handicap prospect after this.
runners.comment: Two satisfactory runs last season, more interesting as a handicap prospect after this

runners.name: African Affair
runners.spotlight: Finished with only one behind him in a 16-runner 7f Naas maiden last autumn; 100-1 shot then; has been gelded.
runners.comment: Finished with only one behind him in a 16-runner 7f Naas maiden last autumn, 100-1 then

runners.name: Searcog
runners.spotlight: First reserve; won a Punchestown handicap last year over 1m1f; some decent hurdle runs over the winter and a solid Leopardstown return over 1m; considered.
runners.comment: Won a Punchestown h'cap last year; solid Leop'town return; considered; reserve

runners.name: Obscenity
runners.spotlight: Has posted some good runs in defeat since tackling handicaps including on return at Bellewstown over nearly 1m4f; needs more and is unproven in this ground but a player if adapting; visor replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Some good runs in defeat including on return at Bellewstown; needs more; visor on

runners.name: Pepper Noir
runners.spotlight: Three AW wins up to 1m4f and some decent efforts in defeat over the winter season; best turf effort when second in a Leopardstown handicap over this trip last year; a player if repeating that.
runners.comment: Best turf effort when second in a Leopardstown handicap over this trip last year; a player

runners.name: Frank Pickle
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; maiden has posted some fine runs in defeat including on return at Curragh (1m); stays 1m4f so can do better over this trip; one to consider.
runners.comment: Some fine runs in defeat incl' on return at Curragh (1m); one to consider; reserve

runners.name: Master Sydney
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner went closest on the Flat in his four starts over 2m at Tramore last year; this trip looks on the short side on return.
runners.comment: Bumper winner went close on the Flat over 2m at Tramore last year; this trip looks short

runners.name: Spanish Rising
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; not bad maiden runs in UK; poor on heavy ground debut for this yard behind a stablemate; can do better now handicapping.
runners.comment: Not bad maiden runs in UK; poor on heavy ground debut for this yard; do better; reserve

runners.name: Pebble Island
runners.spotlight: Maiden was beaten a long way over 7f last time at Leopardstown and while he doesn't hold that much appeal can do better over this trip.
runners.comment: Maiden was beaten a long way over 7f last time but can do better over this trip

runners.name: Pedro The Poet
runners.spotlight: Tailed-off in two bumpers but better runs in two AW maidens (1m-1m4f) either side of a poor run on heavy ground; hood added for handicap debut and can run well.
runners.comment: Solid runs in AW maidens from 1m-1m4f; hood added for handicap debut and can run well

runners.name: Some Good Men Gone
runners.spotlight: Best run when second in a Naas handicap over this trip last year; has been poor on softer ground the last twice but can do better today if returning to best form.
runners.comment: Best run when 2nd in a Naas handicap over this trip last year; has to return to that form

runners.name: Gotta Catch'em All
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 22 runs but has been placed several times up to 1m4f; not a bad effort at Leopardstown over 1m on return and should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Maiden after 22 runs but placed several times; should be thereabouts
runners.owner: Mrs S Bury & Ralph Kelly & Odhran O'Neill & Thomas Cox

runners.name: Rain
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m4f handicap at Galway last year on soft ground but has form on good too; was well beaten on her Curragh return so needs much more.
runners.comment: Won a 1m4f h'cap at Galway last year; well beaten on Curragh return so needs much more

runners.name: Maeve Brennan
runners.spotlight: Decent efforts last year in three maidens up to 1m4f and not a bad run over that trip in a Curragh handicap on return; can improve from that and one to consider.
runners.comment: Not a bad run over 1m4f in a Curragh handicap on return; can improve from that; considered
runners.owner: Eleanora Kennedy & Mrs J S Bolger

runners.name: Lohengrin
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Laytown maiden last year but stays this trip too; poor at Dundalk last winter and brought down halfway at Bellewstown on return; looks vulnerable off this mark.
runners.comment: Won a 7f Laytown maiden last year but stays this trip; looks vulnerable off this mark

runners.name: Captain Ciano
runners.spotlight: Maiden has been poor in two runs on softer ground recently and is not easy to fancy on that form.
runners.comment: Poor in two runs on softer ground recently and not easy to fancy on that form

runners.name: Raydamann
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner is a maiden on the Flat but has posted some fine runs; however, has been poor in both codes last two starts so needs a return to form.
runners.comment: Hurdle winner is a maiden on the Flat but some fine runs; needs a return to form

runners.name: Forlio
runners.spotlight: Won well over C&D last October but wasn't as good off higher marks after and needs more on return down 2lb; eyecatching jockey booking, though.
runners.comment: Won well over C&D last October but not as good off higher marks after; needs more

runners.name: Faro Island
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m3f Down Royal handicap last autumn but was poor on AW after (found to have mucus in his lungs); needs best on return but can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Won a 1m3f Down Royal h'cap last autumn but was poor on AW after (had excuse); needs best

runners.name: Wolf Prince
runners.spotlight: Smart hurdler has run well on the Flat in defeat but is returning off a long absence and can only be watched.
runners.comment: Smart hurdler has run well on the Flat in defeat but is returning off a long absence

runners.name: Casheldale Lass
runners.spotlight: Won Gowran and Naas handicaps last year over similar trips to this; not as good last two runs last term and looks vulnerable off this mark.
runners.comment: Won Gowran and Naas h'caps last year over similar trips to this; vulnerable off this mark

runners.name: Tofino
runners.spotlight: Won a Curragh handicap over 1m last year and some decent runs since between 7f and that trip; up in trip on return and has to prove she stays.
runners.comment: Won Curragh h'cap over 1m last year and some decent runs since; new trip on return

runners.name: El Regalo
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden was pulled-up over hurdles when last seen; seems best watched back in this sphere after a layoff; new yard.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose maiden seems best watched back in this sphere after a layoff

runners.name: Sylkie
runners.spotlight: Won three handicaps at Galway in 2024; no win last year but some solid efforts up to 1m4f; is fit from hurdling over the winter and can run well.
runners.comment: Won three h'caps at Galway in 2024 but no win last year; fit from hurdling; run well

runners.name: Sonic Invader
runners.spotlight: Not bad runs last two maiden starts of 2025 over 7f and 1m, the latter at this track; has to be ready after a long absence; the market can help.
runners.comment: Not bad runs last two maidens last year up to 1m; has to be ready after long absence

runners.name: Roman Bull
runners.spotlight: Hasn't had much racing in the last two years; is dropping in the weights but still best watched.
runners.comment: Not much racing in last two years; dropping in the weights but best watched

runners.name: Talk Show Host
runners.spotlight: Best run came when beaten only 2.25l in a 1m maiden at this track last September; was below that level on testing ground next time; this trip is a query on return.
runners.comment: Best run came when in a 1m maiden at this track last September; trip is a query on return

races.race_name: Cusack Hotel Group Family Day July 11th Handicap

runners.name: Kakori
runners.spotlight: Signed off last season with a promising run behind this year's Irish Lincoln winner Ribee at Gowran (7f, heavy); picked up an injury on seasonal debut; also had an excuse at Bellewstown, hampered under half a mile out; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Picked up an injury on seasonal debut, also had an excuse at Bellewstown, not ruled out

runners.name: Trubshaw
runners.spotlight: Two wins last season, both gained at the main expense of members of this field; edged out God The Highness at Sligo; ended the campaign with a 1m6f Curragh victory over Quiz Test; 9lb higher now, same apprentice claiming 7lb.
runners.comment: Ended last term with a 1m6f Curragh victory over Quiz Test, 9lb higher, same apprentice

runners.name: God The Highness
runners.spotlight: Chance here with fitness advanced by a hurdles run at Fairyhouse; went close against Trubshaw in a small-field Sligo event last August; probably a better effort third of 23 over 1m6f here in October.
runners.comment: Chance here with fitness advanced by a hurdles run, third of 23 over 1m6f here last autumn

runners.name: Arcland
runners.spotlight: First reserve; went very close in a similar race here on his penultimate Flat start last year; went on to win over hurdles before a below-par Bellewstown run; durable sort.
runners.comment: First reserve, went very close in a similar race here on penultimate Flat start last year

runners.name: Radar Ahead
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner on good ground; 0-10 on Flat; ran poorly on seasonal debut at Bellewstown; stable appears to have a better chance with Celtic Druid.
runners.comment: Ran poorly on seasonal debut, stable appears to have a better chance with Celtic Druid

runners.name: Celtic Druid
runners.spotlight: Progressive in winning back-to-back 1m4f handicaps at Dundalk; appeared to have plenty left in the tank last time; those two runs, if not his pedigree, suggest the extra two furlongs will be within his range; holds a leading chance here with Sadhbh Tormey's claim in play again.
runners.comment: Progressive in winning twice at Dundalk, definite chance here with Sadhbh Tormey's claim

runners.name: Earthsong
runners.spotlight: Best effort came on handicap debut over1m6f at this venue on yielding ground last August, staying on from the back for a 2l third of 11; should be better for her seasonal debut here last month; has a slight edge with Kool One.
runners.comment: Should be better for her seasonal debut here last month, closely matched with Kool One

runners.name: Plunkett Street
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m4f Cork handicap on return last year and some good runs off higher marks after; won over hurdles in November but disqualified after a positive sample; failed to run to market expectations at Gowran on seasonal debut, capable of better.
runners.comment: Failed to run to market expectations at Gowran on seasonal debut, capable of better

runners.name: Edge Of Darkness
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; a four-time turf winner whose recent Dundalk form has been poor.
runners.comment: Second reserve, four-time turf winner whose recent Dundalk form has been poor

runners.name: Juniper Sling
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to the talented Thunder Kiss; third in two of her four starts in maidens; ran poorly over 1m2f on heavy ground at Leopardstown on her only handicap start; premature to write off.
runners.comment: Ran poorly over 1m2f on heavy ground on her only handicap start, premature to write off

runners.name: Kool One
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m4f at Roscommon (good) last August and added a Wexford maiden hurdle (good) ten days later; should be better for last month's run when fifth over 1m6f here, 1 3/4l behind the reopposing Earthsong.
runners.comment: Winning form on Flat and over hurdles last August, should be better for last month's run

runners.name: Rich Belief
runners.spotlight: Second at Clonmel last September has been the only bright spot since a 2024 Chester win; some merit in his recent Clonmel run.
runners.comment: Second at Clonmel last September has been the only bright spot since a 2024 Chester win

runners.name: Quiz Test
runners.spotlight: Sole win came at Cork in 2023; decent form in defeat last autumn, including a close third over 1m4f at Galway and second to Trubshaw in a 19-runner contest at the Curragh, with Plunkett Street, Earthsong and Bruant behind; each-way chance if reappearing in similar shape.
runners.comment: Decent form without winning last autumn, each-way chance if reappearing in same shape

runners.name: God Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Out of form since a Limerick win last June; towards the rear on both turf starts this term.
runners.comment: Out of form since a Limerick win last June, towards the rear on both turf starts this term

runners.name: Goin'
runners.spotlight: Twice successful in Germany, suited by 1m4f; struggled over hurdles during the winter; not without a chance on last year's best Flat form, namely a third placing over this trip at Down Royal.
runners.comment: Struggled over hurdles during the winter, not without a chance on last year's Flat form

runners.name: Daboya
runners.spotlight: Versatile mare has won on turf/AW and over hurdles; ground should be coming right for her; lacks the advantage of a recent outing.
runners.comment: Versatile mare has won on turf/AW and over hurdles, ground should be coming right for her

runners.name: Bruant
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose winner has been well beaten last six Flat starts; continues to drop in the handicap; recent Gowran run provides limited encouragement.
runners.comment: Continues to drop in the handicap, recent Gowran run provides limited encouragement

runners.name: Granville Street
runners.spotlight: Only Flat win came in 2023; fit from hurdling but hard to fancy on recent evidence.
runners.comment: Only Flat win came in 2023, fit from hurdling but hard to fancy on recent evidence

runners.name: Miss Gitana
runners.spotlight: Three Flat wins, twice in Britain and over 2m at Tramore last April for Gordon Elliott; disappointing over hurdles on last two starts in 2025; makes stable debut.
runners.comment: Disappointed over hurdles for Gordon Elliott after a 2m Flat win at Tramore,  stable debut

runners.name: High Honour
runners.spotlight: Had placed form over hurdles in 2025, only Flat win was for Godolphin at two; not an appealing prospect under a big weight.
runners.comment: Had placed form over hurdles in 2025, only Flat win was for Godolphin at two

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Signora
runners.spotlight: Took third in a Naas Group 3 on debut last term (6f, good); beaten favourite in Albany Stakes next time and no help to favourite backers in two maiden runs this term either; collared late on at the Curragh (7f, heavy) when second; filled the same spot despite 1-2 odds at Cork last time (7f, soft to heavy); drying ground could help her and may finally make the breakthrough.
runners.comment: Group placed on debut but beaten fav' in three runs after; may finally make breakthrough
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Expert Dancer
runners.spotlight: Eyecatcher when making late gains on debut at Naas over 6f in October; another solid run when a 3.5l third on heavy at the Curragh (6f, heavy) eight days later; 2.25l behind Signora when third at Cork on seasonal return; place claims.
runners.comment: 2.25l to find with Signora on the basis of their meeting at Cork last time; place claims

runners.name: Ridersinthesky
runners.spotlight: Third at the Curragh on debut (7f, good to yielding) and again at Cork (7f, good); again made the frame in a Curragh Group 3 in August (1m, good); below her best on final start at Cork when sixth; player.
runners.comment: Third in opening three runs inc' in a Gr. 3; sixth on final run last season; player

runners.name: Rayzera
runners.spotlight: Looked in need of further when 9l behind on Gowran debut (7f, good to yielding) last September; appreciated extra trip when 5l fifth of 16 over 1m at Cork (yielding); well beaten behind impressive winner back at Gowran on seasonal return (1m, soft); could be in the mix with improvement but drop to this trip may not be the answer.
runners.comment: Well beaten behind impressive winner on seasonal return; could feature with improvement
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Lamberella
runners.spotlight: Last of 16 on debut at Cork but showed good improvement to finish ninth of 15 in a strong maiden at Leopardstown (1m, good to yeilding) 13 days ago; vast improvement required all the same.
runners.comment: Took ninth on second start at Leopardstown when 125-1; improvement required

runners.name: Billy Jean
runners.spotlight: Improved over poor 6f Dundalk debut when a 8.25l sixth for first turf run at the Curragh (7f, soft to heavy); others preferred.
runners.comment: Improved for switch to turf last time when sixth of 13 at the Curragh; others preferred

runners.name: Divine Legend
runners.spotlight: Exhibited just modest improvement over her Leopardstown debut when a 12l seventh at Cork on seasonal return (7f, soft to heavy); much more needed.
runners.comment: Just modest improvement over debut run when seventh of 11 at Cork; much more needed
runners.owner: Exors of the Late J Connaughton

runners.name: White Lily
runners.spotlight: Vastly improved over her moderate debut when outrunning 125-1 odds to take third at Naas (6f, yielding); others preferred.
runners.comment: Vast improvement over debut when third at Naas at 125-1 but others preferred

runners.name: Green Interlude
runners.spotlight: Finished just 2.75l behind Ridersinthesky when fifth at the Curragh on debut last season (7f, good to yielding); didn't kick on from there in two subsequent starts, including when 13.5l behind at Dundalk when last seen in February.
runners.comment: Just 2.75l behind Ridersinthesky on debut last season; didn't build on that subsequently

runners.name: Moon Aura
runners.spotlight: Weakened into a 12.5l eighth at Leopardstown on debut (1m, yielding to soft); others appeal more.
runners.comment: Weakened into a 12.5l eighth at Leopardstown; others appeal more

runners.name: Ballerina Dancer
runners.spotlight: Finished last of 14 when sent off at 250-1 for Leopardstown debut (1m, yielding to soft); very unlikely.
runners.comment: 250-1 and finished last of 14 at Leopardstown on debut

runners.name: Curious Jane
runners.spotlight: Beaten 16l over 7f at Dundalk and 15l over 10.5f there last time; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Beaten similar distances in two Dundalk runs; hard to fancy

runners.name: Carmel Valley
runners.spotlight: Never better than midfield on debut at the Curragh (7f, soft to heavy) when 33-1; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Never better than midfield on Curragh debut at 33-1; others appeal more

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden

runners.name: Zenford
runners.spotlight: Close third on debut at Leopardstown (7f, yld-sft) in a hood last October; beaten 5.5l at the Curragh on seasonal return last month (7f, sft-hvy) minus the hood; the hood returns now and he looks the main threat to The Publican's Son.
runners.comment: Third in both starts, hood worn for promising debut returns, looks the main danger

runners.name: God Of Power
runners.spotlight: Sound effort when a 1l fourth of 13 at Dundalk on debut last November but failed to back it up over the same C&D when last seen in December and gelded since; yard in form so worth a market check.
runners.comment: Promise on Dundalk debut but didn't back it up, gelded since, check the market

runners.name: Casamorati
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; 11-2 but never landed a blow on debut at Gowran (9.5f, soft); the drop back in trip here should suit but yard has stronger chance with The Publican's Son.
runners.comment: 11-2 but no show on debut at Gowran, yard has better chance with The Publican's Son

runners.name: Dark Leader
runners.spotlight: Dark Angel gelding; 65,000gns 2yo; closely related to Italian 7f/7.5f 2yo winner Bridge Dress Me, half-brother to winners The Wizard Of Eye (6f-1m inc 2yo/AW Listed; RPR 112) and Arran (5f 2yo; 87); dam maiden (56); a bit of appeal on paper and worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: Dark Angel gelding, half-brother to Listed winner, worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Little Roy
runners.spotlight: Galileo Gold gelding; unraced dam closely related to US 1m Grade 2 winner Ryehill Dreamer, out of 1m winning half-sister to French 2,000 Guineas winner No Pass No Sale; yard not known for Flat maiden winners; watch.
runners.comment: Galileo Gold gelding, dam related to US Grade 2 winner, best watched on debut

runners.name: Oro Blanco
runners.spotlight: Havana Grey gelding; E36,000 2yo; dam placed 7f 2yo (RPR 65), half-sister to French 1m7f Listed winner Only A Pleasure, out of US 1m 2yo Grade 1 winning half-sister to French 1,000 Guineas winner Valentine Waltz; market best guide on debut.
runners.comment: Havana Grey gelding, dam placed at 7f, market best guide on debut

runners.name: Sancerre Rock
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica colt; half-brother to winners Queen's Course (7f inc AW; RPR 89) and Aussie View (7f 2yo; 82); dam 5f-1m winner (inc 6f 2yo Group 3; 106), sister to prolific 5f-7f winner Vhujon; nicely bred and yard in form; market check advised.
runners.comment: St Mark's Basilica colt, dam Group 3 winner, yard in form, check the market

runners.name: Absoluteassignment
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival home on debut at Gowran when an 80-1 shot; needs more time.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival when 80-1 for Gowran debut, may need more time

runners.name: Shivoo
runners.spotlight: 22-1 and never better than midfield on debut over 1m2f at Leopardstown 10 days ago; best watched dropped back in trip.
runners.comment: 22-1 and never better than midfield on debut, drops in trip, best watched

runners.name: Big Cypress
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; sent off at 12-1 on debut at the Curragh (7f, sft-hvy) last October and was beaten 14.5l; bred to do better and could improve for a sounder surface so can't discount.
runners.comment: Well beaten on testing ground on debut, bred to do better, could improve, can't discount

runners.name: Garryowen Green
runners.spotlight: 100-1 and no impact on debut at Dundalk in December; seems safe to put a line through.
runners.comment: 100-1 and never figured on debut at Dundalk, seems safe to put a line through

runners.name: Spacebound
runners.spotlight: 6l runner up on debut at Dundalk (7f) in January behind a now 100 rated rival; ran on well for a 0.5l second behind a Ballydoyle colt on seasonal return over 7f at the Curragh (RPR 91, 5l clear of third); could go one place better upped in trip if acts on the ground.
runners.comment: Runner up behind smart sort on debut, ran on well over 7f latest, big chance up in trip

runners.name: Senna's Girl
runners.spotlight: 0-13 and rated 77; ground versatile and smart juvenile form included a 4l third on debut at the Curragh behind Lake Victoria and Red Letter; good run from wide draw in 7f Leopardstown handicap latest, staying on well; cheekpieces added to tongue tie; big player.
runners.comment: 0-13, rated 77, good handicap run at Leopardstown latest, cheekpieces on, big player

runners.name: Treasure Rose
runners.spotlight: Rated 76; knocking on the door in maidens last season at 7f-1m2f on good and yielding ground; obvious chance in this company if ready to rock for her seasonal return.
runners.comment: Knocking on the door in maidens at 7f-1m2f last season, obvious chance if fit on return

runners.name: Coul Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; dual-purpose; hurdles winner is 0-7 on turf on the Flat but has run well in defeat at times; well below best on seasonal return at the Curragh last month though and needs to leave that well behind.
runners.comment: Hurdles winner, 0-7 on turf on the Flat, needs to leave last run well behind; reserve
runners.owner: Mrs Suzanne McAuley & Ms C A Jennings

runners.name: Tyraxes
runners.spotlight: Rated 71 after seven starts on the AW; placed in three of five runs for this stable including a 3l third over this trip at Dundalk on latest; place chance if acts on turf but more needed to win.
runners.comment: Raced solely on AW, 3l third over 1m at Dundalk latest, place chance if acts on turf

runners.name: Je Suis Celebre
runners.spotlight: Rated 73; placed in one of two starts on AW for Ian Williams; hung left when beaten 5.25l on debut for this yard at Dundalk in February; cheekpieces now go on for turf debut; needs more.
runners.comment: Rated 73, hung left on debut for this yard on AW in Feb', cheekpieces on, more needed

runners.name: Enchanted Lady
runners.spotlight: New Bay filly was a E38,000 yearling; half-sister to 1m4f winner Cycladic (RPR 99); dam 1m2f/1m4f winner (101), half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Unnefer and Australian 1m2f Group 3 winner Mount Popa; may need further than this in time.
runners.comment: E38,000 New Bay filly, dam 1m2f/1m4f winner, may need further than this in time

runners.name: Ballysax Stew
runners.spotlight: Profitable gelding was pulled up in a Punchestown maiden hurdle in November 2024 when 200-1 and not seen since; best watched on Flat debut.
runners.comment: Pulled up over hurdles when last seen in Nov' 2024, best watched

runners.name: Sron Na Caise
runners.spotlight: Pleasing debut when a never nearer fourth of 17 on debut at Gowran (9.5, soft); not sure this step back in trip will suit but should be doing his best work at the finish; place shout.
runners.comment: Kept on well over 9.5f on Gowran debut, drop in trip not sure to suit but place shout

runners.name: Western Bandit
runners.spotlight: First reserve; tailed off when 28-1 on debut at Gowran 16 days ago; may need more time.
runners.comment: Tailed off on debut at Gowran 16 days ago, may need more time; reserve

runners.name: El Rehaiby
runners.spotlight: Shadwell cast off showed definite promise in two AW starts at 1m-1m2f for Owen Burrows last autumn; bought for 60,000gns and dam from a talented family (half-sister to Baaeed and Hukum); worth a market check on turf debut.
runners.comment: Well-bred, promise both starts for Owen Burrows, bought for 60,000gns, check market

runners.name: Poet's Willow
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in 1m4f and 1m maidens at Dundalk in first two starts; would be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in 1m4f/1m maidens at Dundalk, would be a surprise winner

runners.name: Queen Lioness
runners.spotlight: Sole start in June 2023 for Johnny Murtagh wasn't devoid of promise but off the track since; best watched on first run for new yard.
runners.comment: Off since sole run in June 2023, new yard now, best watched

runners.name: Phidias Ovation
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in maidens at Naas (1m, yielding) and Dundalk (7f) last year; may do better when sent handicapping.
runners.comment: No impact in two maidens last year, may do better when sent handicapping

runners.name: Torcalot
runners.spotlight: Modest effort when midfield on debut at Naas in March 2025 (1m, sft-hvy); not seen since and likely best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Modest effort on debut at Naas in March 2025, off since, watch unless market speaks

runners.name: Lauderdale King
runners.spotlight: Showed little at big prices in two turf maidens for John Burke last year; lacks a run and is likely best watched on stable debut.
runners.comment: No show in two turf maidens last year, new yard now, lacks a run, best watched

runners.name: Friars Gate
runners.spotlight: Massive odds and ran accordingly in two 1m maidens last year; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Huge odds and ran accordingly in two 1m maidens last year, seems safe to rule out

runners.name: Moyassr
runners.spotlight: Flying on the AW over the winter, winning over 1m in October and twice in January; winless from ten on turf, although ran okay at times; rated 9lb lower on this surface; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Three wins on AW over winter; 0-10 on this surface and 9lb lower on it; not ruled out

runners.name: Deuteronomy
runners.spotlight: In the form of his life last June, notching three wins on the trot at 1m/1m1f following addition of visor/blinkers; final leg of the trio came over this C&D off 54 (good); well beaten in two starts this season but interestingly the blinkers go back on now.
runners.comment: Won three on the bounce last term after addition of headgear and blinkers go back on now

runners.name: Mags
runners.spotlight: Off the mark when winning a 1m handicap at Dundalk in November 2024; ran just twice last season, finishing last on both occasions; makes stable debut now in a first-time hood.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Dundalk in Nov' 2024; finished last in both starts last season

runners.name: Burford Beauty
runners.spotlight: Just fair form in handicaps to date but her mark is beginning to drop and she is lightly raced; finished a 5.5l fifth of 13 at Down Royal when last seen on turf (7f, yielding); got upset in stalls and weakened quickly into tenth at Dundalk in November (10.5f).
runners.comment: Just fair form in h'caps but mark is dropping; fifth of 13 when last seen on turf

runners.name: La Tulipe Noire
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner was successful on turf twice last autumn at Cork (1m, yielding) and Roscommon (7.5f, soft); ran some solid runs in defeat afterwards; capable of more than what she has shown in two runs this season.
runners.comment: Successful twice last autumn; capable of more than she has shown in two outings this term

runners.name: Glamazon
runners.spotlight: Consistent form in defeat in seven turf runs as a juvenile over 6f-7.5f; way below her best on AW debut at Dundalk in October; demonstrated a good attitude to take a 7.5l fifth on seasonal return at Bellewstown (1m, soft); dropped 1lb and could improve.
runners.comment: Consistent in defeat on turf last season; good attitude to finish fifth on seasonal return

runners.name: Medieval Night
runners.spotlight: Moderate form overall in six runs for Dermot Weld apart from a standout runner-up finish on handicap debut in a 21-runner contest at Leopardstown in a first-time visor (1m1f, good); that run came after an absence so perhaps this is the time to catch him on stable/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Took second spot on h'cap debut after a break last term; maybe this is time to catch him

runners.name: Bella Colombia
runners.spotlight: Added a second AW win at Dundalk last month (1m) off 3lb lower; ran some decent races in defeat on turf in 2024; hurdling wasn't her bag; could go well.
runners.comment: Added second AW win last month off 3lb lower at Dundalk; could go well

runners.name: Electric Night
runners.spotlight: Some decent efforts in defeat in Dundalk maidens over 7f-1m; respectable handicap debut (7f) in February when fourth of 13; makes turf debut now and this trip should suit.
runners.comment: Some decent efforts in defeat at Dundalk; makes turf debut and trip should suit

runners.name: Nicky Larson
runners.spotlight: Moderate maiden form; may do better now entering handicaps.
runners.comment: Moderate maiden form; may do better now entering handicaps

runners.name: Teffian Warrior
runners.spotlight: Best effort from five maidens came at Galway in July when a 6l fifth of 14 (7f, yielding); led before fading into fourth of 8 on handicap debut at Leopardstown ten days ago (1m1f, yielding to soft); likely capable of better; cheekpieces applied now.
runners.comment: Led before fading into fourth of 8 on h'cap debut ten days ago; likely capable of more

runners.name: Cannonball Queen
runners.spotlight: Promise on Curragh debut when beaten 5.5l (7f, good) but hasn't been able to build on that initial effort; finished last of 12 on return at Leopardstown 10 days ago (7f, yielding to soft); others more appealing.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on minor promise shown on debut; last of 12 on return earlier this month

runners.name: Linger For Longer
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner was below par in six runs last year; now 17lb below last winning mark but must return to form.
runners.comment: Three-time winner was below par last year; attractive mark if returning to form

runners.name: Lady Arwen
runners.spotlight: Had been on a losing run of over 2.5 years before a short head victory at Gowran last time provided her fourth career turf win (1m, soft); runner-up hasn't done much for the form in the meantime; still on an attractive mark compared to her previous wins if continuing on an upward curve.
runners.comment: Gowran winner last time but form doesn't look great; mark still okay if improving

runners.name: Loingseoir
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner wasn't disgraced on seasonal return at the Curragh six days ago (7f, soft) when 3.5l behind the winner; could improve for that first run back; not discounted.
runners.comment: Seven-time winner wasn't disgraced on seasonal return; could improve
runners.owner: W Keeling & M C Grassick & Mrs M J Grassick

runners.name: Bay Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Won twice for James Ferguson last year, including once on turf at Nottingham (8.5f, good) off 9lb lower and filled the runner-up spot the next twice; picked up for 10,000gns in October but was tailed off on stable debut at Bellewstown when badly hampered; all form has come on good or better.
runners.comment: Won twice in Britain; badly hampered when tailed off on stable debut; form on good ground

runners.name: Lady Odette
runners.spotlight: Best run for Ollie Sangster was when runner-up in a 1m3f Southwell novice contest in March last year; poor on final British start and got off to a bad start on stable debut at Gowran earlier this month when well beaten; visor goes on.
runners.comment: Decent form for Ollie Sangster but poor on final British start and similar on Irish debut

runners.name: Smithfield
runners.spotlight: Just denied in a Galway maiden on debut last August over extended 1m trip (good to yielding); hasn't reproduced that form since, including in two handicap starts on heavy ground; could go well on seasonal return with the ground drying.
runners.comment: Couldn't repeat form of m'den debut when just denied; could go well on seasonal return

runners.name: Faoladh
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a good spell on turf last summer, winning at Leopardstown in June off 66 (1m, good); moderate Dundalk run in March but creditable return to turf at Leopardstown when fourth of 17 (1m, good to yielding); in the mix.
runners.comment: Won off 66 last summer; creditable return to turf last time when fourth; in the mix

runners.name: Minaun View
runners.spotlight: Maiden has run well in defeat several times for former yards up to 1m; moderate stable debut at Gowran (1m, soft) when 12l behind the winner; others preferred.
runners.comment: M'den has run okay for former yards; moderate stable debut at Gowran last time

runners.name: Green Kite
runners.spotlight: Best maiden run came at Killarney on penultimate run last season when a 5.5l runner-up (1m, soft to heavy); way below that at Gowran next time (7f, heavy); claims if repeating his Killarney effort off this mark.
runners.comment: Claims if repeating his penultimate Killarney m'den run when runner-up

runners.name: Red Hugh O'Donnell
runners.spotlight: Killarney maiden winner (1m, yielding to soft) in July last year; didn't look like gaining a handicap win in two subsequent runs; may need some help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: M'den winner didn't look like gaining a h'cap win in two runs after; mark on high side

runners.name: Green Icon
runners.spotlight: Won two on the bounce for Owen Burrows in Britain in 2024; two Curragh runs this term don't suggest he's on the verge of adding to his tally.
runners.comment: Back-to-back wins in 2024; two recent Curragh runs don't suggest he will add to his tally

runners.name: Sayidah New Bay
runners.spotlight: Filled the third spot in final two starts last year at Killarney (1m3f, soft) and Musselburgh (1m5f, soft); prominent before weakening into a 19.5l tenth of 13 on seasonal return; could improve.
runners.comment: Filled third spot in final two starts last year; well beaten on seasonal return

runners.name: Selective Power
runners.spotlight: Three-time turf winner who appreciates an ease in the ground; 3lb above his last success in September at Roscommon (7.5f, soft); positive return to turf at Bellewstown (1m, soft) two weeks ago when fourth of 14 following a poor effort at Dundalk prior; no surprise to see him feature.
runners.comment: Three-time turf winner made a positive return to turf last time; could feature

runners.name: Amemri
runners.spotlight: Back to form when recording a tenth win under rules' at Dundalk last month (7f); 6f perhaps a bit sharp back at Dundalk last time and was hampered turning in; she was a C&D winner last June off 5lb higher, so a few things in her favour back on turf.
runners.comment: Recorded tenth win under rules' on AW last month; C&D winner last year off 5lb higher

runners.name: Mythical Rock
runners.spotlight: 1-26 after signing off with a win for former yard at Dundalk in January (10.5f); some good placed efforts on turf last season; check market for stable debut.
runners.comment: Signed off with a win for former yard on AW last time; check market for stable debut

runners.name: Only One Scobie
runners.spotlight: Beat precious few rivals in opening three AW maidens nor on turf debut at Navan last month; sixth of ten at Gowran last time was an improvement (1m, soft); handicap debutant.
runners.comment: Beat previous few rivals in opening m'dens; sixth of ten at Gowran last time

runners.name: Monvoe
runners.spotlight: Finished runner-up at Dundalk on final two starts last year when upped to 7f; 2lb wrong here but not out of it if bringing his AW form.
runners.comment: Runner-up in final two AW starts last year upped to 7f; not out of it if bringing best

runners.name: Fits Perfect
runners.spotlight: Towards the rear and well beaten in opening three maidens; gelded since last seen at Galway in October; has shown nothing to date to recommend him but may improve now handicapping.
runners.comment: Has shown modest form in m'dens; may improve now handicapping

runners.name: Bear Right
runners.spotlight: Not a threat in three maidens; beaten 11l on handicap debut at Naas when last seen in September (1m, yielding to soft) but did receive a bump at a cruicial stage; down 3lb and could yet improve.
runners.comment: Received a bump when 11l behind on h'cap debut; down 3lb and could improve

runners.name: Collecting Coin
runners.spotlight: Consistent form last season included a Galway maiden win (7f, yielding); sound effort on handicap debut and seasonal return at Naas (7f, soft) last month; sharper for that and place shout at least stepped back up to 1m off 1lb lower.
runners.comment: Galway maiden winner, good effort on h'cap debut latest, 1lb lower now, chance

runners.name: Great Mover
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; off the mark at Gowran (9.5f, heavy) last September off 10lb lower; not a bad effort back there on latest but was beaten 9l and just 2lb lower now; others look better treated.
runners.comment: Won at Gowran last Oct, beaten 9l back there latest, others better treated; reserve

runners.name: Um Wadi
runners.spotlight: French AW winner; promise on debut for this yard at Leopardstown but blood in both nostrils when a beaten favourite for her next run at Navan; back on track after a break at Dundalk (7f) last month when a 1l third; chance off the same mark if backs that up.
runners.comment: French AW winner, back to form on AW latest, return to 1m a plus, chance off same mark
runners.owner: Mark Feighery & Kevin Blake & Paul McKeon

runners.name: Fast Tara
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in a Bellewstown maiden in April 2023; has run well in defeat at times and should strip fitter for her comeback run at Gowran 17-days ago; place chance if steps forward from that.
runners.comment: Sole win in April 2023 but has run well in defeat, strip fitter for latest, place chance

runners.name: Spanish Temptress
runners.spotlight: Went up a cumulative 21lb for three handicap wins last season; beaten 7.25l when third of 16 at Gowran on latest and dropped 1lb; has placed C&D form but may need a bit more help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Up 21lb for three wins last season, beaten 7.25l when third latest, just 1lb lower now

runners.name: Lady Lilac
runners.spotlight: 1-9 on turf; won a maiden at Killarney last October (1m, sft-hvy); fine effort on seasonal reappearance in a Curragh handicap (1m, sft-hvy) when beaten a head off 4lb lower; cheekpieces now go on; ground versatile and obvious chance.
runners.comment: Maiden winner, fine effort off 4lb lower at Curragh latest, cheekpieces on, obvious chance

runners.name: Highbury See See
runners.spotlight: Won two handicaps last season; below best on seasonal return in Irish Lincolnshire but much better on latest back at the Curragh (1m, sft-hvy) when a 4l fifth of 21; dropped 1lb for that but may need softer ground than this.
runners.comment: Two h'cap wins last season, solid fifth at the Curragh latest, may need softer ground

runners.name: Grey Leader
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner for Joe Murphy; tailed off on debut for this stable in the Irish Lincolnshire last month; acts on most ground but can only be watched after that latest effort.
runners.comment: Tailed off on debut for this yard in the Irish Lincoln, can only be watched after that

runners.name: Orandi
runners.spotlight: Last year's Irish Lincolnshire winner has lost way since a Galway premier handicap third last August; beaten 21l in both starts this season and remains 5lb above last winning mark; revival needed.
runners.comment: Last year's Irish Lincoln winner, beaten 21l in both starts this season, revival required

runners.name: Crypto Force
runners.spotlight: Classy 2yo won a three runner race Gowran last July, his third win on turf; has failed to fire since and was never sighted in the Irish Lincolnshire last time, although heavy ground may not have suited; sliding mark but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Classy 2yo, no impact since Gowran win last July, well beaten latest, others preferred

runners.name: Motu Proprio
runners.spotlight: French AW winner; 0-4 on turf but wasn't beaten far behind a 97 rated rival when fourth in a 1m listed heat at Longchamp (soft) last June (RPR 97); could be well treated and market can reveal expectations on debut for new yard.
runners.comment: French AW winner, potentially well treated on debut for new yard, check market

runners.name: Dark Summit
runners.spotlight: French winner on good to soft (1m); shaped promisingly enough on debut for this yard in the Irish Lincolnshire (1m, heavy); 1m2f may have stretched latest; dropped another 3lb, moves back in trip and drying ground in his favour; could go well.
runners.comment: Promise on stable debut in Irish Lincoln, trip too far latest, down 3lb, could go well

runners.name: Expound
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 3-22 on turf on the Flat; all three Flat wins have come over an extended 1m1f on good ground; has run well over C&D before and fit from the AW but remains 4lb above last win and beaten off lower AW mark on latest.
runners.comment: All Flat wins over an extended 1m1f, has run well over C&D but may be on tough mark

runners.name: Hellorhighwater
runners.spotlight: Placed over C&D (good) on debut for Gavin Cromwell; won a 1m Cork maiden (soft) on third and final start for that stable last September; opening mark looks fair; market best guide on stable/handicap debut after a break.
runners.comment: Cork maiden winner on final start for former yard, stable/h'cap debut now, check market

runners.name: San Aer
runners.spotlight: No win since October 2023; promise on first two starts for this yard last October but two runs this season have not been good (very slowly away latest); others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: On long losing run, below best both starts this season, others arrive in better form

runners.name: Follow Me
runners.spotlight: Has come up dry since back-to-back wins at the Curragh in 2024; no impact in three runs last season; hood now reached for; sliding mark and stable form the main positives but likely best watched on seasonal reappearance.
runners.comment: No win since 2024, out of form last year, hood goes on, stable form a positive

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Chablis Rock
runners.spotlight: Improved plenty over Cork debut last September (1m, yielding) when taking the third spot at Gowran; good return to action at the Curragh when a 2.75l second (1m2f, soft to heavy) and 18l clear of third; tongue tie goes on and she's a big player with the best form on offer.
runners.comment: Well clear of third when runner-up on seasonal return; big player with best form

runners.name: Benaki
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister to winners Charlus (Listed-placed 9.5f/1m2f inc 2yo/AW, RPR 96; 2m hurdle), Kosciuszko (1m-1m2f inc 2yo/Spain; 94) and Aquileo (Australian 1m-11.5f); dam French Group-placed 10.5f winner (102); trainer also runs newcomer Near Perfection.
runners.comment: Half-sister to three winners inc' Charlus (RPR 96); trainer also runs Near Perfection

runners.name: Near Perfection
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to five winners inc Anthony Van Dyck (7f-1m4f inc 2yo/Derby; RPR 121), Bounding (NZ/Australian 4.4f-7f inc 2yo/Group 1; 112) and Twinkle (7f 2yo; 88); dam Australian 5.5f 2yo Group 3 winner, sister to 5f Group 2 winner Kuroshio; market can split trainer's two newcomers.
runners.comment: Half-sister to five winners inc' Anthony Van Dyck; market can split trainers' two
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Pleased
runners.spotlight: 500,000gns Y; sixth foal; half-sister to 7f 2yo winner Fast Attack (inc AW/Group 3; RPR 104); dam 5f/6f winner (inc triple Listed; 109), closely related to 5f 2yo winner Xanthe, out of 5f 2yo Group 2 winner; Chablis Rock looks the pick of the stable's pair.
runners.comment: 500,000gns yearling; half-sister to Fast Attack (RPR 104); Chablis Rock also runs for yard

runners.name: Nathema
runners.spotlight: Moderate Gowran debut when eighth of 11 (8.5f, heavy); tailed off at the Curragh earlier this month (1m2f, soft to heavy); stable in good form but much more needed.
runners.comment: Modest form in two maiden runs; stable in good form but much more needed

runners.name: Zitkala Sa
runners.spotlight: Beaten 17l on debut at Dundalk earlier this month; tongue tie needs to spark a big improvement.
runners.comment: Beaten 17l on AW debut earlier this month; tongue tie needs to spark a big improvement

runners.name: Sofi's Gift
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in opening two maidens.
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential in opening two maidens

runners.name: Jurality
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden; not a bad run over hurdles at Bellewstown a week ago and had been shaping okay at this trip on the AW earlier this year; best Flat run came over C&D last July when a 0.5l second off 2lb lower; interesting.
runners.comment: Decent run over hurdles latest, best Flat run came over C&D, interesting

runners.name: Patrick Street
runners.spotlight: Last of two career wins came in October 2023; ran well at Dundalk in February but soundly beaten back on turf at Gowran on latest; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: On long losing run, below best again at Gowran on latest, others make more appeal

runners.name: Deed Pole
runners.spotlight: Two-time AW winner is 0-7 on turf; out of sorts over hurdles when last seen in October; best watched on return.
runners.comment: Two-time AW winner, 0-7 on turf, out of form over hurdles when last seen, watch

runners.name: Kilgharrahs Love
runners.spotlight: Promise as a juvenile in 2024 but lost way last year and no signs of a revival in two runs this term; new headgear combination tried.
runners.comment: Promise as a juvenile but has lost way, new headgear combo tried now

runners.name: Church Mountain
runners.spotlight: Dual-course winner at 1m is capable off this mark and he shaped promisingly at Navan (1m2f, heavy) last month (3l behind Calmint); should go well stepped back up in trip (has won over 1m4f on AW).
runners.comment: Dual 1m course winner, sound 1m2f effort at Navan latest, has won at 1m4f, go well

runners.name: Captain Hanley
runners.spotlight: 0-17 on the Flat (0-9 on turf); went close off 6lb higher at Tramore last August (1m4f, gd-yld); some fair efforts in defeat on AW this year; could go well with drying ground a plus.
runners.comment: 0-9 on turf, went close off 6lb higher at Tramore last Aug, drying ground a plus

runners.name: Almenfee
runners.spotlight: First reserve; German winner has failed to fire in four runs for this stable; beaten 17.5l on seasonal return at Bellewstown on latest; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: German winner, poor form so far for this stable, hard to fancy; reserve

runners.name: Amavasya
