runners.spotlight: Maiden shaped okay at times last season without really threatening to win; her best run came over this trip at Cork when a 2.75l fourth and she's 4lb lower now; lacks a run but can't dismiss.
runners.comment: Maiden, best effort off 4lb higher at Cork (1m4f) last year, lacks a run but can't dismiss

runners.name: Khormor Syd
runners.spotlight: Best effort yet when a 1.25l second of 14 at Gowran (9.5f, soft) on seasonal return; shaped like this longer trip would suit and is a player off just 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Best effort yet on latest at Gowran, step up in trip a plus, player off 1lb higher

runners.name: My Girl Robyn
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; showed little in maidens and similar story in first two handicaps; major improvement needed.
runners.comment: Showed little in maidens or in first two h'caps, much more needed; reserve

runners.name: Calamint
runners.spotlight: No win since scoring off 2lb higher at Naas in April 2024 (1m, yielding); back on song with a 2.5l second at Navan (1m2f, heavy) on seasonal return (Church Mountain 3l behind); worth another go at this trip on that evidence; chance.
runners.comment: Back on song at Navan latest over 1m2f, worth another go at this trip, chance

runners.name: Hosanna Power
runners.spotlight: No win since May 2024; hasn't looked like ending the drought on the AW on recent starts; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: No win since May 2024, poor form on AW recently, hard to make a case for

runners.name: Miss Crinshawn
runners.spotlight: Returned from a 136-day break with a staying on third at Dundalk (10.5f); not discounted off the same mark upped in trip if repeating that on return to turf.
runners.comment: Staying on third at Dundalk (10.5f) after break, moves up in trip, chance with a repeat

runners.name: Piatra Neamt
runners.spotlight: Shaped like this trip might suit when a 5l sixth of 17 at Navan on handicap debut (1m2f, yielding) in October; never travelled on heavy ground at Galway later that month; this ground more suitable but lacks a recent run.
runners.comment: Promise on Navan h'cap debut, struggled on heavy latest, ground more suitable here

runners.name: Rapid Sequence
runners.spotlight: Modest efforts in maidens at 7f-12.5f for former yard last season; best watched on stable and handicap debut after a 242 day break unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Modest maiden form last year, new yard now, watch on h'cap bow unless market speaks

runners.name: Ellaat
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose veteran has won eight races including four on turf on the Flat; not a bad run on final start for Ian O'Connor in a Cork handicap hurdle in November; off since and entitled to need this on first run for Charles Coakley.
runners.comment: Not a bad run over timber when last seen at Cork in Nov', entitled to need this

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Pageant Girl
runners.spotlight: March foal; dam was a 5f winner (RPR 94); showed speed before weakening on recent Beverley debut (5f, fillies' maiden; 12-1, seventh of 11); that race run in a marginally slower time than division 1 - contested by Bluestone Lady - earlier in the afternoon.
runners.comment: Showed speed before weakening on recent Beverley debut (5f, fillies' maiden; 12-1).

runners.name: Thunda Struck
runners.spotlight: April foal; 16,000euros yearling; second foal from a 6f winner (including 2yo; RPR 96), who was a half-sister to two winners (7.5f-1m4f); trainer is 0-1 with his juveniles this year; let the market guide.
runners.comment: April foal; 16,000euros yearling; second foal from a 6f winner (including 2yo; RPR 96).

runners.name: Mind Those Steps
runners.spotlight: April foal; £40,000 yearling; fourth foal from a Listed-placed 5f-6f winner (RPR 114); half-brother to two winners, most notably Twilight Spinner (6f-7f including Group 3; 113); yard is 0-12 with juveniles this year.
runners.comment: April foal; £40,000 yearling; fourth foal from a Listed-placed 5f-6f winner (RPR 114).

runners.name: Navy Light
runners.spotlight: February foal; 42,000euros foal, 50,000gns yearling; third foal from a Listed-placed 5f-6f winner (including 2yo/AW; RPR 95); half-brother to 6f winner Invincible Boy (84); one of the likelier types among the newcomers.
runners.comment: February foal; 50,000gns yearling; third foal from a Listed-placed 5f-6f winner (RPR 95).

runners.name: Thai Ginger
runners.spotlight: February foal; 100,000gns yearling; first foal from the 2021 Queen Mary Stakes winner Quick Suzy (RPR 106); stable is yet to really get going with juveniles this year but has operated at a 12% strike-rate (24-196) over the last five years; notable candidate.
runners.comment: February foal; 100,000gns yearling; first foal from 2021 Queen Mary Stks winner Quick Suzy

runners.name: Bluestone Lady
runners.spotlight: February foal; half-sister to two winners (6f-8.6f); never better than midfield, eighth of 11 to the promising Princesse d'Orange at Beverley (5f, fillies' novice, good to soft; 9-2) ten days ago; is in capable hands though, so could do better with that experience banked.
runners.comment: Only eighth of 11 at Beverley (5f, fillies' novice; 9-2) ten days ago; could do better.

runners.name: Bettabebougie
runners.spotlight: April foal, and a half sister to a 1m1f Czech winner; hung left-handed when last of four on recent Southwell debut (5f, novice; 10-1; beaten over 14l); will need to improve markedly on that opening AW effort to stake a realistic claim.
runners.comment: Hung left-handed when last of four on recent Southwell debut (5f, novice; 10-1; btn 14l+).

races.race_name: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Romance Spirit
runners.spotlight: Teofilo filly who made an encouraging start to her career when second of five on 1m Southwell debut (Tapeta) 23 days ago, keeping on as if this longer trip is sure to suit; leading claims with improvement likely.
runners.comment: Promising second on 1m debut; this trip should suit better; the one to beat

runners.name: Always Blue
runners.spotlight: By Masar and a sister to a 1m winner in Sweden; in good hands but a watching brief is advised this time unless the betting hints otherwise.
runners.comment: In good hands but best watched this time unless the betting hints otherwise

runners.name: Triumph
runners.spotlight: Good pedigree, being a half-sister to seven winners, including Sheraz (1m3f-1m7f including Listed; RPR 116), Lakota Sioux (7f 2yo including Group 3; 100), Shehiyr (9.5f-1m4f including AW; 99) and Surrey Gold (8.6f-1m6f; 98); dam 1m2f Group 3 winner; doesn't have that high a standard to aim at and market confidence would look significant.
runners.comment: Good pedigree and a threat to all if the betting speaks in her favour

runners.name: Mudita
runners.spotlight: Showed a bit of promise amid inexperience when 5.75l third of eight on 7f Wolverhampton debut in March; didn't achieve a great deal but is likely to improve up nearly 3f in trip.
runners.comment: Some promise amid inexperience when third on 7f debut; up in trip; likely to improve

runners.name: Miss Malone To You
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to connections' top stayer Big Orange and several other winners; 28-1, 5l eighth of 12 on 1m Lingfield debut in December; has the potential for better over longer trips in 2026.
runners.comment: Only eighth on 1m debut but this half-sister to Big Orange has potential for longer trips

runners.name: Tarbat Ness
runners.spotlight: Out-and-out stayer who won twice at Newcastle (2m, Tapeta) at the start of the year but the handicapper has been winning the argument since; it remains to be seen whether a return to turf makes any difference in that regard.
runners.comment: Two wins at Newcastle at the start of the year but handicapper in charge since

runners.name: Rupert The Prince
runners.spotlight: Won three of his last five Flat runs for Paddy Butler, including a clearcut success at Bath (2m1f, soft) in October; no impact on hurdle debut for new yard in January but could fare a lot better back on the Flat.
runners.comment: Well held on hurdle debut for new yard but progressive on Flat last year

runners.name: No More Bolero
runners.spotlight: Ready winner back from a break at Bath (2m1f, good to firm) eight days ago; his 4lb rise is offset by Mason Paetel's claim; likely to follow up.
runners.comment: Ready win at Bath last week and 4lb rise if offset by rider's claim; can follow up

runners.name: Stitching Wheel
runners.spotlight: Displayed early promise on the Flat in France but hasn't shown much since coming to Britain, including back on the Flat at Catterick (1m4f) 17 days ago; up markedly in trip now with a tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Displayed early promise on Flat in France but hasn't shown much in Britain; tongue tied

runners.name: Treasure Islands
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who was never involved on his recent 1m4f Pontefract handicap debut but did show some promise in his qualifying runs; up 4f in trip now.
runners.comment: Never involved on recent 1m4f handicap debut but remains unexposed now up in trip

runners.name: Diamand De Vindecy
runners.spotlight: Arrives fit from hurdling and some of his best Flat form last year came in April, winning at Pontefract before second over C&D (both good).
runners.comment: Fit from hurdling and feasibly treated on last April's Flat form

runners.name: Zimmerman
runners.spotlight: Drew a blank in 2025 but he did reach the frame on both C&D visits, including C&D reappearance; off for 196 days; David Allan partners the other Tim Easterby runner.
runners.comment: Drew a blank in 2025 but did reach the frame on both C&D visits; off for 196 days

races.race_name: M.C.H. Hutchinson Memorial Trophy Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Dancingwithmyself
runners.spotlight: Close second in this race (good) on her 2025 reappearance and went on to score twice at about 1m6f later in the year; returned with a career-best second of 13 at Musselburgh (1m6f, soft) three weeks ago, arguably deserving extra credit considering she met a bit of trouble; successful on her two other visits to Ripon; reasons to be positive.
runners.comment: Fine second on reappearance and Ripon form figures are 112; leading claims

runners.name: Ashnak
runners.spotlight: Pretty useful at up to 1m4f in France but only eighth of 12 in 1m2f Doncaster handicap on British debut four weeks ago; the type to click for this yard at some point but possibly a bit further down the line.
runners.comment: Pretty useful in France but no obvious short-term encouragement on yard debut

runners.name: Stressfree
runners.spotlight: Smart handicapper who won at Haydock (1m4f, good) off 4lb lower last May and recorded several good efforts in defeat later in 2025; has gone well fresh before.
runners.comment: Smart handicapper who ran several good races last year; has gone well fresh before

runners.name: Gibside
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground winner over this trip at Catterick last autumn; hard to derive much encouragement from his recent Thirsk reappearance but the fact he's 2-3 over C&D (all 2022) provides some hope.
runners.comment: Hard to derive much encouragement from recent reappearance but he is 2-3 over C&D

runners.name: Prince Of The Seas
runners.spotlight: Second career success when shading a tight finish at Goodwood (1m4f, good to firm) last August; ended the year with a poor run at Newcastle (1m4f) in September; subsequently sold out of Ralph Beckett's stable for 30,000gns; new yard runs three and the betting is perhaps the best guide to expectations.
runners.comment: Dual winner for Ralph Beckett; new yard runs three and the betting may guide

runners.name: Prydwen
runners.spotlight: Last five outings on AW, posting his most encouraging effort when second of 12 in a 2m Newcastle Good Friday handicap; nudged back up 2lb for that but he's still 3lb lower than when runner-up at Chester (2m, good to soft) last September; should go well.
runners.comment: Good second in a warm race on AW over Easter and should go well back on turf

runners.name: Castan
runners.spotlight: Placed three times on AW over the winter before resuming winning ways over 5f at Southwell 18 days ago; hasn't been successful above a mark of 76 so a career best will be needed if he's to follow up.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell this month but career best needed to follow up off 6lb higher

runners.name: Equity Law
runners.spotlight: Reopposing stablemate Vince L'Amour was ahead in second when sixth of 17 at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft) 13 days ago but it was still quite an encouraging return to action from this 5yo who has dipped to only 1lb above the mark he defied at Catterick (5f, good) last September.
runners.comment: Recent reappearance sixth of 17 quite encouraging and only 1lb above his last winning mark

runners.name: Vince L'Amour
runners.spotlight: Took a good step forward from his reappearance when second (Equity Law sixth) of 17 at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft) 13 days ago; respected off only 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Stepped up on reappearance when second in competitive race at Musselburgh latest

runners.name: Tiva
runners.spotlight: Improved when visor was added to her tongue-tie last year, winning over C&D (good) and at Newmarket (good to firm) in August; starts this campaign 5lb above the mark she defied at Newmarket and might be best watched after 196 days off with both the visor and tongue-tie missing.
runners.comment: C&D win last summer but might be best watched minus usual headgear after 196 days off

runners.name: Dyonisos
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over 6f in France who ran his best race in Britain when second of eight to same owner's Jakajaro at Newmarket (5f, good to soft) 11 days ago; bold show likely from an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Encouraging return to Britain at Newmarket last week; bold show likely from same mark

runners.name: Seven Questions
runners.spotlight: Losing spell goes back to his Palace House success two years ago but he has a reduced mark to work with as a result and he ran well when third on his Pontefract reappearance (5f, good to soft) 18 days ago; player if he can build on that.
runners.comment: Drawn a blank since 2024 Group 3 success but reappearance third quite encouraging

runners.name: Copper Knight
runners.spotlight: None of today's opponents were foaled when he won his first race but this 12yo was still pretty useful last year, winning three times over 5f; the fact he's never won first time up suggests it may pay to look elsewhere this time, though.
runners.comment: This 12yo added three more wins last year but has yet to win first time out

runners.name: Dream Composer
runners.spotlight: Ten-time winner down the years for James Evans; drew a blank in 2025 but runner-up twice off marks in the 90s, including reappearance, so well treated starting out for Tony Carroll (same owners); interesting to see what the betting makes of him.
runners.comment: Multiple winner who is on a good mark on return for new trainer; betting informative

runners.name: Hostility
runners.spotlight: Maiden (0-14), runner-up five times, including in a 6f Southwell novice last November; ran respectably (keen) when fourth in a newly applied visor at Catterick (7f, good) latest; should stay a mile (first go) and is on a fair mark, more so if factoring in Mason Paetel's 5lb claim.
runners.comment: 0-14; ran respectably when fourth in a newly applied visor at Catterick (7f, good) latest.

runners.name: Amidst The Chaos
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back at Doncaster (1m) and Musselburgh (1m1f) last August, running down Drumcondra latterly; held off higher marks since, including when ninth of 13 at Thirsk (1m, good; beaten nearly 6l) latest; Ethan Tindall's 5lb claim is helpful.
runners.comment: Won at Doncaster (1m) & Musselburgh (1m1f) last August; ninth of 13 at Thirsk (1m) latest.

runners.name: Up The Jazz
runners.spotlight: Last turf win was here (1m2f, good to soft; from 6lb higher) in June 2024; fair third at Wolverhampton (8.5f; 10-1) on penultimate start but took a backward step at Doncaster (1m2f) subsequently; easing mark isn't enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Third at Wolverhampton (8.5f; 10-1) penultimate; backward step at Doncaster (1m2f) since.

runners.name: Poet's Dawn
runners.spotlight: Veteran (12-121); seventh (beaten 4l; 40-1) in this last year; broadly consistent since accounting for Ravishing Beauty at Carlisle (1m, soft; similar terms) last September; should strip fitter for recent Pontefract (1m; fourth of nine; 33-1) comeback run.
runners.comment: Veteran (12-121); seventh last year; should strip fitter for Pontefract (1m) comeback run.

runners.name: Kiss Me My Love
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Beverley (8.5f, soft; from 2lb lower) last September but reportedly ran too freely when soundly beaten at Nottingham next time; caught the eye when fourth in a Class 4 at Southwell (1m; 40-1) recently, but it mightn't be wise to take that form literally.
runners.comment: Sits 2lb above last winning mark; fourth in a Class 4 at Southwell (40-1) latest.

runners.name: Ravishing Beauty
runners.spotlight: In fine fettle last summer, winning at Pontefract (1m) and Nottingham (8.5f); found Poet's Dawn too strong at Carlisle (1m, soft; similar terms) in September; off since finishing third at Newcastle in October, so may strip fitter for this comeback run.
runners.comment: Won twice last summer (8f-8.5f); runner-up to Poet's Dawn at Carlisle (1m) in September.

runners.name: The Sweet Escape
runners.spotlight: Dropped back in trip when getting off the mark at the 13th attempt at Nottingham (8.5f, good) latest; the winning margin there was just a nose but, with a 3lb rise, he remains on a reasonably competitive mark; Hollie Doyle is back aboard.
runners.comment: Off the mark at the 13th attempt at Nottingham (8.5f, good) latest; now 3lb higher.

runners.name: Keats House
runners.spotlight: Lit up by a visor (too keen) when a weakening third over C&D last June; did manage to win twice thereafter (with headgear discarded), including from 7lb lower at Musselburgh (1m, good to soft) in September; potentially vulnerable off a 192-day absence.
runners.comment: Won from 7lb lower at Musselburgh (1m, good to soft) in September; potentially vulnerable.

runners.name: Rosemary May
runners.spotlight: Rallied to win a modest maiden at Ayr (1m, good; 11-2) last April; disappointing since introduced to handicapping, scarcely managing to beat a rival in three attempts (7f-1m); an easing mark could aid her cause on return from ten-month absence; stablemate of Poet's Dawn.
runners.comment: Disappointing efforts handicapping (7f-1m); easing mark for return from ten-month absence.

runners.name: Highland Olly
runners.spotlight: Built on Wolverhampton comeback run (7f) when showing a willing attitude to make all at Nottingham (8.5f, good) last Saturday; 3lb rise isn't insurmountable but he is set to compete from a career-high mark, so further progress is required.
runners.comment: Showed a willing attitude to make all at Nottingham (8.5f, good) last Saturday; 3lb higher

races.race_name: Watch Sky Sports Racing In HD Handicap

runners.name: Arishka's Dream
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough at Wolverhampton (6f, maiden; 4-1) in February; has bettered that since (on RPRs) with narrow defeats at Lingfield and Newcastle; 2lb higher again, but possible he can stake a claim if proving as effective on turf (debut).
runners.comment: 6f Wolverhampton maiden winner; twice narrowly beaten since; merits respect on turf debut.

runners.name: Liverpool Star
runners.spotlight: Yet to win (0-10), going closest when beaten a head at Newcastle (6f nursery) in December; bumped at the start but recovered to do solid late work at Catterick (5f, good; third of ten, beaten 1l) 17 days ago; same mark (holds Contorno) for return to 6f.
runners.comment: Bumped at the start but recovered to do solid late work at Catterick (5f) latest; 6f fine.

runners.name: Contorno
runners.spotlight: Dictated a steady pace to maintain his unbeaten Newcastle record (5f; 2-2) on penultimate start; no excuses when a weakening fifth (Liverpool Star third) on turf return at Catterick (5f, good; 6-1) 17 days ago; 2lb above last winning mark (AW).
runners.comment: No excuses when a weakening fifth (Liverpool Star third) on turf return at Catterick (5f).

runners.name: Ecclefechan
runners.spotlight: 0-8; lost out in tight finishes at Musselburgh (5f novice) and Lingfield (6f nursery) as a juvenile; gelded over the winter and fifth of eight on Kempton return (7f; 14-1, beaten 4l) six weeks ago; 1lb lower but likely needs a fraction more.
runners.comment: 0-8; fifth of eight on Kempton return (7f; 14-1, beaten 4l) six weeks ago; 1lb lower.

runners.name: Blue Nguru
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Hamilton and Catterick (6f fillies' maiden; first-time cheekpieces) prior to being slowly away at Lingfield (7f) in early December; took her chance - always handy - back there (5f novice) a fortnight later, and has been given a four-month break since; retains potential.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Lingfield (5f novice) in December; retains potential off four-month break.

runners.name: Vencedora
runners.spotlight: Shaped well on Haydock debut (third) and off the mark next time in a modest three-runner Newcastle novice (6f; 2-1; made all); possibly needed her recent comeback run at Southwell (7f; fifth of seven), and dropping back in trip looks sensible; chance, despite a poor draw.
runners.comment: Newcastle novice winner (6f); possibly needed comeback run at Southwell (7f) ten days ago.
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Solar Cooper
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress for Brian Ellison (0-4; 5f-6f); hung left-handed and dropped out accordingly on recent stable debut at Beverley (7.5f; 13-2, eighth of ten); may draw benefit from that comeback run but others make way more appeal.
runners.comment: Hung left-handed and dropped out accordingly on recent stable debut at Beverley (7.5f).

runners.name: Siouxpersonic
runners.spotlight: With Ed Bethell when knuckling down to land the odds in a 5f fillies' maiden at Southwell (runner-up 2-3 since) last September; joined this trainer the following month for 40,000gns; is fairly weighted back over 6f in handicap company.
runners.comment: 5f Southwell winner for Ed Bethell; sold for 40,000gns; returns to 6f for stable debut.

runners.name: Koffee And Kale
runners.spotlight: Rewarded for a series of consistent efforts when scrambling home in a 5f novice at Leicester (6-5 favourite) last October; gelded, and back from a winter break with the possibility of more to come; shouldn't be troubled by returning to 6f.
runners.comment: Scrambled home in 5f novice at Leicester (6-5 fav) last October; gelded; back from a break

runners.name: Hares Bredth
runners.spotlight: Didn't offer much initially, but did at least get closer to the principals when fifth of ten on handicap debut at Pontefract (6f, nursery; soft; in a first-time tongue-tie) last October; hasn't that many miles on the clock but does lack a recent run; others preferred.
runners.comment: Closer when fifth on handicap debut at Pontefract (6f; first-time tongue-tie) last October

runners.name: Baldosa
runners.spotlight: Enters calculations on her solid 6f Newcastle third in March but she came in only 11th in 6f Pontefract handicap 18 days ago; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Only 11th in 6f Pontefract handicap 18 days ago; she needs to bounce back

runners.name: Sparkling Pink
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in 6f Pontefract nursery in July (for Jonathan Portman) but she has failed to build on it and beat only one on her seasonal return in 6f Newcastle handicap last month; not easy to make a case for.
runners.comment: Beat one on her return in 6f Newcastle handicap last month; not easy to make a case for

runners.name: Space Moon
runners.spotlight: Modest form shown in her five runs thus far; posted a solid sixth of 17 in 7f Leicester handicap 12 days ago so she needs considering eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Solid sixth of 17 in 7f Leicester handicap 12 days ago so she needs considering eased 1lb

runners.name: Thanos
runners.spotlight: Son of Lope Y Fernandez who took a big step forward when sent handicapping as 2yo, signing off with a very good second of seven at Southwell (7f) in November; since gelded; likely player nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Very good second at Southwell in November; since gelded and likely player nudged up 1lb

runners.name: Popty Ping
runners.spotlight: In very good form on the AW this winter and she resumed winning ways in 7f Southwell handicap last month; up 4lb but she can make her presence felt again back on turf.
runners.comment: Resumed winning ways at Southwell last month; can make her presence felt back on turf

runners.name: Antique Blue
runners.spotlight: Hinted at promise when a debut Wolverhampton third (for Andrew Balding) last June but he's failed to build on it for his current yard, hooded when only eighth on his handicap debut at Pontefract (6f) 18 days ago; more is required.
runners.comment: Hooded when eighth on his handicap debut at Pontefract (6f) 18 days ago; more is required
runners.owner: Miss K Padgett & Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Mojito
runners.spotlight: Palace Pier filly who was brought along steadily last autumn, going very keenly when last of ten in 7f Newmarket maiden in September; she's likely capable of better now going into handicaps for her in-form yard.
runners.comment: Palace Pier filly; brought along steadily last autumn; capable of better on h'cap debut

runners.name: Thankfully Simmy
runners.spotlight: Proving consistent; gelded before a keen-going fourth of seven in 6f Newcastle handicap last month; he remains with some potential at this trip if proving more tractable.
runners.comment: Keen-going fourth at Newcastle latest; remains with some potential if more tractable

runners.name: Torbados
runners.spotlight: Made a good start in nurseries when third at Lingfield (7f) in October but too free when beating one in similar company at Kempton (6f) in December; since gelded and this son of Ardad could bounce back.
runners.comment: Too free when beating one at Kempton in December; since gelded and could bounce back

runners.name: Maximum Velocity
runners.spotlight: Dandy Man gelding who arrives in very good nick, second of 17 in 7f Leicester handicap 12 days ago; he can go well again despite a 2lb weights rise in his bid for a maiden success.
runners.comment: Arrives in very good nick, second of 17 at Leicester 12 days ago; he can go well again

runners.name: Twilight Serenade
runners.spotlight: Just poor form shown as 2yo, tried in visor when a below-par fourth in 7f Wolverhampton nursery in November; needs a subsequent gelding operation to spark improvement.
runners.comment: Below-par fourth at Wolverhampton in November; needs gelding operation to spark progress

runners.name: Twilight Dawn
runners.spotlight: Offered little in her five juvenile runs, just seventh of ten in 7f Newcastle nursery in November; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Offered little in her five juvenile runs, seventh in 7f Newcastle nursery in November

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Proposal
runners.spotlight: Progressive Frankel filly who came in second of nine in 7f Newcastle novice in October; she has more to offer so must enter calculations on her seasonal return.
runners.comment: Frankel filly ended 2025 with a good Newcastle second; must enter calculations on return

runners.name: Tahalel
runners.spotlight: Daughter of Blue Point who made a promising start when second in a 6f Haydock maiden last May but she failed to build on it in two subsequent outings, although not totally disgraced when third in 7f Salisbury maiden in October; she fetched 500,000euros as a yearling and could still do better.
runners.comment: Promising Haydock second but twice failed to build on it; could still do better in 2026

runners.name: Venetian Gold
runners.spotlight: 62,000gns 2yo; dam 5f-6f winner (Listed; RPR 111), half-sister to Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner Looks A Million; this daughter of St Mark's Basilica is in good hands and can't be ruled out on her debut.
runners.comment: This daughter of St Mark's Basilica is in good hands and can't be ruled out on her debut

runners.name: Yasalam
runners.spotlight: Night Of Thunder filly who failed to build on a debut 7f Wolverhampton fourth in August when last of five in 6f Kempton novice last month; she's worth another chance.
runners.comment: Failed to build on debut when last of five at Kempton last month; worth another chance

runners.name: Ashley Pool
runners.spotlight: Expert Eye filly; dam unraced half-sister to winners Eileendover (useful bumper and 1m6f Flat) and Special Mayson (6f/7f), out of 1m winner, is a granddaughter of 1,000 Guineas winner Speciosa; the market can guide for this newcomer.
runners.comment: Expert Eye filly; the market can guide for this newcomer

runners.name: Melting Snow
runners.spotlight: Too Darn Hot filly; dam a Group-placed 1m2f winner (RPR 105), is closely related to 1m2f Listed winner John Leeper, out of Oaks, Irish Oaks and Irish Champion winner Snow Fairy; bred to be well above average and she needs considering on her first run.
runners.comment: Too Darn Hot filly; bred to be well above average and needs considering on her first run

runners.name: Moving Rubber
runners.spotlight: £22,000 yearling; Zoustar filly; dam placed at 5f/6f (RPR 72), is a half-sister to winners Gilt Edge Girl (5f Group 1 Abbaye) and Godfrey Street (5f 2yo Group 2); the betting can prove an accurate guide for this newcomer.
runners.comment: Zoustar filly; the betting can prove an accurate guide for this newcomer

runners.name: Eva The Deeva
runners.spotlight: Never dangerous in 7f Newcastle maiden and 6f Pontefract novice this spring; this Territories filly can do better in the longer term.
runners.comment: Never dangerous in Newcastle maiden and Pontefract novice; can do better in due course

runners.name: Ivat Ghia
runners.spotlight: 80-1, always behind when eighth of 12 in 7f Kempton novice on her debut in February; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Always behind when eighth in Kempton novice on her debut in February; lots more is needed

runners.name: Lunar Melody
runners.spotlight: 7-2 and green, shaped with plenty of promise when a debut fifth of ten in novice at Carlisle (6f, good) in September, finishing well after a very slow start; this daughter of Havana Grey was a 500,000euros buy at the breeze-ups as a juvenile and is a big player here with a lot better to come.
runners.comment: Very promising debut fifth at Carlisle in September; capable of a lot better and a player

runners.name: Time To Take Off
runners.spotlight: Starman filly who impressed when making a winning start in 5f Nottingham maiden in October; again shaped well when an odds-on second of three in 5f Wolverhampton novice on her return last month, not enjoying a clear passage, so she's very much the one to beat.
runners.comment: Debut Nottingham winner; shaped well when Wolverhampton second on return; the one to beat

runners.name: Lady Grey
runners.spotlight: £20,000 yearling; dam 1m AW/1m2f turf winner (RPR 64); the betting can prove an accurate guide for this Havana Grey newcomer.
runners.comment: The betting can prove an accurate guide for this Havana Grey newcomer

runners.name: You Mystify Me
runners.spotlight: 16,000euros yearling, 55,000gns 2yo; dam, 5f winner (RPR 70), is closely related to 6f winner So Many Questions; this A'Ali filly is no forlorn hope on her first run.
runners.comment: This A'Ali filly is no forlorn hope on her first run
runners.owner: N Bradley Racing 29 & South Bank Racing

runners.name: Hallandale Beach
runners.spotlight: Havana Grey colt who has hinted at promise in a pair of 6f novices at Chelmsford and Wolverhampton this spring, fifth on latter occasion eight days ago; holds place prospects.
runners.comment: Hinted at promise in pair of 6f novices at Chelmsford and Wolverhampton; place prospects

runners.name: Manatee Mehmas
runners.spotlight: 105,000gns yearling; brother to 5f winner Tiva (RPR 86); dam placed at 6f (RPR 71), is closely related to 6f Group 2 winner Tasleet; this son of Mehmas is in good hands and can't be discounted on his debut, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Son of Mehmas can't be discounted on his debut, especially if market vibes are positive

runners.name: Sanny Doo
runners.spotlight: Sent of 11-4 but he came in a tailed-off last of four in 6f Wolverhampton novice on his sole run for James Tate ten months ago; since gelded and hood goes on for his new yard.
runners.comment: Last at Wolverhampton on sole run for James Tate ten months ago; hooded for new yard

runners.name: Tickhill Flyer
runners.spotlight: 40-1, last of seven in novice at Ripon (6f, good to soft) on his debut nine days ago; significantly more is required from this son of Tasleet.
runners.comment: Last at Ripon on debut nine days ago; significantly more is required

runners.name: Aurora's Doublesix
runners.spotlight: Yet to offer much for three different trainers, undergoing wind surgery before beating one in 6f Newcastle classified event in February; blinkers are tried now.
runners.comment: Yet to offer much and beat one at Newcastle in February; blinkers are tried now

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Premier Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Naga
runners.spotlight: Low mileage and ended 2025 firmly on the up with a pair of 1m handicap victories at Chelmsford and Newmarket in October; up another 5lb but this well-related Dubawi filly remains on a workable mark; big shout on her first go at 1m2f.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with 1m h'cap wins at Chelmsford and Newmarket; big shout going up in trip

runners.name: Golden Muse
runners.spotlight: Progressive Golden Horn filly who bids for a hat-trick after bagging novices at Wolverhampton (9.5f) and Southwell (1m4f) last month; capable of better still now venturing into handicap company on her turf debut.
runners.comment: Bagged novices at Wolverhampton and Southwell; capable of better on h'cap/turf debut

runners.name: Rockin' The Boat
runners.spotlight: Still to register a victory but she's proving a reliable filly and returned from eight months off with a clear second of five in 1m Southwell handicap last month; can race off the same mark here and this step up in trip promises to suit so she's a player.
runners.comment: Clear second on return at Southwell; a player with this step up in trip promising to suit

runners.name: Amora Queen
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start over 7f here and followed up in 1m Chelmsford novice last summer; failed to beat a rival in the Fillies' Mile at Newmarket after but she remains with potential on her return in this less exacting company.
runners.comment: Course winner; failed to beat rival in Fillies Mile at Newmarket; remains with potential

runners.name: Silky Lass
runners.spotlight: Built on earlier promise when gaining her breakthrough victory in 1m novice at Newcastle in February; this Massaat filly is another who can't be ruled out on her handicap debut stepping up in distance.
runners.comment: Breakthrough win at Newcastle in February; not ruled out on h'cap debut up in distance

runners.name: Vilaine Verlaine
runners.spotlight: A dual 1m2f winner at 2yrs but she's been off the track since coming in last of ten in 1m2f Sandown handicap last July; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Dual 1m2f winner but off since coming in last at Sandown last July; others are preferred

races.race_name: M&D Foundations Piling Specialists Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Sailor Song
runners.spotlight: Made debut in February; improved when second in maidens at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) and Southwell (1m3f, AW; under Toby Moore) and now has cheekpieces after Al Azd proved too strong for him late on in the latest race; should be competitive on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces now after Al Azd proved too strong late on Southwell (1m3f, AW); player

runners.name: Al Azd
runners.spotlight: Looks quirky but he followed three seconds at 1m/1m2f by asserting late on from Sailor Song in a 1m3f maiden at Southwell (AW) two weeks ago, looking suited by the extra distance; blinkered on last two starts; 2025 turf form to start career was modest but another extra furlong should help; player.
runners.comment: Looks quirky but beat Sailor Song in Southwell maiden (AW) when suited by move up to 1m3f

runners.name: Shushi
runners.spotlight: Fair form on all three starts, best form on soft ground on second occasion; latest was her closest finish, though, when making most in a 1m3f maiden at Kempton (AW) and that performance suggests she will stay 1m4f; needs to find extra to get off the mark on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: 1m3f AW maiden latest suggests she will stay 1m4f; needs to find extra on handicap debut

runners.name: Director's Cut
runners.spotlight: Two runs in November were on AW, rallying to win a novice at Newcastle (1m) on second occasion; fair fifth of nine in a novice at Nottingham (1m2f, good) from the front on reappearance did not press his claims for today's handicap debut, but these are early days.
runners.comment: Fair 5th at Nottingham (1m2f, good) on return did not press his claims for handicap debut

runners.name: Alba Gu Brath
runners.spotlight: Made debut in December; runner-up on first two starts before making all at 4-9 in a five-runner novice at Newcastle in January; all those races were 1m on AW and he kept hanging left last time, but he makes a striking move up to 1m4f for this handicap/turf debut and needs a close look.
runners.comment: Makes a striking move from 1m to 1m4f for this handicap/turf debut and needs a close look

runners.name: Stoneacre Donny
runners.spotlight: 33-1 winner in an eight-runner novice at Thirsk (7f, soft) last September, coming from towards rear to lead in final strides; good third of seven in nursery at Nottingham (1m2f, good to soft; gelded since) in October made 1m4f looks plausible but he needs further improvement off the same mark.
runners.comment: Good third of seven off this mark at Nottingham (1m2f) in October; gelded since

runners.name: Lopeo
runners.spotlight: Showed staying potential with a 1m win on heavy ground in three-runner race at Goodwood last September, which was far better than his starts either side; gelded since; should be capable of better this season switched to handicapping but it is needed.
runners.comment: 1m win on heavy at Goodwood (three ran) last September, far better than starts either side

runners.name: Sea Of Kings
runners.spotlight: Shaped well without getting his head in front last season, signing off with a good fourth in the Melrose at York in August; this brother to the Irish Oaks winner Sea Of Class has since been gelded and looks a good prospect for 2026; player.
runners.comment: Shaped well without winning last term; gelded since and remains a good prospect for 2026

runners.name: Charmaine
runners.spotlight: A two-time AW scorer (around 1m4f) last spring and she resumed from six months off with a solid second of seven in 1m4f Wolverhampton handicap three weeks ago; she can make her presence felt back on turf.
runners.comment: Off six months off before solid second at Wolverhampton recently; can make presence felt

runners.name: Something
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m4f scorer for Jedd O'Keeffe; resurgent for his new yard when runner-up at Kempton (1m3f) in February but well held at Yarmouth two weeks ago; the sort to bounce back though.
runners.comment: Good second at Kempton but well held at Yarmouth since; the sort to bounce back

runners.name: Kimeko Glory
runners.spotlight: Bagged three 1m2f handicaps last season (final one for Brian Toomey) and started well for her new handler until beating just two in 1m2f Lingfield handicap in January; returns from a break here.
runners.comment: Started well for new yard until beating two at Lingfield in January; returns from a break

runners.name: Ghaiyya
runners.spotlight: A three-time 1m4f winner last summer but she ended 2025 under something of a cloud; has gone well fresh though so not out of things on her seasonal return.
runners.comment: A three-time 1m4f winner but ended 2025 under a cloud; has gone well fresh though

runners.name: Maghlaak
runners.spotlight: Ended his time with Adrian Keatley below par last autumn but a winner over fences at Southwell for his new yard before being pulled up at Market Rasen in December; needs to bounce back after a layoff.
runners.comment: Won over fences at Southwell before pulled up at Market Rasen in December; more is needed

runners.name: Barleybrown
runners.spotlight: Without a win since October 2024 and he failed to build on a reappearance Newcastle third when just seventh in 1m handicap there 12 days ago; needs to take a step forward.
runners.comment: Without a win since October 2024; just seventh at Newcastle 12 days ago; more needed

runners.name: Pisanello
runners.spotlight: A useful 1m winner at his best but this veteran seems not so good nowadays; he has gone well off a break though so is no forlorn hope on his seasonal return.
runners.comment: Veteran is on a losing run but has gone well off a break though so is no forlorn hope

runners.name: Mariner
runners.spotlight: Took this 12 months ago; not seen after June in 2025 but hinted at a revival on his second run back when seventh of 13 in 1m Thirsk handicap 15 days ago; he's one to be interested in off a 2lb lower mark here.
runners.comment: Took this 12 months ago; hinted at revival with Thirsk seventh latest; interesting

runners.name: Relevant Range
runners.spotlight: Already a dual 1m AW winner in 2026 but he saw his run of good form come to an end when tenth in 1m2f handicap here four weeks ago; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Dual 1m AW winner in 2026; tenth in h'cap here four weeks ago so needs to bounce back

runners.name: Pretty Spirited
runners.spotlight: Scored twice (around 1m) at Leicester and Lingfield last summer but not seen out after a solid third in 8.5f Wolverhampton handicap in September; she has gone well fresh though so can't be discounted.
runners.comment: In good form when last seen in the autumn; has gone well fresh too so can't be discounted
runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya

runners.name: Queen Of Good News
runners.spotlight: Underwent breathing surgery before gaining a breakthrough win on her seasonal return in 1m Yarmouth handicap two weeks ago; back up 4lb but she's not taken lightly in her current mood.
runners.comment: Underwent breathing surgery before breakthrough Yarmouth win; up 4lb but not taken lightly

runners.name: Union Island
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways at Lingfield (1m) in February but he beat one in 1m Southwell handicap 20 days ago; needs to bounce back on turf.
runners.comment: Won at Lingfield but beat one in Southwell h'cap 20 days ago; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Chuzzlewit
runners.spotlight: Without a victory since March 2024 and he's yet to hit top form in a trio of handicaps at Newcastle this year, only seventh of nine over 1m there last month.
runners.comment: Winless since March 2024; yet to hit top form in trio of handicaps at Newcastle this year

runners.name: Fire Eyes
runners.spotlight: A dual 7f/1m scorer at Redcar last autumn and she comes here in decent nick, suffering a poor run when seventh of ten in 8.5f Beverley handicap ten days ago; needs considering off a slipping mark.
runners.comment: In decent nick, suffering a poor run when seventh at Beverley latest; considered

runners.name: U S S Constitution
runners.spotlight: Made a winning return after a spell in Hong Kong in 7f Kempton handicap and backed it up with a solid third of seven at Lingfield (7f) later in February; not out of things back on turf with this step up to 1m a likely plus.
runners.comment: Winning return at Kempton; solid Lingfield third since so not out of things back on turf

runners.name: Wolf Of Badenoch
runners.spotlight: Scored on his debut here in June 2024 but without a victory since and fitted with eyeshields when failing to beat a rival in 7f Wolverhampton handicap last time; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in 7f Wolverhampton handicap last time; hard to warm to

runners.name: Siam Fox
runners.spotlight: It's now 14 starts since his last success and he beat just one in 1m Southwell handicap when last seen in October; this C&D winner needs a subsequent wind operation to spark a resurgence.
runners.comment: Beat one at Southwell in October; this C&D winner needs a wind op to spark a resurgence

races.race_name: Celebrate With Sponsorship At Southwell Racecourse "Confined" Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Clover Time
runners.spotlight: Seemingly aided by cheekpieces when runner-up five consecutive times (once here) over 6f in September-January, and last month's 5f fourth at Chelmsford came in a slightly better race than this; worth persisting with over this trip.
runners.comment: Runner-up five times over 6f since September; fourth in better 5f race than this in March

runners.name: Sonic Si
runners.spotlight: 0-12 overall but has made the frame on the last three of his four starts for Tony Carroll, most recently when fourth at Lingfield (5f) last month; also suited by Tapeta and continues to edge down the weights; considered.
runners.comment: 0-12 overall but has made the frame on last three of his four stable starts; considered

runners.name: Irish Dancer
runners.spotlight: Won twice over C&D last year and came second twice over C&D last month; needs to rebound from a disappointing run ten days ago but he's largely consistent and features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Didn't fire here recently but came second twice over C&D last month; must be considered

runners.name: Man On A Mission
runners.spotlight: Raced exclusively over 6f since returning from a break in December, making the frame on three of his last five appearances (twice here); visored for first time since 2022 (has sometimes worn other headgear since); probably reliant on a good pace to aim at over this trip.
runners.comment: In the frame on three of his last five 6f starts; needs a good pace to aim at over 5f

runners.name: Cabeza De Llave
runners.spotlight: Consistent 7yo who kept on from the rear for third under James Glennon at Chelmsford (5f) in February, after a layoff; has never run here before but has form on Tapeta at Wolverhampton; can make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Consistent 7yo who was placed under James Glennon at Chelmsford in February

runners.name: Coast
runners.spotlight: Well down the field over 6f here on latest outing six weeks ago and remains without a win since summer 2024; very difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Well beaten over 6f here last month and remains without a win since summer 2024

runners.name: Brian The Snail
runners.spotlight: Veteran with 14 wins on his CV; kept on for third when dropped back to 5f at Newcastle 12 days ago but is inconsistent nowadays and younger rivals appeal more.
runners.comment: Placed at Newcastle recently but inconsistent nowadays; younger rivals appeal more

races.race_name: Summer Social Events At Southwell Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Abila
runners.spotlight: Ex-French; has only beaten three rivals in four runs for current trainer; cheekpieces worn when last of ten at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) last month are persevered with; needs to show significant improvement for the return to this longer trip.
runners.comment: Has only beaten three rivals in four runs for current trainer.

runners.name: Hickton
runners.spotlight: Won here (1m4f) off 4lb lower last October; reported to have bled from the nose when beaten 34l over C&D last month, but soundly beaten at Catterick (1m4f, good) since; holds claims if he can return to form.
runners.comment: Won here (1m4f) off 4lb lower last October; soundly beaten twice since.

runners.name: Argentum
runners.spotlight: Placed in a couple of low-grade handicaps at around 1m4f but well held on last two Flat starts and also on hurdles debut last month; more required.
runners.comment: Well held on last two Flat starts and also on hurdles debut last month.

runners.name: Apache Eagle
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden but didn't fare too badly when third of eight over C&D last month; has place claims if able to reproduce that effort.
runners.comment: 14-race maiden but didn't fare too badly when third over C&D last month.

runners.name: Sovereign Applause
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who was beaten 24l at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) four weeks ago in first-time cheekpieces (replaced by first-time visor here); steps back up in trip and sliding down the weights but difficult to make a case for.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden who was beaten 24l at Doncaster four weeks ago.

runners.name: Ring Fenced
runners.spotlight: Sole success came when trained by Andrew Balding in 2023; plenty of water under the bridge since but showed signs that another win may not be far away when second of ten over C&D last month, running on after her path was blocked; place claims at least.
runners.comment: Showed signs that another win may not be far away when second of ten over C&D last month.

runners.name: Angelardo
runners.spotlight: The step up in distance did the trick when he scored at Newcastle (2m, AW) last month; always in rear when last of eight at Chelmsford (13.5f, AW) since; holds claims if leaving latest effort behind.
runners.comment: The step up in distance did the trick when he scored at Newcastle last month.

runners.name: Easter Sundae
runners.spotlight: Placed for the first time when second of nine at Newcastle (12.5f, AW; 100-1) in February in first-time cheekpieces (retained since); beat only one home when beaten 26l here (1m4f) 20 days ago; has possibilities if able to show his best form.
runners.comment: Placed for the first time when second at Newcastle in February, but well beaten since.

runners.name: Trouble In Chicago
runners.spotlight: Huge prices and well beaten in five runs to date (6f-1m2f); takes a significant step up in trip on her handicap debut in first-time blinkers; one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Huge prices and well beaten in five runs to date (6f-1m2f).

runners.name: Nala The Lioness
runners.spotlight: Runner-up four times for Karl Burke but poor form in three runs for new yard following a 400-day absence; has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Poor form in three runs this year for new yard following a 400-day absence.

runners.name: Rose Warrior
runners.spotlight: Showed bits of form when trained by the Kublers, latest when fourth of 16 at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) last October in first-time blinkers; sold for 8,000gns subsequently and switches to first-time cheekpieces on stable debut; one to watch out for in the betting.
runners.comment: Has shown bits of form, latest when fourth at Lingfield last October; new headgear.

races.race_name: Book The Bistro In Sherwood Restaurant Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Chasing Time
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut for top yard at Wolverhampton (1m4f) three weeks ago and normal progress should see him bang there at the finish.
runners.comment: Made a winning debut for top yard at Wolverhampton three weeks ago.

runners.name: Kenneth
runners.spotlight: Encouraging second to a subsequent Group winner on his debut in a Newbury maiden (1m3f, good) last April; off since and has been gelded; must be taken seriously.
runners.comment: Second to a subsequent Group winner on his debut in a Newbury maiden last April.

runners.name: Birgham Dub
runners.spotlight: 240,000euros yearling; by Sea The Moon out of a close relation to 1m2f Group 1 winner Sultanina; second favourite for seven-runner maiden at Haydock (7f, good) last July, which he nearly blew when making an awkward start and first off the bridle but he came from the back and stuck his neck out well for a narrow win; absent since but should be open to major improvement on AW debut.
runners.comment: Won seven-runner maiden at Haydock last July on debut.

runners.name: Peat And Diesel
runners.spotlight: Second foal; by Flag of Honour; dam tailed off in bumper, half-sister to winners Grand Canyon (1m1f/1m2f Flat and 2m hurdle) and Alquimia (7f/1m), out of 7f winning half-sister to US 6f 2yo Grade 2 winner Discreet Cat; likely to need more time.
runners.comment: Minor appeal on paper and makes his Flat debut in a warm novice.

runners.name: Ramli
runners.spotlight: Finished third in a Huntingdon bumper three weeks ago when trained by Michael Keady; this looks a tough assignment on his Flat debut.
runners.comment: Third in a Huntingdon bumper for Michael Keady three weeks ago; tough task on Flat debut.

runners.name: Florentine Law
runners.spotlight: Slowly away on his debut when fourth of five at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) two weeks ago; will be sharper with that behind him and should win races in time.
runners.comment: Slowly away on his debut when fourth of five at Yarmouth two weeks ago.

runners.name: Temple
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in a couple of maidens (around 1m) last October; steps up in trip on AW debut but may be a longer-term project; Birgham Dub looks the trainer's main hope.
runners.comment: Showed promise in a couple of maidens (around 1m) last October.

runners.name: Dartmouth Jet
runners.spotlight: Landed a bumper at Worcester on his racecourse debut last September; well-held on first Flat start here (1m3f; 66-1) in November; has his work cut out in this company.
runners.comment: Well-held on first Flat start here (1m3f; 66-1) in November.

runners.name: Winston's Warrior
runners.spotlight: Showed improved form in first-time blinkers when getting off the mark at Kempton (1m, AW) last September and was then a good third at Lingfield the following month; headgear left off on this return to action and he has been gelded; not out of this.
runners.comment: Won at Kempton last September and a good third at Lingfield the following month.

runners.name: Farasi Lane
runners.spotlight: Returned to winning form at Wolverhampton (8.5f) last month and followed up back there (7f) seven days later; back up in grade in search of a three-timer and although this looks tougher he cannot de discounted.
runners.comment: Returned to winning form at Wolverhampton last month with two wins.

runners.name: Selection
runners.spotlight: Had been running well in AW handicaps since winning at Newcastle (1m) last October, until disappointing when last of six here (1m) 20 days ago; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Had been running well in AW handicaps until finishing last here recently.

runners.name: Down To The Kid
runners.spotlight: Nine wins at this course (7f-1m4f), including three this year; remains in top form, having finished runner-up here on his last two starts; must be strongly considered on his favoured track.
runners.comment: Nine wins at this course (7f-1m4f), including three this year, and he remains in top form.

runners.name: Eligible
runners.spotlight: Seven of his 12 wins have been here (five over this trip); has never won off a mark this high however and most likely needs to drop a few pounds.
runners.comment: Seven of his 12 wins have been here (five over this trip); never won off a mark this high.

runners.name: Believe The Storm
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut in December 2024 and ran well in defeat on several occasions last year; signed off with a poor effort when ninth of 11 at Newbury (7f, good to soft) last September but handicapper has given him a chance if he can hit the boards running.
runners.comment: Made winning debut in December 2024 and ran well in defeat on several occasions last year.

runners.name: Rock Iguana
runners.spotlight: Won maiden here (6f) last August on stable debut and performed with credit on two of his last three starts; returns from a break and still unexposed; holds claims.
runners.comment: Won maiden here last August and performed with credit on two of his last three starts.

runners.name: Kisskodi
runners.spotlight: All four of his wins were on turf last year, but he was runner-up on his sole AW start at Newcastle; his progressive profile gives him a squeak but he may be better for his first outing since October.
runners.comment: Won four on turf last year, but he was runner-up on his sole AW start at Newcastle.

runners.name: Bell Shot
runners.spotlight: Has nine wins (6f-7.5f); 33-1 winner here (6f) in January in reapplied visor (retained since) but below that form at Lingfield (7f) subsequently; returns from a short break and is 2lb above his last winning mark; needs a career-best.
runners.comment: 33-1 winner here (6f) in January but below that form at Lingfield (7f) since.
runners.owner: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd

races.race_name: Conference & Events At Southwell Racecourse Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C & D) (GBB)

runners.name: Sandy Craic
runners.spotlight: Better with each of her three runs last summer and started among the favourites on her handicap debut/comeback from nine months off recently; didn't look to see out a stiff 7.4f that day and has the edge over most in terms of experience.
runners.comment: Didn't look to see out 7.4f on comeback; has the edge over most in terms of experience

runners.name: Positive Thoughts
runners.spotlight: Three 7f runs last autumn, her C&D second looking a notch above her two soft-ground efforts either side; needs more back from six months off on her first crack at sprinting.
runners.comment: Three 7f runs last autumn; needs more back from six months off on first crack at sprinting

runners.name: Zarinca
runners.spotlight: Came away with a subsequent winner, despite needing the experience, on her 6f Newcastle debut last September; didn't look to see out 7f from a wide stall when a beaten favourite on her next start here and retains potential for a yard in good form.
runners.comment: Didn't look to see out 7f here when last seen; retains potential for a yard in good form

runners.name: Rumble Ruby
runners.spotlight: 25,000gns yearling who's a half-sister to five winners, including Queues Likely (2-2 on Tapeta) and course winner Singoura; interesting to see how she goes in the market against stablemate Riviera Queen.
runners.comment: Half-sister to five winners; interesting to see how she goes in the market

runners.name: Luna Celeste
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns breeze-up 2yo who came right away with two subsequent winners on her Chelmsford 6f debut last December; signs of greenness that night and she's shortlisted in a first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Came away with two subsequent winners on her Chelmsford 6f debut last December; player

runners.name: Whazzimo
runners.spotlight: 4,600gns foal, 9,000gns yearling who's a half-sister to winners over various trips, notably Valiant (1m2f) and Culturati (6f-7f); yard 6-31 with 3yos on the AW this year and she's arguably the most interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Yard 6-31 with 3yos on the AW this year and she's arguably the most interesting newcomer

runners.name: Rianka
runners.spotlight: Third foal of a mare who won in France at up to 9.5f; every chance she'll want more of a stamina test and the market will guide on debut.
runners.comment: Every chance she'll want more of a stamina test and the market will guide on debut

runners.name: Highland Bear
runners.spotlight: Not without hope in two Newcastle starts at big prices but has been off for well over a year since; low-grade handicaps are probably next on the agenda for a yard also responsible for The Wind And Sun.
runners.comment: Off for well over a year; low-grade handicaps are probably next on the agenda

runners.name: Rivera Queen
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to two fair sprint winners (first time out, and second time out, as 2yos); finished some way off the two market principals but shaped well enough at a big price on her Redcar debut last spring; market useful after 11 months off.
runners.comment: Shaped well enough at a big price on debut last spring; market useful after 11 months off
runners.owner: D Macauliffe, A Don & J Joyce

runners.name: The Wind And Sun
runners.spotlight: 14-1 for a similar maiden at Newcastle five weeks ago, when slowly away and last of 11 runners; will want to be sharper out from stall 12 and needs a big step forward.
runners.comment: Slowly away when last of 11 on debut; will want to be sharper out from stall 12

runners.name: Zambezi Diamond
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well beaten in four runs as a 3yo (5f-1m), often not looking straightforward in the process; yard among the winners but she's hard to fancy returning from a long absence.
runners.comment: Big prices/well beaten in four runs as a 3yo; hard to fancy returning from a long break

runners.name: Dreamadera
runners.spotlight: Related to winners, including the yard's useful Excellent Times, but she started 100-1 for her turf debut 12 days ago and ran accordingly; likely best watched for now.
runners.comment: 100-1 for her turf debut 12 days ago and ran accordingly; likely best watched for now

runners.name: Moonlight Angel
runners.spotlight: Finished lame when last of seven in one run for Michael Appleby last summer; 100-1, offered little immediate promise when beaten a long way (behind Dreamadera) at Leicester for her new yard recently.
runners.comment: 100-1, little immediate promise when beaten a long way (behind Dreamadera) at Leicester

runners.name: What Fools Believe
runners.spotlight: Dam won twice over 1m for the Crisford yard and this filly appreciated the step up in trip when winning over C&D 16 days ago; that was only a 0-55 and she's 4lb higher in first-time headgear, but there could be better to come.
runners.comment: Appreciated the step up in trip when winning over C&D 16 days ago; this tougher upped 4lb

runners.name: Delinquent
runners.spotlight: Stamina on the dam's side and he looked sure to be suited by the step up in trip over C&D last time having stayed on nicely on his 7f comeback here; hit traffic that evening and this is less competitive; for a yard in fine form, he has to be considered.
runners.comment: Hit traffic latest and this is less competitive; for a yard in fine form, he's shortlisted

runners.name: Kalokalo
runners.spotlight: Made all in a small-field Yarmouth nursery off 3lb lower last autumn; looked to go too hard in front on Tapeta in one more start and may well be able to get his own way tonight, in which case he'll be dangerous.
runners.comment: May well be able to get his own way tonight, in which case he'll be dangerous

runners.name: My Champion
runners.spotlight: First run beyond sprint trips when winning an ordinary, small-field Wolverhampton handicap (8.6f) in January; met traffic upped to 9.5f latest (first-time cheekpieces; switches to a visor here) and can't be ruled out under experienced 5lb claimer Ashley Lewis.
runners.comment: Met traffic over 9.5f latest; can't be ruled out under experienced claimer Ashley Lewis

runners.name: Safe Harbor
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to a Wolverhampton 8.6f winner; showed enough in three 7f runs in the winter to suggest she'll be competitive now handicapping at a sensible level; back from a break, though, and needs to settle better than when last seen.
runners.comment: Enough in three 7f runs last winter to suggest she'll be competitive now handicapping

runners.name: Marry The Night
runners.spotlight: Record over C&D currently reads 22115112; didn't look to have any excuses in a race run to suit here three weeks ago and it's possible he's reached his ceiling mark now, but he's ultra consistent and looks set to get involved once again.
runners.comment: Record over C&D reads 22115112; ultra consistent and looks set to get involved once again

runners.name: King Of York
runners.spotlight: Five-time 7f winner here (well suited by a good test over that trip) who's currently 0-17 over C&D; patchy since a win here off 1lb lower in February and others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Five-time 7f winner here who's currently 0-17 over C&D; others have more pressing claims
runners.owner: A Baker D Slack M Wilson & Partners

runners.name: Soames Forsyte
runners.spotlight: Four-time course winner, all in the winter months, latterly off this mark in January; usually misses the break and again hung left when behind Marry The Night over C&D last time; a stronger pace would help for last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: Usually misses the break/can hang left; good pace would help for last year's winning yard

runners.name: Iconic Times
runners.spotlight: Two wins for Heather Main over 6f and he hasn't always convinced when tried over 7f, although he turned in a solid effort from the front over that trip last time; stamina the concern for his new trainer, who's been among winners with her jumpers lately.
runners.comment: Stamina the concern for his new trainer, who's been among winners with her jumpers lately

runners.name: Age Of Baroque
runners.spotlight: 7f winner here for Marco Botti last summer off 2lb higher; patchy since for his current yard but the return here could help (third over C&D on his only other visit, last month).
runners.comment: Patchy for his current yard but the return here could help

runners.name: Avatar Jet
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 5yo who makes his AW debut (half-brother to a Polytrack winner) having been beaten a long way on his first run in 18 months for new connections four weeks ago; ran well to a point in that Doncaster race, though, before not seeing out 1m2f.
runners.comment: Ran well to a point at Doncaster before not seeing out 1m2f on his first run in 18 months

runners.name: Superfortress
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns yearling who was bought unraced for 5,000gns in October 2024; back to last autumn's debut levels when best of the rest behind an easy, long odds-on winner over C&D on comeback a month ago and may have more to offer now handicapping.
runners.comment: Back to last autumn's debut level over C&D latest; may have more to offer now handicapping

runners.name: Goldie Trickett
runners.spotlight: Turned in four moderate efforts for William Haggas at shortish prices last year; perhaps the switch to the trainer of her Group 1-winning sister Saffron Beach and the fitting of a first-time hood will do the job.
runners.comment: Four moderate efforts for William Haggas at shortish prices last year; can do better

runners.name: Johnjay
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard and didn't see it out on his hurdling debut last month; two wins for Roger Teal came in the spring but it's hard to find many other positives on this Tapeta debut in a first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Two wins for Roger Teal came in the spring but it's hard to find many other positives

runners.name: Give Me The Night
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who can pull hard (wore a hood once) and has finished tamely in four runs over tonight's trip; entitled to have come on from his recent Kempton comeback but will need to settle better.
runners.comment: Regressive maiden who can pull hard; will need to settle better

runners.name: Plutarch
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns yearling who went for just 5,000gns as a 3yo; fair debut last winter but well held in two runs since (1m2f-1m4f) and the handicapper seems to have erred on the side of caution; Megan Smith takes just a second ride under rules.
runners.comment: Handicapper seems to have erred on side of caution; Megan Smith takes just a second ride

runners.name: Verzain
runners.spotlight: Gave solid kick from handy position to triumph this track last outing. Might go on with it now.
runners.owner: B Cannon, P D Mullen Et Al

runners.name: Ole Go
runners.spotlight: Prominent and showed plenty of ticker on a soft deck to just go under in thrilling finish in the Without Fear Stakes here at his second race start. Maps to get a sweet run. Stripping fitter and hard to toss.
runners.owner: Mrs T L Meekin, C A Meekin & C J Swadling

runners.name: Wonderful Sky
runners.spotlight: Ridden positively and showed plenty of mettle to win at Pakenham at her second race start. Awkwardly drawn this time. Sharper now and can make her presence felt again if things pan out.
runners.owner: Mrs K W Fenton-Ellis MNZM & R Lau

runners.name: Czech Out
runners.spotlight: Posted some decent efforts from three appearances. Settled at rear, produced a grinding charge into the placings this venue at second run back. Improver, fitter third-up. Stripping fitter and can play a role.
runners.owner: M D Sheehan, T A Hams Et Al

runners.name: Aragog
runners.spotlight: Game effort and narrowly prevailed at Geelong after blowing in the market at his second race start. Switching to 1200m. Tackling stronger line-up.

runners.name: Vandaross
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in soft going here. Could add some value to exotics.
runners.owner: M J Seyers, J S Hylan Et Al

runners.name: Gimonnet
runners.spotlight: Reasonable debut performance when battled to line here when drifting in the betting. Could get into the placings.

runners.name: Autumn Mystery
runners.spotlight: Sent out $101 when never got into the race at Caulfield over this distance. Among the dangers with even luck.
runners.owner: N J Thompson & Mrs D A Thompson

runners.name: McWoody
runners.spotlight: Third outing for new trainer/ this region. Chasing hat-trick. Annexed the 3yo (Bm64) at Sandown-Lakeside 25 Mar. Won in decisive fashion this venue.
runners.owner: Ms R A McDonald, Skywood Racing Et Al

runners.name: Wigmore
runners.spotlight: Let-up (49-days). Ex C E Myers in New Zealand. NZer having first run across the ditch. Market watch advised.
runners.owner: OTI Wigmore, P G Bruce Et Al

runners.name: Matahga
runners.spotlight: Did little in soft going here. First real test of stamina here. Very hard to enthuse.

runners.name: Engine Of War
runners.spotlight: Sent out $5 when unable to threaten at Caulfield in second run from spell. All the better and will take stopping in this company.
runners.owner: MyRacehorse No 1, D E Gwynne Et Al

runners.name: Rousing Refrain
runners.spotlight: Won the Hcp (58) at Balaklava two back 1 Apr. Went home tamely on a soft deck this venue at $71. First real test of stamina here. Goes into wider exotics.
runners.owner: Ms K Harrison, G W Pfeiler Et Al

runners.name: Flying Brant
runners.spotlight: Just missed at Morphettville 28 Mar. Battled home on a soft deck this venue. Can lead or take a sit. First real test of stamina here.
runners.owner: Campolina Pty Ltd, 84/85 Premiership Et Al

runners.name: Boomeroo
runners.spotlight: Broke maiden at Sale 29 Mar. Sent out $26 when unable to threaten on a soft deck this venue. First real test of stamina here. Respect on previous sound form.

runners.name: Big Rita June
runners.spotlight: Failed to threaten on a soft deck this venue as a despised outsider. Stamina on trial here. Needs a form spike.
runners.owner: L B Grantham, Ms E G Grantham Et Al

runners.name: Angling Angel
runners.spotlight: Just missed at Sale 29 Mar. Never a threat after showing no early speed from the barriers here. Stamina on trial here. Can fill the minors here.
runners.owner: B Sinclair, G D Sutton & I C Jones

runners.name: Impulsive Reaction
runners.spotlight: Out of the money in soft going at Cranbourne last outing when in the market. Others with greater appeal.

runners.name: Starfleet Command
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Tame to line in small field at Sandown-Lakeside at second run back. First real test of stamina here. Others are packing more punch.
runners.owner: Australian Thoroughbred Bloodstock Pty Ltd, D Wagner et al

races.race_name: Sportsbet Queen Of The South Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: On Display
runners.spotlight: Last assignment this trip when stationed midfield and never landed a blow at Caulfield 11 Apr. Drawn well. Key player.
runners.owner: Sheamus Mills Bloodstock P/L, Newton T'Breds Racing Et Al

runners.name: Blindedbythelight
runners.spotlight: Given time to freshen up after 1st-up run. Close placing in the Summoned Stakes at Caulfield. Trailed speed, kept coming but winner too strong at Rosehill at resumption. Fitter second-up.

runners.name: Cilacap
runners.spotlight: Handily placed and gave her all at Caulfield over this distance after a market ease. Decent alley. Form good enough for this.
runners.owner: Echo Beach Bloodstock Pty Ltd & B N Singer

runners.name: Rumbled Again
runners.spotlight: Experienced campaigner. Never sighted at resumption at Caulfield. Will be fitter second-up. Could give some cheek at odds.
runners.owner: T J Ford, M P Trotter Et Al

runners.name: Mission Of Love
runners.spotlight: First-up (47-weeks). Void of early speed and needs the breaks to fall her way. First time around Morphettville. Wait until she shows something.
runners.owner: J G Symons, P Morley Et Al

runners.name: Private Legacy
runners.spotlight: Never a threat after showing no early speed from the barriers here at resumption. Others with stronger claims.
runners.owner: G D Hickman, A P Carney Et Al

runners.name: Wild Imagination
runners.spotlight: Made running in small field but gave away in drive 5.1 len last at Warracknabeal. Others more persuasive.
runners.owner: D V Page, Mrs R A Page Et Al

runners.name: Terilee
runners.spotlight: Made short work of the small field in the 3yo+ F&M (Bm78) at Caulfield at this trip. Among the dangers.
runners.owner: M C Kent, T J Eastwood Et Al

runners.name: World's My Oyster
runners.spotlight: Won a 1600m Benchmark 78 at Morphettville two back. Raced in back half and was never in a position to mount a serious challenge this track. Respect on previous sound form.

runners.name: Terrestar
runners.spotlight: Last win was 17 Apr 2024. No match but still very brave in defeat in 3yo+ F&M (Bm74) at Caulfield second-up. First time around Morphettville. Tackling stronger line-up.

runners.name: Teardrop Rotation
runners.spotlight: Did little on a soft deck here. Has a tricky draw. Appreciates a dry track but has plenty to find.
runners.owner: C M Bieg, C F Sherratt Et Al

runners.name: Moonlight Circus
runners.spotlight: Ran on late from the back at Caulfield first up. Fitter second-up. Could get into the placings.
runners.owner: Cross Park, L C Mugridge Et Al

runners.name: Brave Miss
runners.spotlight: Got back, cut loose late to fill the minors at Caulfield first up. First time around Morphettville. Can't enthuse.
runners.owner: Mrs B K Saunders, B A E Saunders & Mrs R R K Chidley

runners.name: Mystic Wonder
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Strong win over 1550m (Bm66) at Morphettville Parks. Others with more appeal.
runners.owner: Ms J Heaney, R M Warnock Et Al

runners.name: Isuspectjeu
runners.spotlight: Let-up (63-days). Settled back and didn't figure over a longer test in the Lord Reims Stakes this venue. Others rate stronger.
runners.owner: Ms A M Lear & M P Cornell

runners.name: Maisy
runners.spotlight: Freshened. In the thick of it at Flemington two back 28 Feb. Comfortably beaten in soft going at Rosehill at second run back. Has an adverse draw.

runners.name: Brown Nose Day Gal
runners.spotlight: Won from just off the pace-setters at Oakbank. Has a tricky draw and up in weight. Harder now.

runners.name: Niance
runners.spotlight: Resuming (28-weeks). Hits straps quickly. Highly fancied for a winning return.
runners.owner: Ace Bloodstock Racing Pty Ltd, Ace Club Et Al

runners.name: New York Lustre
runners.spotlight: Resuming (25-weeks). Won first-up at Moonee Valley last campaign. Drawn to effect. Capable of figuring again fresh in this line-up.

runners.name: Watchme Win
runners.spotlight: Narrow miss in the R N Irwin Stakes in soft going here 11 Apr. Strips fitter second run back. Could get into the placings.
runners.owner: A J Hehir, D W Rasheed Et Al

runners.name: Recommendation
runners.spotlight: Long time since last saluted. Knuckled down for third at Ballarat when off an extended spell as a market drifter. Others are packing more punch.
runners.owner: N S Psaltis, Mrs G C Psaltis Et Al

runners.name: Coleman
runners.spotlight: Third run now in new stable/region. Ridden cold and never in the picture at Ballarat at first run back. Will be fitter second-up. Awkwardly drawn and rising in weight.
runners.owner: Mrs S Cummings, Mrs J Groves Et Al

runners.name: Pisanello
runners.spotlight: Third run now in new stable/region. Settled back and didn't figure in soft going at second run back in the R N Irwin Stakes this venue. Lift needed on last effort.

runners.name: Power Beau
runners.spotlight: Now 4 weeks between runs. Sent forward and boxed on bravely in the small field for 1 len second this venue. Draws to do no work. One for exotics.

runners.name: Taunting
runners.spotlight: Resuming (51-weeks). Ex Michael Hickmott in Victoria. Proven record wet/track. Form sound at this course and journey.
runners.owner: M Hickmott, M Pilkington Et Al

runners.name: Losesomewinmore
runners.spotlight: Resuming (38-weeks). Brought to race fitness with 3 trials and proven here. Could add some value to exotics.

runners.name: Zoupurring
runners.spotlight: Stuck tough, holding off challengers Hills Railway Stakes at Oakbank at second run back at $11. Breaks from a low barrier. Could give some cheek at odds.
runners.owner: Bennett Racing Pty Ltd, R Menchise Et Al

runners.name: Lingani
runners.spotlight: Ran on late from the back at Oakbank over this trip. Since trialled at Balaklava 16 Apr. Can't enthuse.
runners.owner: G G Tully, T K Thompson Et Al

runners.name: Grand Larceny
runners.spotlight: First-up (21-weeks). In the minors at Moonee Valley first-up last campaign. Could get into the placings fresh in this line-up.
runners.owner: Werrett Bloodstock, Madden Bloodstock Et Al

runners.name: Dual Pressure
runners.spotlight: First-up (20-weeks). Went close at Morphettville two back 22 Nov. Keener on others.
runners.owner: A Bain, ABR Thoroughbred Race Club No 1 Et Al

runners.name: Sixteen Reasons
runners.spotlight: First-up (27-weeks). Given a good tune up with 3 trials and proven here. Awkwardly drawn. This doesn't look easy.
runners.owner: M A Crawford, Adelaide Cricket Boys Et Al

runners.name: Thunder Shoc
runners.spotlight: Got back and wasn't totally disgraced at Oakbank over this trip. Others have more zip.
runners.owner: R I Chapman, P A Crawford Et Al

runners.name: Mic Drop
runners.spotlight: Close placing in the Manihi Classic at Morphettville. Not disgraced at Oakbank at this distance. Tactical speed, inside draw. Could get into the placings.
runners.owner: S A Dyson, C Tanzer Et Al

runners.name: Winning Revolution
runners.spotlight: Settled midfield, let down really well at Oakbank over this trip. Leaning towards others.
runners.owner: Ms D K Hamlin & G G Brown

runners.name: Enuff Seduction
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Got back and hit line with a full head of steam at Morphettville Parks. Tackling stronger line-up.
runners.owner: B W Flitter, B J Ives Et Al

runners.name: Swiftie Harriet
runners.spotlight: Wore them down late here first up. Stiffer challenge here.
runners.owner: T J Doudle, S D Bland Et Al

runners.name: Streetcar Fury
