runners.spotlight: Knuckled down well winning the (Bm56) under a big impost at Murray Bridge Gh. Tackling stronger company.
runners.owner: S R Doyle, Ocean Street Racing Et Al

runners.name: Legacy Bound
runners.spotlight: Freshened. Won 3yo Hcp Flemington 7 Mar. Battled home in rain affected ground at Flemington at second run back. Drawn to get a conservative run.

runners.name: Inkaruna
runners.spotlight: Been supported and where there's smoke there's fire. Close placing in the Birthday Card Stakes at Rosehill. Settled back and didn't figure in choppy conditions at this distance in the P J Bell Stakes at Randwick. Can put last run behind her.

runners.name: Tycoon Star
runners.spotlight: Terrific effort when just shaded on a soft deck this track 11 Apr. Can challenge.
runners.owner: Lindsay Park Bloodstock, Yu Long Investments Et Al

runners.name: Thanks Gorgeous
runners.spotlight: Now 4 weeks between runs. Got up in final strides at Flemington. New to Morphettville. Can't enthuse.
runners.owner: Mrs I P Kavanagh & Mrs C L Kavanagh

runners.name: Ferivia
runners.spotlight: Just wacked away without threatening over 1200m F&M (Bm100) at Caulfield in second run from spell. Awkwardly drawn. Others with more appeal.

runners.name: Alpha Sofie
runners.spotlight: Now 5 weeks between runs. Ran on late from the back at Caulfield at this distance. Can fill the minors here.
runners.owner: Murphy Syndicate, Ms L M Lord Et Al

runners.name: Space Rider
runners.spotlight: Now 5 weeks between runs. Raced in back half and was never in a position to mount a serious challenge at Rosehill. Awkwardly drawn. Leaning towards others.
runners.owner: Myracehorse No 1, Aston Bloodstock Et Al

runners.name: Torsheen
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Responded well from back half to get the prize at Mornington at this trip. Shapes as a rough place prospect.

runners.name: Signature Scent
runners.spotlight: Annexed the 3yo (Bm72) at Morphettville 28 Mar. Fought bravely to the wire in the Redelva Stakes this track. Could get into the placings.

runners.name: Rosberg
runners.spotlight: Let-up (63-days). Got back and failed to make a meaningful impression at Caulfield first up. One for exotics fresh in this line-up.
runners.owner: Mrs J R McGrath, D Alysandratos Et Al

runners.name: Extragalactic
runners.spotlight: Aiming for hat-trick. Let down with a fine turn of speed from the back half to win at second run back. Can fill the minors here.
runners.owner: T W Hennessy, Choochoo Racing Et Al

runners.name: Sir Myka
runners.spotlight: Determined winner at Oakbank. Sterner task awaits.
runners.owner: T J Doudle, S D Bland Et Al

runners.name: Virtual Illusion
runners.spotlight: Made late rally from the back here second-up. Usually gets back. Others more persuasive.
runners.owner: R R Jolly, Gts Racing Et Al

runners.name: Mathematician
runners.spotlight: Snagged back and worked home okay in Redelva Stakes here last start. Very hard to enthuse.
runners.owner: Clarken Bloodstock, T Needham Et Al

runners.name: Lawborough
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Led, soon under pressure and dropped off at Flemington 4 weeks back. Coming back in distance. Looking elsewhere.
runners.owner: SZ Racing No 1, M C Wade Et Al

runners.name: Getta Good Feeling
runners.spotlight: Freshened. Highly progressive winning 4 from 9. Determined winner at Caulfield in second run from spell at prohibited odds. All the better and form good enough for this.

runners.name: Strictly Business
runners.spotlight: Never a threat after showing no early speed from the barriers at Ballarat in second run from spell. Usually gets back. Others are packing more punch.
runners.owner: Mhumhain Racing, D Insall Et Al

runners.name: Salty Pearl
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Took out the VOBIS Platinum Guineas at Caulfield 14 Mar. Finished in hot pursuit on a soft deck at Rosehill over this trip. Placed only other visit to Morphettville.
runners.owner: Starblue Racing TLO Malice, CMR LOF Et Al

runners.name: Panova
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Sent out $12 when never got into the race in soft going at Rosehill over this trip. Usually gets back. Shapes as a rough place prospect.

runners.name: Mating Call
runners.spotlight: Wore them down late here. Stamina on trial here. Can fill the minors here.

runners.name: Freedom Flame
runners.spotlight: Prominent and went down fighting in soft going in the Open company this venue. Not fancied.
runners.owner: B H Perks & Ms A M Clough

runners.name: Bona Sforza
runners.spotlight: Stakes winning NZ filly. Won the 11 Apr. Stamina on trial here. Could give some cheek at odds.
runners.owner: F & S Finnegan, A Boyce Et Al

runners.name: Paltrow Miss
runners.spotlight: Merit in both Australian runs. Ran home into the minors 3yo F Hcp at Caulfield in second run from spell. First real test of stamina here. Sharper now and has some claims.
runners.owner: A Kheir, B J O'Brien Et Al

runners.name: Stung
runners.spotlight: Ridden cold and never in the picture this track. First real test of stamina here. Very hard to enthuse.
runners.owner: R & C Legh Racing P/L, B & C Tbreds P/L & Pulitano Tbreds

runners.name: Kazaru
runners.spotlight: Won a 2100m Open at Launceston two back. Started $7 when battled home in Open company in soft going this venue. Can't enthuse.
runners.owner: O T I Racing, A M Kerr Et Al

runners.name: Naraghi
runners.spotlight: Dictated speed, boxed away in the Auraria Stakes this track. First real test of stamina here. Looking elsewhere.
runners.owner: L Howley, C W Kwok Et Al

runners.name: Just Kick
runners.spotlight: Left in winner's wake but ran on soundly for 2.3 len third in the small field at Caulfield. One for exotics.
runners.owner: Sparta Racing, C W Kwok Et Al

runners.name: Synthesise
runners.spotlight: Failed to flatter here. First real test of stamina here. Leaning towards others.
runners.owner: P R Gunner, D R Wagner & S R Wagner

runners.name: Cushioned
runners.spotlight: Won in decisive fashion at Pakenham over this distance. Just out of her maiden and facing tougher challenge here.

runners.name: Oui Oui Ma Cherie
runners.spotlight: Failed to threaten on a soft deck this track as an odds drifter. Unplaced from 5 starts here. Others with greater appeal.
runners.owner: S P Gower, P J Treloar Et Al

runners.name: Boomeroo
runners.spotlight: Went home tamely in soft going here at $26. First real test of stamina here. Looking elsewhere.

runners.name: Savitri
runners.spotlight: On target for hat-trick. Made short work of the small field in the 3yo F Hcp at Caulfield. Stamina on trial here. Can fill the minors here.

runners.name: Charm Stone
runners.spotlight: Freshened after resuming from a break. On pace and courageous in defeat at Caulfield first up.
runners.owner: Sheamus Mills Bloodstock Ladies, Newton Ladies Et Al

runners.name: Skybird
runners.spotlight: Void of early speed but she let down strongly Open company at Randwick at second run back.
runners.owner: M J Scalzo, D J Scalzo Et Al

runners.name: My Gladiola
runners.spotlight: Fresh. Last at turn, ran on late at Flemington.
runners.owner: Redgum Racing Pty Ltd Synd, C A Ahrens Et Al

runners.name: Benedetta
runners.spotlight: 5 weeks since last run. Made late rally from the back at Caulfield over this trip.
runners.owner: Eales Racing Pty Ltd , B M Wilson Et Al

runners.name: Generosity
runners.spotlight: Now 5 weeks between runs. Really good run coming from well back in the Kia Ora Galaxy at Rosehill second-up as an odds drifter.

runners.name: Point Barrow
runners.spotlight: Freshened. Closed with conviction from back half to win at Caulfield in second run from spell.
runners.owner: Mrs B M McClure, R McClure Et Al

runners.name: Super Smink
runners.spotlight: Ridden cold and let down powerfully this track first up.
runners.owner: Mrs C L Morton, McHams Et Al

runners.name: Bridal Waltz
runners.spotlight: 5 weeks since last run. Handy and boxed on solidly at Rosehill in second run from spell.

runners.name: Flying For Fun
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced at Randwick in second run from spell.
runners.owner: M W O'Keeffe & Ms V J King

runners.name: Stretan Angel
runners.spotlight: No threat to winner but ran on for the minors on a soft deck here at first run back when in the market.
runners.owner: J M Maclean, Going Again Syndicate Et Al

runners.name: Bossy Benita
runners.spotlight: The mare hasn't been far away at last two runs including when kept on evenly at Caulfield. Coming back in distance.
runners.owner: M J M Racing & Bloodstock Pty Ltd, D G Benyon Et Al

runners.name: Geegees Mistruth
runners.spotlight: Handily placed and gave her all at Ballarat.

runners.name: Ameena
runners.spotlight: First-up (25-weeks). Wait until she shows something.

races.race_name: bet365 Novices' Championship Final Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Minella Yoga
runners.spotlight: Improving 4yo whose record is 3-4; bought for £360,000 after sole Irish hurdles start; ran well in Grade 2 for sole defeat; easily justified favouritism at Taunton (2m, good) last time; major player.
runners.comment: Improving 4yo whose record is 3-4; scored easily at Taunton last time; major contender

runners.name: Cosmic Connection
runners.spotlight: Scored in tidy fashion at Chepstow (2m, good to soft; handicap debut) last time, taking rules record to 2-3, and is probably still well treated even after a 6lb rise; improving.
runners.comment: Scored in tidy fashion at Chepstow (handicap debut) last time, taking rules record to 2-3

runners.name: Kadastral
runners.spotlight: Non-stayer over 2m4f last time; solid record at 2m otherwise, including two efforts behind smart Sober Glory; this Skelton runner likely still has more to offer; respected handicap debutant.
runners.comment: Has a solid record at 2m and likely still has more to offer; respected handicap debutant

runners.name: Kocktail Bleu
runners.spotlight: Proving consistent and, following a good effort in a Grade 2, he made all at Newbury (2m, good to soft) back down in class last time; far from sure to get an easy lead in this bigger field, however.
runners.comment: Made all at Newbury most recently but is far from sure to get an easy lead in this field

runners.name: Secret Sniper
runners.spotlight: No match for Minella Yoga at Taunton, taking record to 0-6; however, he's related to several winners for his connections and may have potential off his opening mark; one to consider.
runners.comment: Related to several winners for his connections; may have potential off his opening mark

runners.name: Legal Weapon
runners.spotlight: Reasonably consistent but is 0-5 over hurdles, finishing fourth to Wilstar at Warwick most recently; chance of finding the necessary progress largely depends on the effects of first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Chance of finding the necessary progress largely depends on the effects of tongue-tie

runners.name: Way Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Prevailed in second-time hood at Taunton (2m, good) most recently, adding to his Flat win, and may have more to offer in the retained headgear; in deeper waters today but could go well.
runners.comment: Prevailed in second-time hood at Taunton and may have more to offer in this headgear

runners.name: Wilstar
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to Douvan and Jonbon; not in their league but is progressive and has won his last two starts, latest at Warwick (2m, good to soft); enters calculations on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well bred; progressive and has won last two starts; enters calculations on handicap debut

runners.name: Kapitein Kool
runners.spotlight: Dual novice winner but came up well short at Newbury (2m3f) on handicap debut most recently and can be opposed in this competitive field.
runners.comment: Came up well short on handicap debut last time; can be opposed

runners.name: Dutch Corner
runners.spotlight: Posted consistent efforts prior to ready win in Newton Abbot maiden (2m2f, good to soft) last time; may improve further and can't be dismissed, even though Harry Skelton prefers Kadastral.
runners.comment: Scored readily at Newton Abbot last time and may improve further; yard also runs Kadastral

runners.name: Stanners Glen
runners.spotlight: Made all in Wincanton maiden (1m7f, good) in latest hurdles attempt, adding to his point/bumper wins and taking overall record to 3-6; however, this scenario is much more demanding.
runners.comment: Made all at Wincanton most recently but this scenario is more demanding

runners.name: Race To Base
runners.spotlight: Had wind surgery since failing to convince over 2m4f in the EBF Final on this track last time on stable debut; return to 2m looks a plus but he's still seeking first rules win away from Hereford.
runners.comment: Return to 2m looks a plus but he's still seeking first rules win away from Hereford

runners.name: Last Round
runners.spotlight: Finished behind Kadastral on penultimate start but may fare better in this rematch, having scored comfortably in Fontwell maiden (2m1f, good to soft) latest; further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Scored comfortably in Fontwell maiden last time; further progress is possible

runners.name: Bespoke Tailor
runners.spotlight: Has consistent hurdles form; however, his chance of gaining a first success in this sphere largely depends on how well he responds to a physical tweak (wind surgery since last run).
runners.comment: Chance of gaining a first hurdles win depends on how well he responds to wind surgery

runners.name: Desertmore News
runners.spotlight: Made all in small field at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) last time, taking hurdles record to 2-3; however, he faces a stiffer task in this deep handicap with competition for the lead.
runners.comment: Made all in small field at Sedgefield last time; faces stiffer task in this deep handicap

runners.name: Talakan
runners.spotlight: Flat winner in France and knocking at the door over hurdles for new yard, second to Wilstar at Warwick (2m) most recently; possibilities, provided he shows some progress on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Knocking at the door; possibilities, provided he shows progress on handicap debut

runners.name: Love You Back
runners.spotlight: Flat winner whose hurdles form comprises a couple of second-place finishes and a late fall (beaten at the time); even off bottom weight, this looks a difficult task upped in class.
runners.comment: Even off bottom weight, this looks a difficult task upped in class

runners.name: Ambiente Friendly
runners.spotlight: Showed smart Flat form as a 3yo; managed to win in December but has otherwise broadly been too free for his own good since switched to hurdles; ideally needs to settle better.
runners.comment: Broadly too free for his own good since switched to hurdles; needs to settle better

races.race_name: Caffreys Black Stout King Richard III Cup Handicap

runners.name: Zoulu Chief
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably at Lingfield in January when bidding for a 6f AW double; 8lb higher than when second at Newmarket (7f) on last turf attempt but he's proved consistent since being gelded and his first two handicap wins were on good to firm; in the mix.
runners.comment: Reliable type who is very effective on fast ground and he's respected back from a break

runners.name: Betsen
runners.spotlight: Won on stable debut at Southwell (6f, Tapeta) in November and he's posted some solid efforts since then; hard to rule out another good run but this is a step back up in grade and sole turf win was 19 months ago.
runners.comment: In good form on AW but this is a step back up in grade and his sole turf win was in 2024

runners.name: Berkshire Whisper
runners.spotlight: Completed an AW hat-trick when scoring on his handicap debut at Newcastle (6f) last April; held in six subsequent runs but he went close off this mark at Southwell in November and was a creditable third at Newcastle three weeks ago; now returns to turf but he ran well in a fast-ground Listed event last spring and he's still unexposed in this sphere; in the mix.
runners.comment: Placed off this mark at Newcastle latest and he's still unexposed on turf; in the mix
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Strike Red
runners.spotlight: On lengthy losing but he's a smart handicapper on his day and he returned from five months off with a good third at Newcastle (6f, AW) two weeks ago; should benefit from that run and he remains well treated on his second in the Stewards' Cup last August; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Second in Stewards' Cup last summer and runner-up at Newcastle 12 days ago; respected

runners.name: Fast Track Harry
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner who made it 2-2 on AW when completing a double at Newcastle (6f; off 4lb lower) last month; didn't fire in his hat-trick bid at Kempton but he still has potential and is not ruled out in suitable conditions back on turf.
runners.comment: Held in hat-trick bid at Kempton but he still has potential and is not ruled back on turf

runners.name: Dark Cloud Rising
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who ended his 2025 campaign with a success and creditable effort over 5f at Newcastle (AW); was as good as ever in those two runs but he was well below form on his reappearance at Pontefract (5f, good to soft) 18 days ago; may have needed that run but he needs more progress to defy this mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner who scored on AW in November but he needs more progress to defy this mark

runners.name: Roman Dragon
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on his best form last season but he's not easy to predict and has been up and down during another spell in Bahrain since December; comes with risks attached back in Britain and most of his wins have been at Chester; tongue-tie worn last time is removed.
runners.comment: Chester specialist who is not easy to predict and others are preferred

runners.name: Ten Pounds
runners.spotlight: Record of 5-10 for Harry Charlton and his last win was in a conditions event at Kempton (6f, AW) in October; ran without his declared tongue-tie when fourth on stable debut at Southwell in January but he finished down the field in another Class 2 handicap at Newcastle two weeks later; conditions should be fine on this return to turf but he needs to step up again after another break.
runners.comment: Record of 5-12 but he was down the field at Newcastle last time; needs more after a break

runners.name: Annaf
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a game win in Group 3 at Ascot (6f, soft) in October; repeatedly met trouble when only seventh in Saudi Arabia (a race he'd won two years earlier) in February and things didn't really go his way again in a Listed race at Doncaster last month; has possibilities on this drop back in class and his last handicap win was off 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner over 6f last autumn and he has possibilities back in a handicap

runners.name: Prince Of India
runners.spotlight: Won four times during a progressive campaign last year but he's come up short in Listed/Group 3 events this spring; seems ideally suited by fast ground but he's still 4lb higher than for his last win and has some work to do back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Four wins last year but his progress seems to have flattened out; others preferred

runners.name: Fivethousandtoone
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner who has finished close up on Tapeta (5f/6f) in his last three runs; record of 1-26 on turf but he's on a lower mark in this sphere and he ran well in the Great St Wilfrid at Ripon (6f, good to firm) last summer; dangerous.
runners.comment: 1-26 on turf but he's been in good form on AW and he's on lower mark back in this sphere

runners.name: Kylian
runners.spotlight: Listed winner (5f/6f) for his previous yards and he's run respectably in two 5f handicaps for his new trainer this spring; needs to find some extra spark but he's edging down the weights and this step back up in trip looks a good move.
runners.comment: Fair efforts in two runs for new yard and he could be dangerous back up in trip
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xii & Mrs G Cowell

runners.name: Russet Gold
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins for Roger Varian but latest was in June 2023 and he was quiet last season; returns for new yard after wind surgery but he has a mixed record when fresh and this is highly competitive; down the list.
runners.comment: Returns after wind surgery but has mixed record when fresh and his last win was in 2023

races.race_name: bet365 Oaksey Chase (Grade 2) (For The Menorah Challenge Trophy) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Matterhorn
runners.spotlight: Chase record is 5-11, the most recent win at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) last spring; seen only twice since but ran well in a notable handicap at Plumpton (2m1f) the latest time; possibilities at the weights, provided he stays the new trip.
runners.comment: Ran well in notable handicap at Plumpton last time; possibilities if he stays the new trip

runners.name: Doyen Quest
runners.spotlight: Useful novice chaser whose form features second-place finishes in 3m Grade 2 events at Sandown and Windsor; came up rather short in Grade 1 at Aintree last time but looks one of the main contenders returned to this level.
runners.comment: Useful novice chaser who has Grade 2 form at Sandown and Windsor; one of the main players
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late N W Lake

runners.name: Blow Your Wad
runners.spotlight: Has shown good form for new stable, mostly in handicaps, including at the Cheltenham Festival (3m1f); defied top weight at Chepstow (2m3f, good to soft) most recently, looking better than ever; leading chance at the weights in his bid for a second Grade 2 success.
runners.comment: Defied top weight in Chepstow handicap last time, looking better than ever; leading claims

runners.name: Etalon
runners.spotlight: Form since wearing cheekpieces includes good performances (career-best form) at Ascot, winning over 2m3f then third over 2m5f (good to soft and soft); may rebound from a lesser effort last time but Harry Skelton prefers stablemate Doyen Quest.
runners.comment: May rebound from a lesser effort last time but Harry Skelton prefers Doyen Quest

runners.name: Teddy Blue
runners.spotlight: Has registered a couple of useful handicap wins this season, including at Kempton (2m4f, good) on penultimate start; faces a stiffer task conceding weight all round in this grade, with 4lb penalty incurred.
runners.comment: Two handicap wins this term but faces a stiffer task under 4lb penalty in this grade

runners.name: Mahons Glory
runners.spotlight: Broadly consistent in handicaps for current yard and ran respectably in the Topham at Aintree (2m5f) most recently; made all, despite jumping left, at Huntingdon (2m4f; also on good to soft) the time before; this is a harder task.
runners.comment: Consistent in handicaps for current yard but faces a harder task upped in class
runners.owner: C Nightingale & Hold My Beer Partnership

runners.name: Sarab Star
runners.spotlight: His latest Newcastle defeat isn't the run to judge him on and he had been progressing well beforehand; no problem with the return to turf and stepping back up to 7f will be of benefit; interesting with cheekpieces now given a go.
runners.comment: Below par over 6f last time but on the up over 7f beforehand; now tries cheekpieces

runners.name: Cosi Bello
runners.spotlight: Began his career with two 7f AW wins and he was a fine second at Chester on his handicap debut last June (7f, good); fourth of 17 in a 1m handicap at Ascot when last seen nine months ago, a run that can be marked up as he raced away from the first three home; still has potential and he returns with his stable back in better form.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo; stable in better form now and he is one to consider after nine months off

runners.name: Khafiz
runners.spotlight: Began 2025 with 7f novice wins at Kempton and Doncaster; ended his 3yo campaign with a close third at Doncaster (7f, soft) 183 days ago; sold out of Roger Varian's stable for 85,000gns in October; still has low mileage, is yet to run a poor race when wearing a hood and is starting out for a trainer in great form; lots to like.
runners.comment: Conditions should suit and he has good record in a hood; new stable going well at present

runners.name: Korker
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at Doncaster on his return but he wasn't well drawn and has a modest record fresh; stepping up to 7f will give him more time to claw back the ground he's likely to forfeit at the start; on a dangerous mark and he's a lively outsider, for all his losing run stretches back to October 2023.
runners.comment: Doncaster return easy to excuse; on dangerous mark and 7f looks an avenue worth exploring
runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing,Mrs Burke & C Wood

runners.name: Great Acclaim
runners.spotlight: Strong handicap form over 7f last summer, winning at Goodwood and Chepstow as well as finishing second in a 20-runner event at Ascot (all when wearing blinkers); still feasibly weighted on that form and he went without headgear when down the field in the Lincoln; the blinkers return today and he's a leading contender.
runners.comment: Strong 7f handicap form in blinkers in 2025; headgear absent for return but back on today

runners.name: The Angel King
runners.spotlight: The winner of two of his six starts in France, both over 7f on ground slower than good; sold for 36,000euros in February; makes his stable debut in a competitive race.
runners.comment: Two 7f wins on slower ground in France; sold 36,000euros since latest run; others safer

runners.name: Caburn
runners.spotlight: Won a valuable pot as a 2yo; slow to come to hand as a 3yo but he changed yards last summer and made an impressive winning return from a gelding operation last September (6f, good to soft; cheekpieces on for the first time); fine reappearance when third in the Listed Cammidge Trophy at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) four weeks ago; raised 3lb for his troubles and still has to prove his stamina for 7f.
runners.comment: Improved effort when 3rd in 6f Listed event four weeks ago; up 3lb and stamina not assured

runners.name: Croupier
runners.spotlight: Progressive handicapper for the Crisfords and he was last seen finishing second in the Buckingham Palace at Royal Ascot (7f, good to firm) in June 2023; sold for 20,000gns in May 2024; only dropped 3lb during his 1038-day absence; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Smart handicapper for the Crisfords but he has a 1038-day absence to defy on stable debut

runners.name: Cerulean Bay
runners.spotlight: Had strong handicap form over 1m in the second half of 2025, finishing second at York's Ebor meeting (good to firm) before recording back-to-back wins at Goodwood (good to soft and good); ran well for third in a Nottingham Listed race when last seen 178 days ago; only seventh in this race 12 months ago and he's 4lb higher today.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 in fine form at 1m; may come on for this first start since October

runners.name: Darkness
runners.spotlight: Came good at Goodwood last August (7f, good to firm), running on strongly to bag a Class 2 handicap; faced a stiff task in a Thirsk conditions event on his reappearance two weeks ago; opposable with his usual visor remaining absent.
runners.comment: Stiff task on his return and finished well held; the absent visor remains a concern

runners.name: Marshman
runners.spotlight: Close third in Listed company at Wolverhampton last month on his first attempt at 7f and he wasn't beaten for a lack of stamina; not at his best in a hot sprint handicap at Newcastle on Good Friday; no surprise to see him bounce back with a big run.
runners.comment: Unexposed at 7f; better than he showed at Newcastle three weeks ago; not ruled out
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 2 & Mrs E Burke

runners.name: Henlein
runners.spotlight: Finished second in a Group 1 in Australia in October 2024 (1m2f, good; visored first time); well short of his best in two runs in early 2025 and has since been gelded and transferred to Britain; had a wind op prior to this first start for Kevin Ryan; no easy task under top weight.
runners.comment: Ex-Australian; Group 1 placed; absent 420 days; looks to face stiff task on British debut
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Jonbon
runners.spotlight: Multiple Grade 1 winner whose excellent record at Sandown includes form figures of 112 in this contest; perhaps ideally needs further now, having been upped to 2m5f for the second leg of this season's Ascot double, then posted a Ryanair effort (2m4f) that took his Cheltenham Festival form figures to 2222; leading player provided the drop back in trip doesn't backfire.
runners.comment: Multiple Grade 1 winner; good record at Sandown includes form figures of 112 in this race

runners.name: Solness
runners.spotlight: Smart front-runner whose Grade 1 form features three wins at Leopardstown (2m1f) in 2024/2025 and neck second, showing career-best form, at Aintree (2m4f, good to soft) on latest start; one of the main contenders back down in distance.
runners.comment: Close second in similar event at Aintree last time, showing career-best form; major player

runners.name: Thistle Ask
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has shown excellent progress over fences and whose record for current stable reads 11112, the last two wins in Grade 2 handicaps; collared by Jonbon in soft-ground Grade 1 at Ascot (2m1f) when last seen; may turn the tables returned to forecast ideal ground, being 5-5 on good; possibly hasn't reached his ceiling yet; respected.
runners.comment: Progressive; may reverse Ascot placings with Jonbon returned to better ground; 5-5 on good

runners.name: JPR One
runners.spotlight: Form since upped to 2m4f comprises a handicap win at Musselburgh and fourth-place finishes in Grade 1 events at Cheltenham and Aintree; return to shorter may not be an issue but he ideally needs a drop back in class.
runners.comment: Return to shorter may not be an issue but he ideally needs a drop back in class

runners.name: Mirabad
runners.spotlight: Belied his 50-1 odds with 6l success in Grade 1 novice chase at Aintree (2m, good to soft) two weeks ago; however, needs to prove he can back up that form and Harry Skelton prefers solid stablemate Thistle Ask.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he can back up the form of 50-1 success in Grade 1 novice at Aintree

runners.name: Edwardstone
runners.spotlight: Admirable veteran, albeit isn't quite the force of old and gained this season's win in substandard Grade 2 at Kempton (2m4f); also perhaps ideally suited by further than about 2m nowadays; beaten twice in this race.
runners.comment: Admirable veteran but perhaps needs further now; beaten twice in this contest

runners.name: Brookie
runners.spotlight: Has proved difficult to place since last win in March 2025 and form has taken a downturn of late; has the worst chance at the weights back up in class.
runners.comment: Difficult to place and has the worst chance at the weights back up in class

races.race_name: bet365 Gold Cup Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Rock My Way
runners.spotlight: 2-14 record over fences includes some big runs, including when he hacked up at Ascot (3m5f, good) in November and took second in the Midlands National at Uttoxeter (4m2f, soft) on latest outing; the Ascot race was going this way round but so was his penultimate start when he kept jumping to his left at Musselburgh (12l second to Transmission); that's a niggling concern but he's handicapped to go well if this is another good day.
runners.comment: Hacked up at Ascot and second in 4m2f Midlands National; jumped to his left two runs ago

runners.name: Montregard
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to top-class Protektorat; withdrawn from Scottish National last week due to the heavy going; made three notable steps forward on varied ground over 3m at Ascot (unraced here) this season, including for a good prize on latest start when he led two out for a clearcut win; raised another 4lb but he's firmly on the upgrade, likely to prove well handicapped if he gets this trip, which is also feasible; a bold show is on its way.
runners.comment: Firmly on upgrade; can prove well handicapped if he gets this trip, which is feasible

runners.name: Havaila
runners.spotlight: A rising force in the last 15 months and that's gained serious momentum in his last two starts, when winning easily by 7l at Newbury (extended 2m7f, good to soft) and 5l in the Sussex National at Plumpton (3m5f, good); strong-travelling sort who's now seeing out his races really well too; up another 10lb but could still have more in the locker.
runners.comment: Won easily by 7l at Newbury and 5l at Plumpton (3m5f Sussex National, good) last two races

runners.name: Resplendent Grey
runners.spotlight: Won this race (good ground) last year in first-time cheekpieces off a 10lb lower mark; first and second in two small fields this season, alongside two heavy defeats in top handicaps; tried blinkers when fading right out of it at the Cheltenham Festival last time and now reverts to cheekpieces; also back on firmer ground but a new career best is needed.
runners.comment: Won this race (good ground) last year in first-time cheekpieces off a 10lb lower mark

runners.name: Ask Brewster
runners.spotlight: 3m4f resulted in an 8l win at Cheltenham last April and he's won over 3m/3m2f in two of his three starts this season; latest was the Kim Muir at Cheltenham in which he was always front rank under Shane Cotter and responded admirably when Road To Home joined him on the run-in; up 7lb but this trip makes plenty of appeal and all five chase wins have been on good ground or good to firm (withdrawn from Scottish National last week due to the heavy); has a bit to prove going right-handed, with a run here in December prompting concern, but otherwise lots to like.
runners.comment: Bit to prove right-handed but otherwise lots to like, likely to be suited by trip/ground

runners.name: Livin On Luco
runners.spotlight: Failed to fire in the Eider at Newcastle on penultimate start; commendable otherwise this season, making all in the Southern National at Fontwell (3m3f, good to soft), fourth in Welsh Grand National at Chepstow (3m7f, good to soft) and short-headed at Exeter (3m7f, good) five weeks ago; raised another 2lb but has place claims.
runners.comment: Commendable over 3m3f/3m7f in three of his four starts this season; place claims

runners.name: Henry's Friend
runners.spotlight: Seems best making the running or racing prominently; his six races in the last 14 months have been a mixed bag, with an Ascot win (3m, good) in November the highlight; just 1lb higher today and ran respectably on penultimate start, but his two earlier attempts at 3m6f/4m did not work out; a tongue-tie (worn twice in 2023, with a win on first occasion) returns.
runners.comment: Six races in last 14 months were a mixed bag, Ascot 3m win in November the clear highlight

runners.name: Invincible Nao
runners.spotlight: Won Sussex National at Plumpton (extended 3m4f, soft) last February in his first long-distance race but less convincing since in forays over 4m2f, C&D and 3m5f; had a small-field 2m4f win on good ground here in November but this race, from 2lb out of the handicap, demands a big personal best.
runners.comment: Won over extended 3m4f last term; this from 2lb out of handicap demands big personal best

runners.name: In d'Or
runners.spotlight: 3m6f for the NH Chase at Cheltenham last March was his only attempt beyond 3m and he was below form; that was also his last run for Venetia Williams (bought for £220,000 in October at the David Maxwell dispersal) and he's been a good third this winter in 3m handicaps here and at Ascot; needs to build on that, as he's edging up the weights, but he's not fully exposed.
runners.comment: Good third this winter in 3m handicaps here and at Ascot; needs to build on that

runners.name: Transmission
runners.spotlight: Tapped back into last season's initial promising form, and then some, to win by 12l in the Edinburgh National at Musselburgh (3m7f, good to soft; from Rock My Way) in February with a first-time visor, which has been kept since; ridden by 7lb claimer when fair third off this 8lb higher mark behind Havaila in the Sussex National at Plumpton 19 days ago but needs to hit new heights again.
runners.comment: 12l win at Musselburgh (3m7f) with a first-time visor; fair third to Havaila at Plumpton

runners.name: Road To Home
runners.spotlight: He was set to be the only Willie Mullins runner in last week's Scottish National but was withdrawn due to the heavy ground; 7yo who was delivered with what seemed a winning challenge under Patrick Mullins in the Kim Muir at Cheltenham (3m2f, good; furthest he's been) six weeks ago only to find that Ask Brewster battled back; up 6lb but has to be in serious calculations.
runners.comment: Neck 2nd to Ask Brewster in the Kim Muir at Cheltenham (3m2f, good; furthest he's been)

runners.name: Gabbys Cross
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 11yo (six races for current yard) who has not won since 2022 but went close with first-time cheekpieces (kept since) over 3m2f at Newbury in February; second of three at Ascot (3m) in another veterans' race next time was creditable in the end, after he'd been detached at one point; that helps to envisage him as a stayer but he jumped poorly on heavy in Scottish National (40-1, pulled up) last week and this race also looks tough.
runners.comment: Jumped poorly on heavy in Scottish National (40-1) last week; this race also looks tough
runners.owner: J P McGrath & R N Pallas

runners.name: Certainly Red
runners.spotlight: Seventh in this race in 2023 and fourth on good ground in 2024; missed all of 2025 and although he ran encouragingly on reappearance here (3m, soft; 50-1) seven weeks ago, still disputing the lead when making a mess of the second last, others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Plenty of best runs have been at this track but other runners have more pressing claims

runners.name: Our Power
runners.spotlight: Withdrawn from Scottish National last week due to the heavy ground; 6lb lower than this time last year (when third in the Scottish National) as he's not fired on all cylinders this term, but excuses can be made; he's won two major 3m handicaps going right-handed (Ascot and Kempton) on good ground, back in the 2022-23 season admittedly but this 11yo may be dangerous to dismiss, including as first-time blinkers replace his usual cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Not firing on all cylinders this term; first-time blinkers replace his usual cheekpieces

runners.name: Verzain
runners.spotlight: Gave solid kick from handy position to triumph this track last outing. Might go on with it now.
runners.owner: B Cannon, P D Mullen Et Al

runners.name: Ole Go
runners.spotlight: Prominent and showed plenty of ticker on a soft deck to just go under in thrilling finish in the Without Fear Stakes here at his second race start. Maps to get a sweet run. Stripping fitter and hard to toss.
runners.owner: Mrs T L Meekin, C A Meekin & C J Swadling

runners.name: Wonderful Sky
runners.spotlight: Ridden positively and showed plenty of mettle to win at Pakenham at her second race start. Awkwardly drawn this time. Sharper now and can make her presence felt again if things pan out.
runners.owner: Mrs K W Fenton-Ellis MNZM & R Lau

runners.name: Czech Out
runners.spotlight: Posted some decent efforts from three appearances. Settled at rear, produced a grinding charge into the placings this venue at second run back. Improver, fitter third-up. Stripping fitter and can play a role.
runners.owner: M D Sheehan, T A Hams Et Al

runners.name: Aragog
runners.spotlight: Game effort and narrowly prevailed at Geelong after blowing in the market at his second race start. Switching to 1200m. Tackling stronger line-up.

runners.name: Vandaross
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in soft going here. Could add some value to exotics.
runners.owner: M J Seyers, J S Hylan Et Al

runners.name: Gimonnet
runners.spotlight: Reasonable debut performance when battled to line here when drifting in the betting. Could get into the placings.

runners.name: Autumn Mystery
runners.spotlight: Sent out $101 when never got into the race at Caulfield over this distance. Among the dangers with even luck.
runners.owner: N J Thompson & Mrs D A Thompson

runners.name: McWoody
runners.spotlight: Third outing for new trainer/ this region. Chasing hat-trick. Annexed the 3yo (Bm64) at Sandown-Lakeside 25 Mar. Won in decisive fashion this venue.
runners.owner: Ms R A McDonald, Skywood Racing Et Al

runners.name: Wigmore
runners.spotlight: Let-up (49-days). Ex C E Myers in New Zealand. NZer having first run across the ditch. Market watch advised.
runners.owner: OTI Wigmore, P G Bruce Et Al

runners.name: Matahga
runners.spotlight: Did little in soft going here. First real test of stamina here. Very hard to enthuse.

runners.name: Engine Of War
runners.spotlight: Sent out $5 when unable to threaten at Caulfield in second run from spell. All the better and will take stopping in this company.
runners.owner: MyRacehorse No 1, D E Gwynne Et Al

runners.name: Rousing Refrain
runners.spotlight: Won the Hcp (58) at Balaklava two back 1 Apr. Went home tamely on a soft deck this venue at $71. First real test of stamina here. Goes into wider exotics.
runners.owner: Ms K Harrison, G W Pfeiler Et Al

runners.name: Flying Brant
runners.spotlight: Just missed at Morphettville 28 Mar. Battled home on a soft deck this venue. Can lead or take a sit. First real test of stamina here.
runners.owner: Campolina Pty Ltd, 84/85 Premiership Et Al

runners.name: Boomeroo
runners.spotlight: Broke maiden at Sale 29 Mar. Sent out $26 when unable to threaten on a soft deck this venue. First real test of stamina here. Respect on previous sound form.

runners.name: Big Rita June
runners.spotlight: Failed to threaten on a soft deck this venue as a despised outsider. Stamina on trial here. Needs a form spike.
runners.owner: L B Grantham, Ms E G Grantham Et Al

runners.name: Angling Angel
runners.spotlight: Just missed at Sale 29 Mar. Never a threat after showing no early speed from the barriers here. Stamina on trial here. Can fill the minors here.
runners.owner: B Sinclair, G D Sutton & I C Jones

runners.name: Impulsive Reaction
runners.spotlight: Out of the money in soft going at Cranbourne last outing when in the market. Others with greater appeal.

runners.name: Starfleet Command
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Tame to line in small field at Sandown-Lakeside at second run back. First real test of stamina here. Others are packing more punch.
runners.owner: Australian Thoroughbred Bloodstock Pty Ltd, D Wagner et al

races.race_name: Sportsbet Queen Of The South Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: On Display
runners.spotlight: Last assignment this trip when stationed midfield and never landed a blow at Caulfield 11 Apr. Drawn well. Key player.
runners.owner: Sheamus Mills Bloodstock P/L, Newton T'Breds Racing Et Al

runners.name: Blindedbythelight
runners.spotlight: Given time to freshen up after 1st-up run. Close placing in the Summoned Stakes at Caulfield. Trailed speed, kept coming but winner too strong at Rosehill at resumption. Fitter second-up.

runners.name: Cilacap
runners.spotlight: Handily placed and gave her all at Caulfield over this distance after a market ease. Decent alley. Form good enough for this.
runners.owner: Echo Beach Bloodstock Pty Ltd & B N Singer

runners.name: Rumbled Again
runners.spotlight: Experienced campaigner. Never sighted at resumption at Caulfield. Will be fitter second-up. Could give some cheek at odds.
runners.owner: T J Ford, M P Trotter Et Al

runners.name: Mission Of Love
runners.spotlight: First-up (47-weeks). Void of early speed and needs the breaks to fall her way. First time around Morphettville. Wait until she shows something.
runners.owner: J G Symons, P Morley Et Al

runners.name: Private Legacy
runners.spotlight: Never a threat after showing no early speed from the barriers here at resumption. Others with stronger claims.
runners.owner: G D Hickman, A P Carney Et Al

runners.name: Wild Imagination
runners.spotlight: Made running in small field but gave away in drive 5.1 len last at Warracknabeal. Others more persuasive.
runners.owner: D V Page, Mrs R A Page Et Al

runners.name: Terilee
runners.spotlight: Made short work of the small field in the 3yo+ F&M (Bm78) at Caulfield at this trip. Among the dangers.
runners.owner: M C Kent, T J Eastwood Et Al

runners.name: World's My Oyster
runners.spotlight: Won a 1600m Benchmark 78 at Morphettville two back. Raced in back half and was never in a position to mount a serious challenge this track. Respect on previous sound form.

runners.name: Terrestar
runners.spotlight: Last win was 17 Apr 2024. No match but still very brave in defeat in 3yo+ F&M (Bm74) at Caulfield second-up. First time around Morphettville. Tackling stronger line-up.

runners.name: Teardrop Rotation
runners.spotlight: Did little on a soft deck here. Has a tricky draw. Appreciates a dry track but has plenty to find.
runners.owner: C M Bieg, C F Sherratt Et Al

runners.name: Moonlight Circus
runners.spotlight: Ran on late from the back at Caulfield first up. Fitter second-up. Could get into the placings.
runners.owner: Cross Park, L C Mugridge Et Al

runners.name: Brave Miss
runners.spotlight: Got back, cut loose late to fill the minors at Caulfield first up. First time around Morphettville. Can't enthuse.
runners.owner: Mrs B K Saunders, B A E Saunders & Mrs R R K Chidley

runners.name: Mystic Wonder
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Strong win over 1550m (Bm66) at Morphettville Parks. Others with more appeal.
runners.owner: Ms J Heaney, R M Warnock Et Al

runners.name: Isuspectjeu
runners.spotlight: Let-up (63-days). Settled back and didn't figure over a longer test in the Lord Reims Stakes this venue. Others rate stronger.
runners.owner: Ms A M Lear & M P Cornell

runners.name: Maisy
runners.spotlight: Freshened. In the thick of it at Flemington two back 28 Feb. Comfortably beaten in soft going at Rosehill at second run back. Has an adverse draw.

runners.name: Brown Nose Day Gal
runners.spotlight: Won from just off the pace-setters at Oakbank. Has a tricky draw and up in weight. Harder now.

runners.name: Niance
runners.spotlight: Resuming (28-weeks). Hits straps quickly. Highly fancied for a winning return.
runners.owner: Ace Bloodstock Racing Pty Ltd, Ace Club Et Al

runners.name: New York Lustre
runners.spotlight: Resuming (25-weeks). Won first-up at Moonee Valley last campaign. Drawn to effect. Capable of figuring again fresh in this line-up.

runners.name: Watchme Win
runners.spotlight: Narrow miss in the R N Irwin Stakes in soft going here 11 Apr. Strips fitter second run back. Could get into the placings.
runners.owner: A J Hehir, D W Rasheed Et Al

runners.name: Recommendation
runners.spotlight: Long time since last saluted. Knuckled down for third at Ballarat when off an extended spell as a market drifter. Others are packing more punch.
runners.owner: N S Psaltis, Mrs G C Psaltis Et Al

runners.name: Coleman
runners.spotlight: Third run now in new stable/region. Ridden cold and never in the picture at Ballarat at first run back. Will be fitter second-up. Awkwardly drawn and rising in weight.
runners.owner: Mrs S Cummings, Mrs J Groves Et Al

runners.name: Pisanello
runners.spotlight: Third run now in new stable/region. Settled back and didn't figure in soft going at second run back in the R N Irwin Stakes this venue. Lift needed on last effort.

runners.name: Power Beau
runners.spotlight: Now 4 weeks between runs. Sent forward and boxed on bravely in the small field for 1 len second this venue. Draws to do no work. One for exotics.

runners.name: Taunting
runners.spotlight: Resuming (51-weeks). Ex Michael Hickmott in Victoria. Proven record wet/track. Form sound at this course and journey.
runners.owner: M Hickmott, M Pilkington Et Al

runners.name: Losesomewinmore
runners.spotlight: Resuming (38-weeks). Brought to race fitness with 3 trials and proven here. Could add some value to exotics.

runners.name: Zoupurring
runners.spotlight: Stuck tough, holding off challengers Hills Railway Stakes at Oakbank at second run back at $11. Breaks from a low barrier. Could give some cheek at odds.
runners.owner: Bennett Racing Pty Ltd, R Menchise Et Al

runners.name: Lingani
runners.spotlight: Ran on late from the back at Oakbank over this trip. Since trialled at Balaklava 16 Apr. Can't enthuse.
runners.owner: G G Tully, T K Thompson Et Al

runners.name: Grand Larceny
runners.spotlight: First-up (21-weeks). In the minors at Moonee Valley first-up last campaign. Could get into the placings fresh in this line-up.
runners.owner: Werrett Bloodstock, Madden Bloodstock Et Al

runners.name: Dual Pressure
runners.spotlight: First-up (20-weeks). Went close at Morphettville two back 22 Nov. Keener on others.
runners.owner: A Bain, ABR Thoroughbred Race Club No 1 Et Al

runners.name: Sixteen Reasons
runners.spotlight: First-up (27-weeks). Given a good tune up with 3 trials and proven here. Awkwardly drawn. This doesn't look easy.
runners.owner: M A Crawford, Adelaide Cricket Boys Et Al

runners.name: Thunder Shoc
runners.spotlight: Got back and wasn't totally disgraced at Oakbank over this trip. Others have more zip.
runners.owner: R I Chapman, P A Crawford Et Al

runners.name: Mic Drop
runners.spotlight: Close placing in the Manihi Classic at Morphettville. Not disgraced at Oakbank at this distance. Tactical speed, inside draw. Could get into the placings.
runners.owner: S A Dyson, C Tanzer Et Al

runners.name: Winning Revolution
runners.spotlight: Settled midfield, let down really well at Oakbank over this trip. Leaning towards others.
runners.owner: Ms D K Hamlin & G G Brown

runners.name: Enuff Seduction
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Got back and hit line with a full head of steam at Morphettville Parks. Tackling stronger line-up.
runners.owner: B W Flitter, B J Ives Et Al

runners.name: Swiftie Harriet
runners.spotlight: Wore them down late here first up. Stiffer challenge here.
runners.owner: T J Doudle, S D Bland Et Al

runners.name: Streetcar Fury
runners.spotlight: Knuckled down well winning the (Bm56) under a big impost at Murray Bridge Gh. Tackling stronger company.
runners.owner: S R Doyle, Ocean Street Racing Et Al

runners.name: Legacy Bound
runners.spotlight: Freshened. Won 3yo Hcp Flemington 7 Mar. Battled home in rain affected ground at Flemington at second run back. Drawn to get a conservative run.

runners.name: Inkaruna
runners.spotlight: Been supported and where there's smoke there's fire. Close placing in the Birthday Card Stakes at Rosehill. Settled back and didn't figure in choppy conditions at this distance in the P J Bell Stakes at Randwick. Can put last run behind her.

runners.name: Tycoon Star
runners.spotlight: Terrific effort when just shaded on a soft deck this track 11 Apr. Can challenge.
runners.owner: Lindsay Park Bloodstock, Yu Long Investments Et Al

runners.name: Thanks Gorgeous
runners.spotlight: Now 4 weeks between runs. Got up in final strides at Flemington. New to Morphettville. Can't enthuse.
runners.owner: Mrs I P Kavanagh & Mrs C L Kavanagh

runners.name: Ferivia
runners.spotlight: Just wacked away without threatening over 1200m F&M (Bm100) at Caulfield in second run from spell. Awkwardly drawn. Others with more appeal.

runners.name: Alpha Sofie
runners.spotlight: Now 5 weeks between runs. Ran on late from the back at Caulfield at this distance. Can fill the minors here.
runners.owner: Murphy Syndicate, Ms L M Lord Et Al

runners.name: Space Rider
runners.spotlight: Now 5 weeks between runs. Raced in back half and was never in a position to mount a serious challenge at Rosehill. Awkwardly drawn. Leaning towards others.
runners.owner: Myracehorse No 1, Aston Bloodstock Et Al

runners.name: Torsheen
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Responded well from back half to get the prize at Mornington at this trip. Shapes as a rough place prospect.

runners.name: Signature Scent
runners.spotlight: Annexed the 3yo (Bm72) at Morphettville 28 Mar. Fought bravely to the wire in the Redelva Stakes this track. Could get into the placings.

runners.name: Rosberg
runners.spotlight: Let-up (63-days). Got back and failed to make a meaningful impression at Caulfield first up. One for exotics fresh in this line-up.
runners.owner: Mrs J R McGrath, D Alysandratos Et Al

runners.name: Extragalactic
runners.spotlight: Aiming for hat-trick. Let down with a fine turn of speed from the back half to win at second run back. Can fill the minors here.
runners.owner: T W Hennessy, Choochoo Racing Et Al

runners.name: Sir Myka
runners.spotlight: Determined winner at Oakbank. Sterner task awaits.
runners.owner: T J Doudle, S D Bland Et Al

runners.name: Virtual Illusion
runners.spotlight: Made late rally from the back here second-up. Usually gets back. Others more persuasive.
runners.owner: R R Jolly, Gts Racing Et Al

runners.name: Mathematician
runners.spotlight: Snagged back and worked home okay in Redelva Stakes here last start. Very hard to enthuse.
runners.owner: Clarken Bloodstock, T Needham Et Al

runners.name: Lawborough
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Led, soon under pressure and dropped off at Flemington 4 weeks back. Coming back in distance. Looking elsewhere.
runners.owner: SZ Racing No 1, M C Wade Et Al

runners.name: Getta Good Feeling
runners.spotlight: Freshened. Highly progressive winning 4 from 9. Determined winner at Caulfield in second run from spell at prohibited odds. All the better and form good enough for this.

runners.name: Strictly Business
runners.spotlight: Never a threat after showing no early speed from the barriers at Ballarat in second run from spell. Usually gets back. Others are packing more punch.
runners.owner: Mhumhain Racing, D Insall Et Al

runners.name: Salty Pearl
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Took out the VOBIS Platinum Guineas at Caulfield 14 Mar. Finished in hot pursuit on a soft deck at Rosehill over this trip. Placed only other visit to Morphettville.
runners.owner: Starblue Racing TLO Malice, CMR LOF Et Al

runners.name: Panova
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Sent out $12 when never got into the race in soft going at Rosehill over this trip. Usually gets back. Shapes as a rough place prospect.

runners.name: Mating Call
runners.spotlight: Wore them down late here. Stamina on trial here. Can fill the minors here.

runners.name: Freedom Flame
runners.spotlight: Prominent and went down fighting in soft going in the Open company this venue. Not fancied.
runners.owner: B H Perks & Ms A M Clough

runners.name: Bona Sforza
runners.spotlight: Stakes winning NZ filly. Won the 11 Apr. Stamina on trial here. Could give some cheek at odds.
runners.owner: F & S Finnegan, A Boyce Et Al

runners.name: Paltrow Miss
runners.spotlight: Merit in both Australian runs. Ran home into the minors 3yo F Hcp at Caulfield in second run from spell. First real test of stamina here. Sharper now and has some claims.
runners.owner: A Kheir, B J O'Brien Et Al

runners.name: Stung
runners.spotlight: Ridden cold and never in the picture this track. First real test of stamina here. Very hard to enthuse.
runners.owner: R & C Legh Racing P/L, B & C Tbreds P/L & Pulitano Tbreds

runners.name: Kazaru
runners.spotlight: Won a 2100m Open at Launceston two back. Started $7 when battled home in Open company in soft going this venue. Can't enthuse.
runners.owner: O T I Racing, A M Kerr Et Al

runners.name: Naraghi
runners.spotlight: Dictated speed, boxed away in the Auraria Stakes this track. First real test of stamina here. Looking elsewhere.
runners.owner: L Howley, C W Kwok Et Al

runners.name: Just Kick
runners.spotlight: Left in winner's wake but ran on soundly for 2.3 len third in the small field at Caulfield. One for exotics.
runners.owner: Sparta Racing, C W Kwok Et Al

runners.name: Synthesise
runners.spotlight: Failed to flatter here. First real test of stamina here. Leaning towards others.
runners.owner: P R Gunner, D R Wagner & S R Wagner

runners.name: Cushioned
runners.spotlight: Won in decisive fashion at Pakenham over this distance. Just out of her maiden and facing tougher challenge here.

runners.name: Oui Oui Ma Cherie
runners.spotlight: Failed to threaten on a soft deck this track as an odds drifter. Unplaced from 5 starts here. Others with greater appeal.
runners.owner: S P Gower, P J Treloar Et Al

runners.name: Boomeroo
runners.spotlight: Went home tamely in soft going here at $26. First real test of stamina here. Looking elsewhere.

runners.name: Savitri
runners.spotlight: On target for hat-trick. Made short work of the small field in the 3yo F Hcp at Caulfield. Stamina on trial here. Can fill the minors here.

runners.name: Charm Stone
runners.spotlight: Freshened after resuming from a break. On pace and courageous in defeat at Caulfield first up.
runners.owner: Sheamus Mills Bloodstock Ladies, Newton Ladies Et Al

runners.name: Skybird
runners.spotlight: Void of early speed but she let down strongly Open company at Randwick at second run back.
runners.owner: M J Scalzo, D J Scalzo Et Al

runners.name: My Gladiola
runners.spotlight: Fresh. Last at turn, ran on late at Flemington.
runners.owner: Redgum Racing Pty Ltd Synd, C A Ahrens Et Al

runners.name: Benedetta
runners.spotlight: 5 weeks since last run. Made late rally from the back at Caulfield over this trip.
runners.owner: Eales Racing Pty Ltd , B M Wilson Et Al

runners.name: Generosity
runners.spotlight: Now 5 weeks between runs. Really good run coming from well back in the Kia Ora Galaxy at Rosehill second-up as an odds drifter.

runners.name: Point Barrow
runners.spotlight: Freshened. Closed with conviction from back half to win at Caulfield in second run from spell.
runners.owner: Mrs B M McClure, R McClure Et Al

runners.name: Super Smink
runners.spotlight: Ridden cold and let down powerfully this track first up.
runners.owner: Mrs C L Morton, McHams Et Al

runners.name: Bridal Waltz
runners.spotlight: 5 weeks since last run. Handy and boxed on solidly at Rosehill in second run from spell.

runners.name: Flying For Fun
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced at Randwick in second run from spell.
runners.owner: M W O'Keeffe & Ms V J King

runners.name: Stretan Angel
runners.spotlight: No threat to winner but ran on for the minors on a soft deck here at first run back when in the market.
runners.owner: J M Maclean, Going Again Syndicate Et Al

runners.name: Bossy Benita
runners.spotlight: The mare hasn't been far away at last two runs including when kept on evenly at Caulfield. Coming back in distance.
runners.owner: M J M Racing & Bloodstock Pty Ltd, D G Benyon Et Al

runners.name: Geegees Mistruth
runners.spotlight: Handily placed and gave her all at Ballarat.

runners.name: Ameena
runners.spotlight: First-up (25-weeks). Wait until she shows something.

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Committed Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Charles Darwin
runners.spotlight: Brother to Bluebeard (Prix Morny and Middle Park); only fourth of seven when 4-6 for his Curragh debut (5f, good) last April; made handsome amends with wins in a C&D maiden (soft), a four-runner Naas conditions event (5f good) and the Norfolk Stakes, at the main expense of Middle Park winner Wise Approach; top-class sprinting prospect, reportedly in good shape.
runners.comment: A cut above these rivals on his Norfolk Stakes defeat of the subsequent Middle Park winner

runners.name: Chicago Call
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f Fairyhouse maiden over this trip last July; best form when just denied in a Listed event at the same track in September (6f yielding); below form in the Goffs Million on only 7f attempt; soft ground probably not ideal on seasonal debut at Cork.
runners.comment: Winner and Listed-placed at two, has a run under his belt, soft ground may not have suited
runners.owner: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit

runners.name: Frescobaldi
runners.spotlight: Ex-Aidan O'Brien won the 7f Convivial at York on his third outing; confirmed a fair standard of form in 7f Listed races at Doncaster and Dundalk before changing hands for 100,000gns; useful prospect for Danny Murphy; unlikely to match former stablemate Charles Darwin.
runners.comment: Ex-Ballydoyle, Convivial winner, unlikely to match his former stablemate Charles Darwin
runners.owner: N W T Dalzell & R P Behan & Mrs John Murphy

runners.name: Focaccia
runners.spotlight: Debut third at the Curragh four weeks ago suggests she can win races; this looks much too demanding at this stage of her career, but worth a shot with black type available.
runners.comment: Debut third at the Curragh suggests she can win races, this looks much too demanding

runners.name: Starman Tom
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in finishing second behind last season's Queen Mary fourth on debut in a 5f maiden at Cork last month; beaten favourite when third in a big-field 6f maiden of a relatively modest standard at the Curragh last Sunday.
runners.comment: Maiden who failed to build on his promising Cork run when third at the Curragh last Sunday
runners.owner: Poor Fools Syndicate/Deirdre Cotter/Thomas W. McGrath

runners.name: Kindergarten
runners.spotlight: Made most of the running when a 7/1 winner on debut over C&D last October; placed horses Ocean's Breath and Anushka have both won at Dundalk; should not be out of place at this level.
runners.comment: Winner over C&D on debut last October, should not be out of place at this level

runners.name: Silk Braid
runners.spotlight: Beat a Joseph O'Brien-trained odds-on chance in a 6f Curragh maiden last October on her only start at two; hard to know what level she can reach; tough task against Charles Darwin.
runners.comment: 6f Curragh maiden winner, tough task against a colt of the calibre of Charles Darwin

races.race_name: BAR 1 Betting Vintage Crop Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Scandinavia
runners.spotlight: Won four of his five races at three, the exception when a close-up fifth behind Carmers in the 1m6f Queen's Vase; improved with cheekpieces, winning a five-runner 1m5f Group 3 at Newmarket by almost 9l and mastering older stablemate Illinois in the Goodwood Cup (2m, good); dug deep to hold off Rahieb on soft ground in the St Leger (Carmers fifth); top-class stayer.
runners.comment: Goodwood Cup winner, confirmed progression with St Leger triumph, Gold Cup candidate
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Emit
runners.spotlight: Consistent last season apart from a poor run in the Hampton Court Stakes at Royal Ascot. went close in a 1m2f Group 3 at the Curragh in May; Listed winner at Gowran (1m4f, good) in July before runner-up in Group 2 and Group 3 (both over 1m7f on very soft ground) in France; failed to show his best form when fourth behind Layfayette on reappearance at the Curragh.
runners.comment: Good form last season on home soil and in France, well below his best on reappearance

runners.name: Carmers
runners.spotlight: Won his first three races, notably 1m6f Group 2 Queen's Vase at Royal Ascot, beating the subsequent Geoffrey Freer winner; Furthur; lost his unbeaten record but emerged with credit in taking second in the 1m4f Great Voltigeur at York; hung left and dropped away to finish fifth behind Scandinavia when running on soft for the first time in the St Leger.
runners.comment: Queen's Vase winner with Scandinavia fifth, that rival turned the tables in the St Leger

runners.name: Dallas Star
runners.spotlight: Won Leopardstown Group 3 in April 2024 (1m2f, heavy); last win was at Dundalk early last year; no match for Leinster in a St Leger trial at the Curragh last August; twice placed in small-field AW events during the winter; well held in third behind Layfayette at the Curragh early this month; up against it.
runners.comment: Well held in third behind Layfayette at the Curragh early this month, up against it

runners.name: Sunchart
runners.spotlight: Useful dual-purpose sort, 3-34 on Flat (and second on ten occasions); his only two stakes race wins from 28 attempts have been in the 1m2f Naas event in which he was third of eight last month; suited by testing conditions, drying ground against him.
runners.comment: Durable sort, suited by testing conditions, drying ground against him

runners.name: Leinster
runners.spotlight: Stepped outside of handicap company (fourth in Chester Cup and in Ascot Stakes) to win a four-runner St Leger Trial at the Curragh last August, readily brushing aside front-running Dallas Star in the final furlong; faced a very stiff task but ran no sort of race when tailed off in the Irish St Leger.
runners.comment: Beat Dallas Star in a 1m6f Curragh event last August, tailed off in the Irish St Leger

runners.name: Layfayette
runners.spotlight: Winner of ten races; last of six stakes race wins was in 2023 Royal Whip over 1m2f at the Curragh; however, he proved that he retains considerable ability and enthusiasm when winning a conditions event over this trip at the Curragh on seasonal debut;  (Dallas Star third, Emit fourth), that was his first 1m6f attempt, on his 47th outing.
runners.comment: Retains considerable ability and enthusiasm judged on Curragh win on first 1m6f attempt

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Salsabil Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)

runners.name: Thundering On
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly, whose dam won the 1m2f Pretty Polly for this stable; fine effort on second juvenile start when second in 1m Group 3  at the Curragh, 4 3/4l in front of third-placed Ice Dancer; touched off in a 1m maiden at Leopardstown 13 days ago; trip will suit, stable in terrific form; could feature here despite her maiden status.
runners.comment: Trip will suit, stable in terrific form; could feature here despite her maiden status

runners.name: Minerva
runners.spotlight: Ran seven times at two, opening her account on final start, when odds-on in a 7f maiden at Leopardstown; best effort when second to top-class stablemate Diamond Necklace (unbeaten Prix Marcel Boussac winner) in a 7f Curragh conditions event; possible stamina doubt, and Ryan Moore prefers the lower-rated, less exposed Ice Dancer.
runners.comment: Possible stamina doubt, Ryan Moore prefers the lower-rated but less exposed Ice Dancer

runners.name: Emmeleia
runners.spotlight: Half-sister by Camelot to 2025 Lingfield Derby Trial winner Puppet Master; won a 7f barrier trial at Leopardstown last August, second to the promising Yellowstone Lake n a 7f maiden at Dundalk in September; batted well to beat a stablemate over a bit further than this at Dundalk; needs to improve, drying ground should help.
runners.comment: Beat a stablemate at Dundalk, gets this trip, needs to improve, drying ground should help

runners.name: Chasing Paradise
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when 7l second to an experienced Ballydoyle-trained filly over 1m at Leopardstown on sole start at two; stayed this trip well when a winning favourite on soft-to-heavy at the Curragh on reappearance.
runners.comment: Stayed this trip well when a winning favourite on soft-to-heavy at the Curragh
runners.owner: Mrs M V Magnier & Mrs Paul Shanahan & S E Sangster

runners.name: Camino Vino
runners.spotlight: Invincible Spirit filly created a good impression when beating an 86-rated previous winner in a 1m maiden at Dundalk in December on her only juvenile start; interesting, although not guaranteed to stay as far as this on pedigree.
runners.comment: Won 1m maiden at Dundalk, not guaranteed to stay as far as this on pedigree

runners.name: Ice Dancer
runners.spotlight: Had a troubled run on debut; put that behind her when landing the odds in a 1m maiden at Gowran last September; ground was testing then, and same when she was third in a 1m Group 3 race at the Curragh in October; pedigree implies she can improve at this trip; Ryan Moore prefers her to Minerva who is rated 10lb superior (official and RPR figures).
runners.comment: Pedigree implies 1m winner can improve at this trip, Ryan Moore prefers her to Minerva
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Bloom
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly out of a Listed winner by Galileo; stepped up from unplaced debut at Leopardstown to win a 1m maiden at Cork last September; stable's two other runners are more appealing.
runners.comment: Stepped up from unplaced debut at Leopardstown to win a 1m maiden at Cork last September.
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

races.race_name: bet365 Novices' Championship Final Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Minella Yoga
runners.spotlight: Improving 4yo whose record is 3-4; bought for £360,000 after sole Irish hurdles start; ran well in Grade 2 for sole defeat; easily justified favouritism at Taunton (2m, good) last time; major player.
runners.comment: Improving 4yo whose record is 3-4; scored easily at Taunton last time; major contender

runners.name: Cosmic Connection
runners.spotlight: Scored in tidy fashion at Chepstow (2m, good to soft; handicap debut) last time, taking rules record to 2-3, and is probably still well treated even after a 6lb rise; improving.
runners.comment: Scored in tidy fashion at Chepstow (handicap debut) last time, taking rules record to 2-3

runners.name: Kadastral
runners.spotlight: Non-stayer over 2m4f last time; solid record at 2m otherwise, including two efforts behind smart Sober Glory; this Skelton runner likely still has more to offer; respected handicap debutant.
runners.comment: Has a solid record at 2m and likely still has more to offer; respected handicap debutant

runners.name: Kocktail Bleu
runners.spotlight: Proving consistent and, following a good effort in a Grade 2, he made all at Newbury (2m, good to soft) back down in class last time; far from sure to get an easy lead in this bigger field, however.
runners.comment: Made all at Newbury most recently but is far from sure to get an easy lead in this field

runners.name: Secret Sniper
runners.spotlight: No match for Minella Yoga at Taunton, taking record to 0-6; however, he's related to several winners for his connections and may have potential off his opening mark; one to consider.
runners.comment: Related to several winners for his connections; may have potential off his opening mark

runners.name: Legal Weapon
runners.spotlight: Reasonably consistent but is 0-5 over hurdles, finishing fourth to Wilstar at Warwick most recently; chance of finding the necessary progress largely depends on the effects of first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Chance of finding the necessary progress largely depends on the effects of tongue-tie

runners.name: Way Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Prevailed in second-time hood at Taunton (2m, good) most recently, adding to his Flat win, and may have more to offer in the retained headgear; in deeper waters today but could go well.
runners.comment: Prevailed in second-time hood at Taunton and may have more to offer in this headgear

runners.name: Wilstar
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to Douvan and Jonbon; not in their league but is progressive and has won his last two starts, latest at Warwick (2m, good to soft); enters calculations on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well bred; progressive and has won last two starts; enters calculations on handicap debut

runners.name: Kapitein Kool
runners.spotlight: Dual novice winner but came up well short at Newbury (2m3f) on handicap debut most recently and can be opposed in this competitive field.
runners.comment: Came up well short on handicap debut last time; can be opposed

runners.name: Dutch Corner
runners.spotlight: Posted consistent efforts prior to ready win in Newton Abbot maiden (2m2f, good to soft) last time; may improve further and can't be dismissed, even though Harry Skelton prefers Kadastral.
runners.comment: Scored readily at Newton Abbot last time and may improve further; yard also runs Kadastral

runners.name: Stanners Glen
runners.spotlight: Made all in Wincanton maiden (1m7f, good) in latest hurdles attempt, adding to his point/bumper wins and taking overall record to 3-6; however, this scenario is much more demanding.
runners.comment: Made all at Wincanton most recently but this scenario is more demanding

runners.name: Race To Base
runners.spotlight: Had wind surgery since failing to convince over 2m4f in the EBF Final on this track last time on stable debut; return to 2m looks a plus but he's still seeking first rules win away from Hereford.
runners.comment: Return to 2m looks a plus but he's still seeking first rules win away from Hereford

runners.name: Last Round
runners.spotlight: Finished behind Kadastral on penultimate start but may fare better in this rematch, having scored comfortably in Fontwell maiden (2m1f, good to soft) latest; further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Scored comfortably in Fontwell maiden last time; further progress is possible

runners.name: Bespoke Tailor
runners.spotlight: Has consistent hurdles form; however, his chance of gaining a first success in this sphere largely depends on how well he responds to a physical tweak (wind surgery since last run).
runners.comment: Chance of gaining a first hurdles win depends on how well he responds to wind surgery

runners.name: Desertmore News
runners.spotlight: Made all in small field at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) last time, taking hurdles record to 2-3; however, he faces a stiffer task in this deep handicap with competition for the lead.
runners.comment: Made all in small field at Sedgefield last time; faces stiffer task in this deep handicap

runners.name: Talakan
runners.spotlight: Flat winner in France and knocking at the door over hurdles for new yard, second to Wilstar at Warwick (2m) most recently; possibilities, provided he shows some progress on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Knocking at the door; possibilities, provided he shows progress on handicap debut

runners.name: Love You Back
runners.spotlight: Flat winner whose hurdles form comprises a couple of second-place finishes and a late fall (beaten at the time); even off bottom weight, this looks a difficult task upped in class.
runners.comment: Even off bottom weight, this looks a difficult task upped in class

runners.name: Ambiente Friendly
runners.spotlight: Showed smart Flat form as a 3yo; managed to win in December but has otherwise broadly been too free for his own good since switched to hurdles; ideally needs to settle better.
runners.comment: Broadly too free for his own good since switched to hurdles; needs to settle better

races.race_name: Caffreys Black Stout King Richard III Cup Handicap

runners.name: Zoulu Chief
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably at Lingfield in January when bidding for a 6f AW double; 8lb higher than when second at Newmarket (7f) on last turf attempt but he's proved consistent since being gelded and his first two handicap wins were on good to firm; in the mix.
runners.comment: Reliable type who is very effective on fast ground and he's respected back from a break

runners.name: Betsen
runners.spotlight: Won on stable debut at Southwell (6f, Tapeta) in November and he's posted some solid efforts since then; hard to rule out another good run but this is a step back up in grade and sole turf win was 19 months ago.
runners.comment: In good form on AW but this is a step back up in grade and his sole turf win was in 2024

runners.name: Berkshire Whisper
runners.spotlight: Completed an AW hat-trick when scoring on his handicap debut at Newcastle (6f) last April; held in six subsequent runs but he went close off this mark at Southwell in November and was a creditable third at Newcastle three weeks ago; now returns to turf but he ran well in a fast-ground Listed event last spring and he's still unexposed in this sphere; in the mix.
runners.comment: Placed off this mark at Newcastle latest and he's still unexposed on turf; in the mix
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Strike Red
runners.spotlight: On lengthy losing but he's a smart handicapper on his day and he returned from five months off with a good third at Newcastle (6f, AW) two weeks ago; should benefit from that run and he remains well treated on his second in the Stewards' Cup last August; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Second in Stewards' Cup last summer and runner-up at Newcastle 12 days ago; respected

runners.name: Fast Track Harry
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner who made it 2-2 on AW when completing a double at Newcastle (6f; off 4lb lower) last month; didn't fire in his hat-trick bid at Kempton but he still has potential and is not ruled out in suitable conditions back on turf.
runners.comment: Held in hat-trick bid at Kempton but he still has potential and is not ruled back on turf

runners.name: Dark Cloud Rising
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who ended his 2025 campaign with a success and creditable effort over 5f at Newcastle (AW); was as good as ever in those two runs but he was well below form on his reappearance at Pontefract (5f, good to soft) 18 days ago; may have needed that run but he needs more progress to defy this mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner who scored on AW in November but he needs more progress to defy this mark

runners.name: Roman Dragon
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on his best form last season but he's not easy to predict and has been up and down during another spell in Bahrain since December; comes with risks attached back in Britain and most of his wins have been at Chester; tongue-tie worn last time is removed.
runners.comment: Chester specialist who is not easy to predict and others are preferred

runners.name: Ten Pounds
runners.spotlight: Record of 5-10 for Harry Charlton and his last win was in a conditions event at Kempton (6f, AW) in October; ran without his declared tongue-tie when fourth on stable debut at Southwell in January but he finished down the field in another Class 2 handicap at Newcastle two weeks later; conditions should be fine on this return to turf but he needs to step up again after another break.
runners.comment: Record of 5-12 but he was down the field at Newcastle last time; needs more after a break

runners.name: Annaf
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a game win in Group 3 at Ascot (6f, soft) in October; repeatedly met trouble when only seventh in Saudi Arabia (a race he'd won two years earlier) in February and things didn't really go his way again in a Listed race at Doncaster last month; has possibilities on this drop back in class and his last handicap win was off 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner over 6f last autumn and he has possibilities back in a handicap

runners.name: Prince Of India
runners.spotlight: Won four times during a progressive campaign last year but he's come up short in Listed/Group 3 events this spring; seems ideally suited by fast ground but he's still 4lb higher than for his last win and has some work to do back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Four wins last year but his progress seems to have flattened out; others preferred

runners.name: Fivethousandtoone
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner who has finished close up on Tapeta (5f/6f) in his last three runs; record of 1-26 on turf but he's on a lower mark in this sphere and he ran well in the Great St Wilfrid at Ripon (6f, good to firm) last summer; dangerous.
